self.engine.render_to_string(\\\"inheritance20\\\")\\n253:         self.assertEqual(output, \\\"1&a3_\\\")\\n254: \\n255:     @setup(inheritance_templates)\\n256:     def test_inheritance21(self):\\n257:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n258:         Three-level inheritance with {{ block.super }} from parent\\n259:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n260:         output = self.engine.render_to_string(\\\"inheritance21\\\")\\n261:         self.assertEqual(output, \\\"12a34\\\")\\n262: \\n263:     @setup(inheritance_templates)\\n264:     def test_inheritance22(self):\\n265:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n266:         Three-level inheritance with {{ block.super }} from grandparent\\n267:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n268:         output = self.engine.render_to_string(\\\"inheritance22\\\")\\n269:         self.assertEqual(output, \\\"1&a3_\\\")\\n270: \\n271:     @setup(inheritance_templates)\\n272:     def test_inheritance23(self):\\n273:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n274:         Three-level inheritance with {{ block.super }} from parent and\\n275:         grandparent\\n276:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n277:         output = self.engine.render_to_string(\\\"inheritance23\\\")\\n278:         self.assertEqual(output, \\\"1&ab3_\\\")\\n279: \\n280:     @setup(inheritance_templates)\\n281:     def test_inheritance24(self):\\n282:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n283:         Inheritance from local context without use of template loader\\n284:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n285:         context_template = self.engine.from_string(\\n286:             \\\"1{% block first %}_{% endblock %}3{% block second %}_{% endblock %}\\\"\\n287:         )\\n288:         output = self.engine.render_to_string(\\n289:             \\\"inheritance24\\\", {\\\"context_template\\\": context_template}\\n290:         )\\n291:         self.assertEqual(output, \\\"1234\\\")\\n292: \\n293:     @setup(inheritance_templates)\\n294:     def test_inheritance25(self):\\n295:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n296:         Inheritance from local context with variable parent template\\n297:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n298:         context_template = [\\n299:             self.engine.from_string(\\\"Wrong\\\"),\\n300:             self.engine.from_string(\\n301:                 \\\"1{% block first %}_{% endblock %}3{% block second %}_{% endblock %}\\\"\\n302:             ),\\n303:         ]\\n304:         output = self.engine.render_to_string(\\n305:             \\\"inheritance25\\\", {\\\"context_template\\\": context_template}\\n306:         )\\n307:         self.assertEqual(output, \\\"1234\\\")\\n308: \\n309:     @setup(inheritance_templates)\\n310:     def test_inheritance26(self):\\n311:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n312:         Set up a base template to extend\\n313:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n314:         output = self.engine.render_to_string(\\\"inheritance26\\\")\\n315:         self.assertEqual(output, \\\"no tags\\\")\\n316: \\n317:     @setup(inheritance_templates)\\n318:     def test_inheritance27(self):\\n319:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n320:         Inheritance from a template that doesn't have any blocks\\n321:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n322:         output = self.engine.render_to_string(\\\"inheritance27\\\")\\n323:         self.assertEqual(output, \\\"no tags\\\")\\n324: \\n325:     @setup(inheritance_templates)\\n326:     def test_inheritance_28(self):\\n327:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n328:         Set up a base template with a space in it.\\n329:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n330:         output = self.engine.render_to_string(\\\"inheritance 28\\\")\\n331:         self.assertEqual(output, \\\"!\\\")\\n332: \\n333:     @setup(inheritance_templates)\\n334:     def test_inheritance29(self):\\n335:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n336:         Inheritance from a template with a space in its name should work.\\n337:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n338:         output = self.engine.render_to_string(\\\"inheritance29\\\")\\n339:         self.assertEqual(output, \\\"!\\\")\\n340: \\n341:     @setup(inheritance_templates)\\n342:     def test_inheritance30(self):\\n343:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n344:         Base template, putting block in a conditional {% if %} tag\\n345:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n346:         output = self.engine.render_to_string(\\\"inheritance30\\\", {\\\"optional\\\": True})\\n347:         self.assertEqual(output, \\\"123\\\")\\n348: \\n349:     # Inherit from a template with block wrapped in an {% if %} tag\\n350:     # (in parent), still gets overridden\\n351:     @setup(inheritance_templates)\\n352:     def test_inheritance31(self):\\n353:         output = self.engine.render_to_string(\\\"inheritance31\\\", {\\\"optional\\\": True})\\n354:         self.assertEqual(output, \\\"1two3\\\")\\n355: \\n356:     @setup(inheritance_templates)\\n357:     def test_inheritance32(self):\\n358:         output = self.engine.render_to_string(\\\"inheritance32\\\")\\n359:         self.assertEqual(output, \\\"13\\\")\\n360: \\n361:     @setup(inheritance_templates)\\n362:     def test_inheritance33(self):\\n363:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n364:         Base template, putting block in a conditional {% if %} tag\\n365:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n366:         output = self.engine.render_to_string(\\\"inheritance33\\\", {\\\"optional\\\": 1})\\n367:         self.assertEqual(output, \\\"123\\\")\\n368: \\n369:     @setup(inheritance_templates)\\n370:     def test_inheritance34(self):\\n371:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n372: self.engine.render_to_string(\\\"inheritance34\\\", {\\\"optional\\\": 1})\\n376:         self.assertEqual(output, \\\"1two3\\\")\\n377: \\n378:     @setup(inheritance_templates)\\n379:     def parent), still gets overridden\\n400:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n401:         output = self.engine.render_to_string(\\\"inheritance37\\\", {\\\"numbers\\\": \\\"123\\\"})\\n402:         self.assertEqual(output, \\\"_X_X_X_\\\")\\n403: \\n404:     @setup(inheritance_templates)\\n405:     def test_inheritance38(self):\\n406:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n407:         Inherit from a template with block wrapped in an {% for %} tag\\n408:         (in parent), still gets overridden\\n409:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n410:         output = self.engine.render_to_string(\\\"inheritance38\\\")\\n411:         self.assertEqual(output, \\\"_\\\")\\n412: \\n413:     # The super block will still be found.\\n414:     @setup(inheritance_templates)\\n415:     def test_inheritance39(self):\\n416:         output = self.engine.render_to_string(\\\"inheritance39\\\", {\\\"optional\\\": True})\\n417:         self.assertEqual(output, \\\"1new23\\\")\\n418: \\n419:     @setup(inheritance_templates)\\n420:     def test_inheritance40(self):\\n421:         output = self.engine.render_to_string(\\\"inheritance40\\\", {\\\"optional\\\": 1})\\n422:         self.assertEqual(output, \\\"1new23\\\")\\n423: \\n424:     @setup(inheritance_templates)\\n425:     def test_inheritance41(self):\\n426:         output = self.engine.render_to_string(\\\"inheritance41\\\", {\\\"numbers\\\": \\\"123\\\"})\\n427:         self.assertEqual(output, \\\"_new1_new2_new3_\\\")\\n428: \\n429:     @setup(inheritance_templates)\\n430:     def test_inheritance42(self):\\n431:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n432:         Expression starting and ending with a quote\\n433:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n434:         output = self.engine.render_to_string(\\\"inheritance42\\\")\\n435:         self.assertEqual(output, \\\"1234\\\")\\n436: \\n437:     @setup(inheritance_templates)\\n438:     def test_inheritance_empty(self):\\n439:         with self.assertRaisesMessage(\\n440:             TemplateSyntaxError, \\\"'extends' takes one argument\\\"\\n441:         ):\\n442:             self.engine.render_to_string(\\\"inheritance_empty\\\")\\n443: \\n444:     @setup(inheritance_templates)\\n445:     def test_extends_duplicate(self):\\n446:         msg = \\\"'extends' cannot appear more than once in the same template\\\"\\n447:         with self.assertRaisesMessage(TemplateSyntaxError, msg):\\n448:             self.engine.render_to_string(\\\"extends_duplicate\\\")\\n449: \\n450:     @setup(inheritance_templates)\\n451:     def test_duplicate_block(self):\\n452:         msg = \\\"'block' tag with name 'content' appears more than once\\\"\\n453:         with self.assertRaisesMessage(TemplateSyntaxError, msg):\\n454:             self.engine.render_to_string(\\\"duplicate_block\\\")\\n455: \\n456: \\n457: class ExtendsNodeTests(SimpleTestCase):\\n458:     def test_extends_node_repr(self):\\n459:         extends_node = ExtendsNode(\\n460:             nodelist=NodeList([]),\\n461:             parent_name=Node(),\\n462:             template_dirs=[],\\n463:         )\\n464:         self.assertEqual(repr(extends_node), \\\"<ExtendsNode: extends None>\\\")\\n465: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c@674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3/-/blob/tests/template_tests/syntax_tests/test_extends.py\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: from django.template import NodeList, TemplateSyntaxError\n2: from django.template.base import Node\n3: from django.template.loader_tags import ExtendsNode\n4: from django.test import SimpleTestCase\n5: \n6: from ..utils import setup\n7: \n8: inheritance_templates = {\n9:     \"inheritance01\": (\n10:         \"1{% block first %}&{% endblock %}3{% block second %}_{% endblock %}\"\n11:     ),\n12:     \"inheritance02\": \"{% extends 'inheritance01' %}\"\n13:     \"{% block first %}2{% endblock %}{% block second %}4{% endblock %}\",\n14:     \"inheritance03\": \"{% extends 'inheritance02' %}\",\n15:     \"inheritance04\": \"{% extends 'inheritance01' %}\",\n16:     \"inheritance05\": \"{% extends 'inheritance02' %}\",\n17:     \"inheritance06\": \"{% extends foo %}\",\n18:     \"inheritance07\": \"{% extends 'inheritance01' %}{% block second %}5{% endblock %}\",\n19:     \"inheritance08\": \"{% extends 'inheritance02' %}{% block second %}5{% endblock %}\",\n20:     \"inheritance09\": \"{% extends 'inheritance04' %}\",\n21:     \"inheritance10\": \"{% extends 'inheritance04' %}      \",\n22:     \"inheritance11\": \"{% extends 'inheritance04' %}\"\n23:     \"{% block first %}2{% endblock %}{% block second %}4{% endblock %}\",\n24:     \"inheritance12\": \"{% extends 'inheritance07' %}{% block first %}2{% endblock %}\",\n25:     \"inheritance13\": \"{% extends 'inheritance02' %}\"\n26:     \"{% block first %}a{% endblock %}{% block second %}b{% endblock %}\",\n27:     \"inheritance14\": (\n28:         \"{% extends 'inheritance01' %}{% block newblock %}NO DISPLAY{% endblock %}\"\n29:     ),\n30:     \"inheritance15\": \"{% extends 'inheritance01' %}\"\n31:     \"{% block first %}2{% block inner %}inner{% endblock %}{% endblock %}\",\n32:     \"inheritance16\": \"{% extends 'inheritance15' %}{% block inner %}out{% endblock %}\",\n33:     \"inheritance17\": \"{% load testtags %}{% block first %}1234{% endblock %}\",\n34:     \"inheritance18\": \"{% load testtags %}{% echo this that theother %}5678\",\n35:     \"inheritance19\": \"{% extends 'inheritance01' %}\"\n36:     \"{% block first %}{% load testtags %}{% echo 400 %}5678{% endblock %}\",\n37:     \"inheritance20\": (\n38:         \"{% extends 'inheritance01' %}{% block first %}{{ block.super }}a{% endblock %}\"\n39:     ),\n40:     \"inheritance21\": (\n41:         \"{% extends 'inheritance02' %}{% block first %}{{ block.super }}a{% endblock %}\"\n42:     ),\n43:     \"inheritance22\": (\n44:         \"{% extends 'inheritance04' %}{% block first %}{{ block.super }}a{% endblock %}\"\n45:     ),\n46:     \"inheritance23\": (\n47:         \"{% extends 'inheritance20' %}{% block first %}{{ block.super }}b{% endblock %}\"\n48:     ),\n49:     \"inheritance24\": \"{% extends context_template %}\"\n50:     \"{% block first %}2{% endblock %}{% block second %}4{% endblock %}\",\n51:     \"inheritance25\": \"{% extends context_template.1 %}\"\n52:     \"{% block first %}2{% endblock %}{% block second %}4{% endblock %}\",\n53:     \"inheritance26\": \"no tags\",\n54:     \"inheritance27\": \"{% extends 'inheritance26' %}\",\n55:     \"inheritance 28\": \"{% block first %}!{% endblock %}\",\n56:     \"inheritance29\": \"{% extends 'inheritance 28' %}\",\n57:     \"inheritance30\": \"1{% if optional %}{% block opt %}2{% endblock %}{% endif %}3\",\n58:     \"inheritance31\": \"{% extends 'inheritance30' %}{% block opt %}two{% endblock %}\",\n59:     \"inheritance32\": \"{% extends 'inheritance30' %}{% block opt %}two{% endblock %}\",\n60:     \"inheritance33\": (\n61:         \"1{% if optional == 1 %}{% block opt %}2{% endblock %}{% endif %}3\"\n62:     ),\n63:     \"inheritance34\": \"{% extends 'inheritance33' %}{% block opt %}two{% endblock %}\",\n64:     \"inheritance35\": \"{% extends 'inheritance33' %}{% block opt %}two{% endblock %}\",\n65:     \"inheritance36\": (\n66:         \"{% for n in numbers %}_{% block opt %}{{ n }}{% endblock %}{% endfor %}_\"\n67:     ),\n68:     \"inheritance37\": \"{% extends 'inheritance36' %}{% block opt %}X{% endblock %}\",\n69:     \"inheritance38\": \"{% extends 'inheritance36' %}{% block opt %}X{% endblock %}\",\n70:     \"inheritance39\": (\n71:         \"{% extends 'inheritance30' %}{% block opt %}new{{ block.super }}{% endblock %}\"\n72:     ),\n73:     \"inheritance40\": (\n74:         \"{% extends 'inheritance33' %}{% block opt %}new{{ block.super }}{% endblock %}\"\n75:     ),\n76:     \"inheritance41\": (\n77: \"inheritance_empty\": \"{% extends %}\",\n81:     \"extends_duplicate\": \"{% extends 'base.html' %}{% extends 'base.html' %}\",\n82:     \"duplicate_block\": (\n83:         \"{% extends 'base.html' 20. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "cat /home/claude/.local/lib/python3.11/site-packages/django/template/loader_tags.py",
  "description": "Read installed Django loader_tags.py"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "import posixpath\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\nfrom django.utils.safestring import mark_safe\n\nfrom .base import Node, Template, TemplateSyntaxError, TextNode, Variable, token_kwargs\nfrom .library import Library\n\nregister = Library()\n\nBLOCK_CONTEXT_KEY = \"block_context\"\n\n\nclass BlockContext:\n    def __init__(self):\n        # Dictionary of FIFO queues.\n        self.blocks = defaultdict(list)\n\n    def __repr__(self):\n        return f\"<{self.__class__.__qualname__}: blocks={self.blocks!r}>\"\n\n    def add_blocks(self, blocks):\n        for name, block in blocks.items():\n            self.blocks[name].insert(0, block)\n\n    def pop(self, name):\n        try:\n            return self.blocks[name].pop()\n        except IndexError:\n            return None\n\n    def push(self, name, block):\n        self.blocks[name].append(block)\n\n    def get_block(self, name):\n        try:\n            return self.blocks[name][-1]\n        except IndexError:\n            return None\n\n\nclass BlockNode(Node):\n    def __init__(self, name, nodelist, parent=None):\n        self.name = name\n        self.nodelist = nodelist\n        self.parent = parent\n\n    def __repr__(self):\n        return \"<Block Node: %s. Contents: %r>\" % (self.name, self.nodelist)\n\n    def render(self, context):\n        block_context = context.render_context.get(BLOCK_CONTEXT_KEY)\n        with context.push():\n            if block_context is None:\n                context[\"block\"] = self\n                result = self.nodelist.render(context)\n            else:\n                push = block = block_context.pop(self.name)\n                if block is None:\n                    block = self\n                # Create new block so we can store context without thread-safety issues.\n                block = type(self)(block.name, block.nodelist)\n                block.context = context\n                context[\"block\"] = block\n                result = block.nodelist.render(context)\n                if push is not None:\n                    block_context.push(self.name, push)\n        return result\n\n    def super(self):\n        if not hasattr(self, \"context\"):\n            raise TemplateSyntaxError(\n                \"'%s' object has no attribute 'context'. Did you use \"\n                \"{{ block.super }} in a base template?\" % self.__class__.__name__\n            )\n        render_context = self.context.render_context\n        if (\n            BLOCK_CONTEXT_KEY in render_context\n            and render_context[BLOCK_CONTEXT_KEY].get_block(self.name) is not None\n        ):\n            return mark_safe(self.render(self.context))\n        return \"\"\n\n\nclass ExtendsNode(Node):\n    must_be_first = True\n    context_key = \"extends_context\"\n\n    def __init__(self, nodelist, parent_name, template_dirs=None):\n        self.nodelist = nodelist\n        self.parent_name = parent_name\n        self.template_dirs = template_dirs\n        self.blocks = {n.name: n for n in nodelist.get_nodes_by_type(BlockNode)}\n\n    def __repr__(self):\n        return \"<%s: extends %s>\" % (self.__class__.__name__, self.parent_name.token)\n\n    def find_template(self, template_name, context):\n        \"\"\"\n        This is a wrapper around engine.find_template(). A history is kept in\n        the render_context attribute between successive extends calls and\n        passed as the skip argument. This enables extends to work recursively\n        without extending the same template twice.\n        \"\"\"\n        history = context.render_context.setdefault(\n            self.context_key,\n            [self.origin],\n        )\n        template, origin = context.template.engine.find_template(\n            template_name,\n            skip=history,\n        )\n        history.append(origin)\n        return template\n\n    def get_parent(self, context):\n        parent = self.parent_name.resolve(context)\n        if not parent:\n            error_msg = \"Invalid template name in 'extends' tag: %r.\" % parent\n            if self.parent_name.filters or isinstance(self.parent_name.var, Variable):\n                error_msg += (\n                    \" Got this from the '%s' variable.\" % self.parent_name.token\n                )\n            raise TemplateSyntaxError(error_msg)\n        if isinstance(parent, Template):\n            # parent is a django.template.Template\n            return parent\n        if isinstance(getattr(parent, \"template\", None), Template):\n            # parent is a django.template.backends.django.Template\n            return parent.template\n        return self.find_template(parent, context)\n\n    def render(self, context):\n        compiled_parent = self.get_parent(context)\n\n        if BLOCK_CONTEXT_KEY not in context.render_context:\n            context.render_context[BLOCK_CONTEXT_KEY] = BlockContext()\n        block_context = context.render_context[BLOCK_CONTEXT_KEY]\n\n        # Add the block nodes from this node to the block context\n        block_context.add_blocks(self.blocks)\n\n        # If this block's parent doesn't have an extends node it is the root,\n        # and its block nodes also need to be added to the block context.\n        for node in compiled_parent.nodelist:\n            # The ExtendsNode has to be the first non-text node.\n            if not isinstance(node, TextNode):\n                if not isinstance(node, ExtendsNode):\n                    blocks = {\n                        n.name: n\n                        for n in compiled_parent.nodelist.get_nodes_by_type(BlockNode)\n                    }\n                    block_context.add_blocks(blocks)\n                break\n\n        # Call Template._render explicitly so the parser context stays\n        # the same.\n        with context.render_context.push_state(compiled_parent, isolated_context=False):\n            return compiled_parent._render(context)\n\n\nclass IncludeNode(Node):\n    context_key = \"__include_context\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self, template, *args, extra_context=None, isolated_context=False, **kwargs\n    ):\n        self.template = template\n        self.extra_context = extra_context or {}\n        self.isolated_context = isolated_context\n        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)\n\n    def __repr__(self):\n        return f\"<{self.__class__.__qualname__}: template={self.template!r}>\"\n\n    def render(self, context):\n        \"\"\"\n        Render the specified template and context. Cache the template object\n        in render_context to avoid reparsing and loading when used in a for\n        loop.\n        \"\"\"\n        template = self.template.resolve(context)\n        # Does this quack like a Template?\n        if not callable(getattr(template, \"render\", None)):\n            # If not, try the cache and select_template().\n            template_name = template or ()\n            if isinstance(template_name, str):\n                template_name = (\n                    construct_relative_path(\n                        self.origin.template_name,\n                        template_name,\n                    ),\n                )\n            else:\n                template_name = tuple(template_name)\n            cache = context.render_context.dicts[0].setdefault(self, {})\n            template = cache.get(template_name)\n            if template is None:\n                template = context.template.engine.select_template(template_name)\n                cache[template_name] = template\n        # Use the base.Template of a backends.django.Template.\n        elif hasattr(template, \"template\"):\n            template = template.template\n        values = {\n            name: var.resolve(context) for name, var in self.extra_context.items()\n        }\n        if self.isolated_context:\n            return template.render(context.new(values))\n        with context.push(**values):\n            return template.render(context)\n\n\n@register.tag(\"block\")\ndef do_block(parser, token):\n    \"\"\"\n    Define a block that can be overridden by child templates.\n    \"\"\"\n    # token.split_contents() isn't useful here because this tag doesn't accept\n    # variable as arguments.\n    bits = token.contents.split()\n    if len(bits) != 2:\n        raise TemplateSyntaxError(\"'%s' tag takes only one argument\" % bits[0])\n    block_name = bits[1]\n    # Keep track of the names of BlockNodes found in this template, so we can\n    # check for duplication.\n    try:\n        if block_name in parser.__loaded_blocks:\n            raise TemplateSyntaxError(\n                \"'%s' tag with name '%s' appears more than once\" % (bits[0], block_name)\n            )\n        parser.__loaded_blocks.append(block_name)\n    except AttributeError:  # parser.__loaded_blocks isn't a list yet\n        parser.__loaded_blocks = [block_name]\n    nodelist = parser.parse((\"endblock\",))\n\n    # This check is kept for backwards-compatibility. See #3100.\n    endblock = parser.next_token()\n    acceptable_endblocks = (\"endblock\", \"endblock %s\" % block_name)\n    if endblock.contents not in acceptable_endblocks:\n        parser.invalid_block_tag(endblock, \"endblock\", acceptable_endblocks)\n\n    return BlockNode(block_name, nodelist)\n\n\ndef construct_relative_path(\n    current_template_name,\n    relative_name,\n    allow_recursion=False,\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    Convert a relative path (starting with './' or '../') to the full template\n    name based on the current_template_name.\n    \"\"\"\n    new_name = relative_name.strip(\"'\\\"\")\n    if not new_name.startswith((\"./\", \"../\")):\n        # relative_name is a variable or a literal that doesn't contain a\n        # relative path.\n        return relative_name\n\n    new_name = posixpath.normpath(\n        posixpath.join(\n            posixpath.dirname(current_template_name.lstrip(\"/\")),\n            new_name,\n        )\n    )\n    if new_name.startswith(\"../\"):\n        raise TemplateSyntaxError(\n            \"The relative path '%s' points outside the file hierarchy that \"\n            \"template '%s' is in.\" % (relative_name, current_template_name)\n        )\n    if not allow_recursion and current_template_name.lstrip(\"/\") == new_name:\n        raise TemplateSyntaxError(\n            \"The relative path '%s' was translated to template name '%s', the \"\n            \"same template in which the tag appears.\"\n            % (relative_name, current_template_name)\n        )\n    has_quotes = (\n        relative_name.startswith(('\"', \"'\")) and relative_name[0] == relative_name[-1]\n    )\n    return f'\"{new_name}\"' if has_quotes else new_name\n\n\n@register.tag(\"extends\")\ndef do_extends(parser, token):\n    \"\"\"\n    Signal that this template extends a parent template.\n\n    This tag may be used in two ways: ``{% extends \"base\" %}`` (with quotes)\n    uses the literal value \"base\" as the name of the parent template to extend,\n    or ``{% extends variable %}`` uses the value of ``variable`` as either the\n    name of the parent template to extend (if it evaluates to a string) or as\n    the parent template itself (if it evaluates to a Template object).\n    \"\"\"\n    bits = token.split_contents()\n    if len(bits) != 2:\n        raise TemplateSyntaxError(\"'%s' takes one argument\" % bits[0])\n    bits[1] = construct_relative_path(parser.origin.template_name, bits[1])\n    parent_name = parser.compile_filter(bits[1])\n    nodelist = parser.parse()\n    if nodelist.get_nodes_by_type(ExtendsNode):\n        raise TemplateSyntaxError(\n            \"'%s' cannot appear more than once in the same template\" % bits[0]\n        )\n    return ExtendsNode(nodelist, parent_name)\n\n\n@register.tag(\"include\")\ndef do_include(parser, token):\n    \"\"\"\n    Load a template and render it with the current context. You can pass\n    additional context using keyword arguments.\n\n    Example::\n\n        {% include \"foo/some_include\" %}\n        {% include \"foo/some_include\" with bar=\"BAZZ!\" baz=\"BING!\" %}\n\n    Use the ``only`` argument to exclude the current context when rendering\n    the included template::\n\n        {% include \"foo/some_include\" only %}\n        {% include \"foo/some_include\" with bar=\"1\" only %}\n    \"\"\"\n    bits = token.split_contents()\n    if len(bits) < 2:\n        raise TemplateSyntaxError(\n            \"%r tag takes at least one argument: the name of the template to \"\n            \"be included.\" % bits[0]\n        )\n    options = {}\n    remaining_bits = bits[2:]\n    while remaining_bits:\n        option = remaining_bits.pop(0)\n        if option in options:\n            raise TemplateSyntaxError(\n                \"The %r option was specified more than once.\" % option\n            )\n        if option == \"with\":\n            value = token_kwargs(remaining_bits, parser, support_legacy=False)\n            if not value:\n                raise TemplateSyntaxError(\n                    '\"with\" in %r tag needs at least one keyword argument.' % bits[0]\n                )\n        elif option == \"only\":\n            value = True\n        else:\n            raise TemplateSyntaxError(\n                \"Unknown argument for %r tag: %r.\" % (bits[0], option)\n            )\n        options[option] = value\n    isolated_context = options.get(\"only\", False)\n    namemap = options.get(\"with\", {})\n    bits[1] = construct_relative_path(\n        parser.origin.template_name,\n        bits[1],\n        allow_recursion=True,\n    )\n    return IncludeNode(\n        parser.compile_filter(bits[1]),\n        extra_context=namemap,\n        isolated_context=isolated_context,\n    )"
}

pull_request:\\n+    paths:\\n+      - 'tests/**/models.py'\\n+      - 'tests/**/migrations/**'\\n+      - 'tests/**/*migrations/**/*.py'\\n+      - 'scripts/check_migrations.py'\\n+  push:\\n+    branches:\\n+      - main\\n+\\n+concurrency:\\n+  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}\\n+  cancel-in-progress: true\\n+\\n+permissions:\\n+  contents: read\\n+\\n+jobs:\\n+  check-migrations:\\n+    name: Check test migrations\\n+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\\n+    timeout-minutes: 60\\n+\\n+    services:\\n+      postgres:\\n+        image: postgres:15-alpine\\n+        env:\\n+          POSTGRES_DB: django\\n+          POSTGRES_USER: user\\n+          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres\\n+        ports:\\n+          - 5432:5432\\n+        options: >-\\n+ actions/checkout@v6\\n+        with:\\n+          persist-credentials: false\\n+\\n+      - name: Set up Python\\n+        uses: actions/setup-python@v6\\n+        with:\\n+          python-version: '3.14'\\n+          cache: 'pip'\\n+          cache-dependency-path: 'tests/requirements/py3.txt'\\n+\\n+      - name: Install dependencies\\n+        run: |\\n+          python -m pip install import datetime\\n from functools import wraps\\n-\\n-from asgiref.sync import iscoroutinefunction\\n+from inspect import iscoroutinefunction\\n \\n from django.http import HttpResponseNotAllowed\\n from django.middleware.http import ConditionalGetMiddleware\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":4},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":8,\"startLine\":4},\"section\":\"Decorators for views based on HTTP headers.\"}],\"path\":\"django/views/decorators/http.py\"},{\"added\":1,\"deleted\":2,\"hunks\":[{\"body\":\" from functools import wraps\\n-\\n-from asgiref.sync import iscoroutinefunction\\n+from inspect import iscoroutinefunction\\n \\n from django.utils.cache import patch_vary_headers\\n \\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":5,\"startLine\":1},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":6,\"startLine\":1}}],\"path\":\"django/views/decorators/vary.py\"},{\"added\":1,\"deleted\":2,\"hunks\":[{\"body\":\" import logging\\n+from inspect import iscoroutinefunction, markcoroutinefunction\\n from urllib.parse import urlparse\\n \\n-from asgiref.sync import iscoroutinefunction, markcoroutinefunction\\n-\\n from django.core.exceptions import ImproperlyConfigured\\n from django.http import (\\n     HttpResponse,\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":1},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":8,\"startLine\":1}}],\"path\":\"django/views/generic/base.py\"},{\"added\":109,\"deleted\":6,\"hunks\":[{\"body\":\" * Bug fixers: anyone can contribute by opening a pull request and working on a\\n   solution for a ticket.\\n \\n-* Reviewers: anyone can review pull requests and suggest improvements.\\n+* Reviewers: anyone can :ref:`review pull requests <reviewing-patches>` and\\n+  suggest improvements.\\n \\n * Mergers: people with commit access who make the final decision to merge a\\n   change.\\n \\n-Our Trac system is intentionally open to the public, and anyone can help by\\n-working on tickets. Django is a community project, and we encourage\\n-:ref:`triage and collaboration by the community\\n-<how-can-i-help-with-triaging>`. This could be you!\\n+.. admonition:: When we say anyone, we mean you!\\n+\\n+    Our Trac system and GitHub repositories are intentionally open to the\\n+    public. Anyone can help by working on tickets, triaging tickets and\\n+    reviewing changes. Django is a community project, and we encourage\\n+    :ref:`triage and collaboration by the community\\n+    <how-can-i-help-with-triaging>`. We truly want you to help!\\n \\n For example, here's the typical lifecycle of a ticket:\\n \\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":19,\"startLine\":63},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":15,\"startLine\":63},\"section\":\"vote of the :ref:`Steering Council <steering-council>`.\"},{\"body\":\" * Please **don't** promote your own tickets to \\\"Ready for checkin\\\". You\\n   may mark other people's tickets that you've reviewed as \\\"Ready for\\n   checkin\\\", but you should get at minimum one other community member to\\n-  review a patch that you submit.\\n+  :ref:`review a patch <reviewing-patches>` that you submit.\\n \\n * Please **don't** reverse a decision without posting a message to the\\n   `Django Forum`_ to find consensus.\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":418},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":414},\"section\":\"the ticket database:\"},{\"body\":\" Now, report your results on the Trac ticket, and please include the regression\\n test as an attachment. When someone writes a fix for the bug, they'll already\\n have your test as a starting point.\\n+\\n+.. _reviewing-patches:\\n+\\n+Reviewing patches\\n+=================\\n+\\n+A great way to learn the Django codebase is to help review patches. It allows\\n+you to focus on a fixed scope of Django with a particular perspective. Plus,\\n+Django tends to receive more patches than it can review which causes the\\n+`review queue`_ to build up. Contributors can help Django tremendously by\\n+reviewing patches.\\n+\\n+The `review queue`_ is defined by tickets in Trac_ that are in the \\\"Accepted\\\"\\n+state with the flag \\\"has patch\\\" set and the flags \\\"patch needs improvement\\\",\\n+\\\"needs tests\\\" and \\\"needs documentation\\\" unset. There is also a set of\\n+`Pull Requests in GitHub with the \\\"no ticket\\\" label`_ that can be reviewed.\\n+\\n+Code review process\\n+-------------------\\n+\\n+The general process to reviewing a patch for Django is as follows:\\n+\\n+1. Pick a patch to review. This can be from the `review queue`_ or the\\n+   `Pull Requests in GitHub with the \\\"no ticket\\\" label`_. If you're new, pick\\n+   a specific component to stick with. Django is a big codebase, so it's best\\n+   to contribute consistently with a narrow focus and grow it slowly.\\n+\\n+2. Pull the branch. See the :ref:`handling-pull-requests`, including the git\\n+   alias to make checking out other pull requests easier.\\n+\\n+3. Check the tests. If the ticket is for a bug, there should be a regression\\n+   test. Confirm that the test fails if the change is reverted. Check that the\\n+   tests are adequately covering the code and logic as well. See\\n+   :ref:`patch-review-checklist`.\\n+\\n+4. Test in your own Django project. See :ref:`installing-development-version`\\n+   for how. Explore the user interface and user experience with the change.\\n+   Does it feel right? Can you break it? Another approach is to assume\\n+   everything is wrong. Can you demonstrate to yourself that each piece is\\n+   correct?\\n+\\n+5. Check the documentation. Is the documentation understandable? Does it\\n+   provide the information the reader needs at that moment? Does it read well?\\n+   Is it consistent with the documentation before and after it? See\\n+   :ref:`build-documentation-locally`.\\n+\\n+6. Are you happy with the patch? This is fairly subjective, but that's fine.\\n+   You should ask, does the approach make sense to you and is there another\\n+   way to do it that's better? Also ask yourself, is the scope of the change\\n+   appropriate? Are things named appropriately? Renaming things is slow and\\n+   challenging in Django, so we should try to get it right from the start.\\n+   Are there backwards compatibility concerns? Lastly, are there any other\\n+   general concerns such as things that may worry you or that should be looked\\n+   into?\\n+\\n+7. Review your feedback. Before you submit your feedback, consider the\\n+   feedback you want to share. It takes courage to open a Pull Request, w\\n... [diff truncated]\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":102,\"startLine\":488},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":3,\"startLine\":484},\"section\":\"commit where the test fails.\"}],\"path\":\"docs/internals/contributing/triaging-tickets.txt\"},{\"added\":5,\"deleted\":2,\"hunks\":[{\"body\":\"      $ source .venv/bin/activate\\n      $ python -m pip install -r docs/requirements.txt\\n \\n+.. _build-documentation-locally:\\n+\\n Build the documentation locally\\n -------------------------------\\n \\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":8,\"startLine\":124},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":6,\"startLine\":124},\"section\":\"Create and activate a virtual environment, then install the dependencies:\"},{\"body\":\" ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\\n \\n Before you commit your docs, it's a good idea to run the spelling checker.\\n-You'll need to install :pypi:`sphinxcontrib-spelling` first. Then from the\\n-``docs`` directory, run:\\n+You'll need to install :pypi:`sphinxcontrib-spelling` first. The spell checker\\n+also requires a system-level spell checking backend such as `Aspell\\n+<http://aspell.net/>`__. Then from the ``docs`` directory, run:\\n \\n .. console::\\n \\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":9,\"startLine\":180},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":8,\"startLine\":178},\"section\":\"Spelling check\"}],\"path\":\"docs/internals/contributing/writing-documentation.txt\"},{\"added\":1,\"deleted\":1,\"hunks\":[{\"body\":\" \\n * A Unix environment with these tools installed (in alphabetical order):\\n \\n-  * bash\\n+  * bash (version 4.0+)\\n   * git\\n   * GPG\\n   * make\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":63},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":63},\"section\":\"permissions.\"}],\"path\":\"docs/internals/howto-release-django.txt\"},{\"added\":2,\"deleted\":2,\"hunks\":[{\"body\":\" Reporting guidelines\\n --------------------\\n \\n-Include a runnable proof of concept\\n-~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\\n+Include a working proof of concept\\n+~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\\n \\n Please privately share a minimal Django project or code snippet that\\n demonstrates the potential vulnerability. Include clear instructions on how to\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":8,\"startLine\":46},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":8,\"startLine\":46},\"section\":\"the industry-standard 90 days. Confirmed vulnerabilities with a\"}],\"path\":\"docs/internals/security.txt\"},{\"added\":4,\"deleted\":4,\"hunks\":[{\"body\":\" .. code-block:: pycon\\n \\n     >>> City.objects.filter(name__trigram_similar=\\\"Middlesborough\\\")\\n-    ['<City: Middlesbrough>']\\n+    <QuerySet [<City: Middlesbrough>]>\\n \\n .. fieldlookup:: trigram_word_similar\\n \\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":28},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":28},\"section\":\"The ``trigram_similar`` lookup can be used on\"},{\"body\":\" .. code-block:: pycon\\n \\n     >>> Sentence.objects.filter(name__trigram_word_similar=\\\"Middlesborough\\\")\\n-    ['<Sentence: Gumby rides on the path of Middlesbrough>']\\n+    <QuerySet [<Sentence: Gumby rides on the path of Middlesbrough>]>\\n \\n .. fieldlookup:: trigram_strict_word_similar\\n \\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":54},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":54},\"section\":\"The ``trigram_word_similar`` lookup can be used on\"},{\"body\":\" .. code-block:: pycon\\n \\n     >>> City.objects.filter(name__unaccent=\\\"M\u00e9xico\\\")\\n-    ['<City: Mexico>']\\n+    <QuerySet [<City: Mexico>]>\\n \\n     >>> User.objects.filter(first_name__unaccent__startswith=\\\"Jerem\\\")\\n-    ['<User: Jeremy>', '<User: J\u00e9r\u00e9my>', '<User: J\u00e9r\u00e9mie>', '<User: Jeremie>']\\n+    <QuerySet [<User: Jeremy>, <User: J\u00e9r\u00e9my>, <User: J\u00e9r\u00e9mie>, <User: Jeremie>]>\\n \\n .. warning::\\n \\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":10,\"startLine\":99},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":10,\"startLine\":99},\"section\":\"The ``unaccent`` lookup can be used on\"}],\"path\":\"docs/ref/contrib/postgres/lookups.txt\"},{\"added\":1,\"deleted\":1,\"hunks\":[{\"body\":\" MariaDB notes\\n =============\\n \\n-Django supports MariaDB 10.6 and higher.\\n+Django supports MariaDB 10.11 and higher.\\n \\n To use MariaDB, use the MySQL backend, which is shared between the two. See the\\n :ref:`MySQL notes <mysql-notes>` for more details.\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":421},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":421},\"section\":\"non-durable <https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/non-durability.html>`_.\"}],\"path\":\"docs/ref/databases.txt\"},{\"added\":5,\"deleted\":5,\"hunks\":[{\"body\":\" \\n     Built-in database functions (such as\\n     :class:`~django.db.models.functions.Cast`) vary in whether arguments such\\n-    as ``output_field`` can be supplied positionally or only by keyword. For\\n-    ``output_field`` and several other cases, the input ultimately reaches\\n-    ``Func()`` as a keyword argument, so the advice to avoid constructing\\n-    keyword arguments from untrusted user input applies as equally to these\\n-    arguments as it does to ``**extra``.\\n+    as ``output_field`` can be supplied as positional arguments or only by\\n+    keyword. For ``output_field`` and several other cases, the input ultimately\\n+    reaches ``Func()`` as a keyword argument, so the advice to avoid\\n+    constructing keyword arguments from untrusted user input applies as equally\\n+    to these arguments as it does to ``**extra``.\\n \\n ``Aggregate()`` expressions\\n ---------------------------\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":11,\"startLine\":438},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":11,\"startLine\":438},\"section\":\"return type.\"}],\"path\":\"docs/ref/models/expressions.txt\"},{\"added\":5,\"deleted\":5,\"hunks\":[{\"body\":\" .. class:: UUIDField(**options)\\n \\n A field for storing universally unique identifiers. Uses Python's\\n-:class:`~python:uuid.UUID` class. When used on PostgreSQL and MariaDB 10.7+,\\n-this stores in a ``uuid`` datatype, otherwise in a ``char(32)``.\\n+:class:`~python:uuid.UUID` class. When used on PostgreSQL and MariaDB, this\\n+stores in a ``uuid`` datatype, otherwise in a ``char(32)``.\\n \\n Universally unique identifiers are a good alternative to :class:`AutoField` for\\n :attr:`~Field.primary_key`. The database will not generate the UUID for you, so\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":8,\"startLine\":1649},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":8,\"startLine\":1649},\"section\":\"Like all :class:`CharField` subclasses, :class:`URLField` takes the optional\"},{\"body\":\" Note that a callable (with the parentheses omitted) is passed to ``default``,\\n not an instance of ``UUID``.\\n \\n-.. admonition:: Lookups on PostgreSQL and MariaDB 10.7+\\n+.. admonition:: Lookups on PostgreSQL and MariaDB\\n \\n     Using :lookup:`iexact`, :lookup:`contains`, :lookup:`icontains`,\\n     :lookup:`startswith`, :lookup:`istartswith`, :lookup:`endswith`, or\\n     :lookup:`iendswith` lookups on PostgreSQL don't work for values without\\n-    hyphens, because PostgreSQL and MariaDB 10.7+ store them in a hyphenated\\n-    uuid datatype type.\\n+    hyphens, because PostgreSQL and MariaDB store them in a hyphenated uuid\\n+    datatype type.\\n \\n .. _relationship-fields:\\n \\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":13,\"startLine\":1667},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":13,\"startLine\":1667},\"section\":\"it is recommended to use :attr:`~Field.default`::\"}],\"path\":\"docs/ref/models/fields.txt\"},{\"added\":0,\"deleted\":5,\"hunks\":[{\"body\":\" For example ``Index(fields=['headline', '-pub_date'])`` would create SQL with\\n ``(headline, pub_date DESC)``.\\n \\n-.. admonition:: MariaDB\\n-\\n-    Index ordering isn't supported on MariaDB < 10.8. In that case, a\\n-    descending index is created as a normal index.\\n-\\n ``name``\\n --------\\n \\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":6,\"startLine\":81},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":11,\"startLine\":81},\"section\":\"field's name.\"}],\"path\":\"docs/ref/models/indexes.txt\"},{\"added\":17,\"deleted\":23,\"hunks\":[{\"body\":\"     >>> blogs = Blog.objects.alias(entries=Count(\\\"entry\\\")).filter(entries__gt=5)\\n \\n ``alias()`` can be used in conjunction with :meth:`annotate`, :meth:`exclude`,\\n-:meth:`filter`, :meth:`order_by`, and :meth:`update`. To use aliased expression\\n-with other methods (e.g. :meth:`aggregate`), you must promote it to an\\n-annotation::\\n+:meth:`filter`, :meth:`order_by`, and :meth:`update`. To use an aliased\\n+expression with other methods (e.g. :meth:`aggregate`), you must promote it to\\n+an annotation::\\n \\n     Blog.objects.alias(entries=Count(\\\"entry\\\")).annotate(\\n         entries=F(\\\"entries\\\"),\\n     ).aggregate(Sum(\\\"entries\\\"))\\n \\n :meth:`filter` and :meth:`order_by` can take expressions directly, but\\n expression construction and usage often does not happen in the same place (for\\n example, ``QuerySet`` method creates expressions, for later use in views).\\n-``alias()`` allows building complex expressions incrementally, possibly\\n-spanning multiple methods and modules, refer to the expression parts by their\\n-aliases and only use :meth:`annotate` for the final result.\\n+``alias()`` allows building complex expressions incrementally (possibly\\n+spanning multiple methods and modules), referring to the expression parts by\\n+their aliases, and only using :meth:`annotate` for the final result.\\n \\n ``order_by()``\\n ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":20,\"startLine\":330},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":20,\"startLine\":330},\"section\":\"interested in the exact number of entries, you could do this:\"},{\"body\":\" .. code-block:: pycon\\n \\n     >>> Author.objects.distinct()\\n-    [...]\\n-\\n+    <QuerySet [...]>\\n     >>> Entry.objects.order_by(\\\"pub_date\\\").distinct(\\\"pub_date\\\")\\n-    [...]\\n-\\n+    <QuerySet [...]>\\n     >>> Entry.objects.order_by(\\\"blog\\\").distinct(\\\"blog\\\")\\n-    [...]\\n-\\n+    <QuerySet [...]>\\n     >>> Entry.objects.order_by(\\\"author\\\", \\\"pub_date\\\").distinct(\\\"author\\\", \\\"pub_date\\\")\\n-    [...]\\n-\\n+    <QuerySet [...]>\\n     >>> Entry.objects.order_by(\\\"blog__name\\\", \\\"mod_date\\\").distinct(\\\"blog__name\\\", \\\"mod_date\\\")\\n-    [...]\\n-\\n+    <QuerySet [...]>\\n     >>> Entry.objects.order_by(\\\"author\\\", \\\"pub_date\\\").distinct(\\\"author\\\")\\n-    [...]\\n+    <QuerySet [...]>\\n \\n .. note::\\n     Keep in mind that :meth:`order_by` uses any default related model ordering\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":17,\"startLine\":549},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":22,\"startLine\":549},\"section\":\"Examples (those after the first will only work on PostgreSQL):\"},{\"body\":\" .. code-block:: pycon\\n \\n     >>> Pizza.objects.all()\\n-    [\\\"Hawaiian (ham, pineapple)\\\", \\\"Seafood (prawns, smoked salmon)\\\"...\\n+    <QuerySet [<Pizza: Hawaiian (ham, pineapple)>, <Pizza: Seafood (prawns, smoked salmon)>, ...]>\\n \\n The problem with this is that every time ``Pizza.__str__()`` asks for\\n ``self.toppings.all()`` it has to query the database, so\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":1199},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":1204},\"section\":\"and run:\"},{\"body\":\"     <QuerySet [<Person: ...>, ...]>\\n \\n The ``postgresql``, ``oracle``, and ``mysql`` database backends support\\n-``select_for_update()``. However, MariaDB only supports the ``nowait``\\n-argument, MariaDB 10.6+ also supports the ``skip_locked`` argument, and MySQL\\n-supports the ``nowait``, ``skip_locked``, and ``of`` arguments. The ``no_key``\\n-argument is only supported on PostgreSQL.\\n+``select_for_update()``. However, MariaDB only supports the ``nowait`` and\\n+``skip_locked`` arguments, and MySQL supports the ``nowait``, ``skip_locked``,\\n+and ``of`` arguments. The ``no_key`` argument is only supported on PostgreSQL.\\n \\n Passing ``nowait=True``, ``skip_locked=True``, ``no_key=True``, or ``of`` to\\n ``select_for_update()`` using database backends that do not support these\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":9,\"startLine\":1988},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":10,\"startLine\":1993},\"section\":\"them:\"},{\"body\":\" \\n     >>> prefetch = Prefetch(\\\"choice_set\\\", queryset=voted_choices, to_attr=\\\"voted_choices\\\")\\n     >>> Question.objects.prefetch_related(prefetch).get().voted_choices\\n-    [<Choice: The sky>]\\n+    <QuerySet [<Choice: The sky>]>\\n     >>> Question.objects.prefetch_related(prefetch).get().choice_set.all()\\n     <QuerySet [<Choice: Not much>, <Choice: The sky>, <Choice: Just hacking again>]>\\n \\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":4234},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":4240},\"section\":\"attribute:\"}],\"path\":\"docs/ref/models/querysets.txt\"},{\"added\":9,\"deleted\":0,\"hunks\":[{\"body\":\"+===========================\\n+Django 4.2.28 release notes\\n+===========================\\n+\\n+*February 3, 2026*\\n+\\n+Django 4.2.28 fixes three security issues with severity \\\"high\\\", two security\\n+issues with severity \\\"moderate\\\", and one security issue with severity \\\"low\\\" in\\n+4.2.27.\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":9,\"startLine\":1},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":0,\"startLine\":0}}],\"path\":\"docs/releases/4.2.28.txt\"},{\"added\":4,\"deleted\":7,\"hunks\":[{\"body\":\" Django 5.2.11 release notes\\n ===========================\\n \\n-*Expected February 3, 2026*\\n+*February 3, 2026*\\n \\n-Django 5.2.11 fixes several bugs in 5.2.10.\\n-\\n-Bugfixes\\n-========\\n-\\n-* ...\\n+Django 5.2.11 fixes three security issues with severity \\\"high\\\", two security\\n+issues with severity \\\"moderate\\\", and one security issue with severity \\\"low\\\" in\\n+5.2.10.\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":8,\"startLine\":2},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":11,\"startLine\":2}}],\"path\":\"docs/releases/5.2.11.txt\"},{\"added\":11,\"deleted\":3,\"hunks\":[{\"body\":\" Django 6.0.2 release notes\\n ==========================\\n \\n-*Expected February 3, 2026*\\n+*February 3, 2026*\\n \\n-Django 6.0.2 fixes several bugs in 6.0.1.\\n+Django 6.0.2 fixes three security issues with severity \\\"high\\\", two security\\n+issues with severity \\\"moderate\\\", one security issue with severity \\\"low\\\", and\\n+several bugs in 6.0.1.\\n \\n Bugfixes\\n ========\\n \\n-* ...\\n+* Fixed a visual regression in Django 6.0 that caused the admin filter sidebar\\n+  to wrap below the changelist when filter elements contained long text\\n+  (:ticket:`36850`).\\n+\\n+* Fixed a regression in Django 6.0 where ``auto_now_add`` field values were not\\n+  populated during ``INSERT`` operations, due to incorrect parameters passed to\\n+  ``field.pre_save()`` (:ticket:`36847`).\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":19,\"startLine\":2},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":11,\"startLine\":2}}],\"path\":\"docs/releases/6.0.2.txt\"},{\"added\":12,\"deleted\":2,\"hunks\":[{\"body\":\" * The admin site login view now redirects authenticated users to the next URL,\\n   if available, instead of always redirecting to the admin index page.\\n \\n+* The admin's ``FilteredSelectMultiple`` widget now uses ``<optgroup>``\\\\s to\\n+  preserve :ref:`named groups <field-choices-named-groups>` (e.g.\\n+  ``choices=[(\\\"Group\\\", [(\\\"1\\\", \\\"Item\\\")]), ...]``).\\n+\\n :mod:`django.contrib.admindocs`\\n ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\\n \\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":10,\"startLine\":95},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":6,\"startLine\":95},\"section\":\"Minor features\"},{\"body\":\" \\n * Management commands now set :class:`~argparse.ArgumentParser`\\\\'s\\n   ``suggest_on_error`` argument to ``True`` by default on Python 3.14, enabling\\n-  suggestions for mistyped subcommand names and argument choices.\\n+  suggestions for incorrectly typed subcommand names and argument choices.\\n \\n * The :djadmin:`loaddata` command now calls\\n   :data:`~django.db.models.signals.m2m_changed` signals with ``raw=True`` when\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":242},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":238},\"section\":\"Management Commands\"},{\"body\":\" ~~~~~~~~~\\n \\n * :func:`~django.utils.dateparse.parse_duration` now supports ISO 8601\\n-  durations expressed in weeks (``PnW``).\\n+  time periods expressed in weeks (``PnW``).\\n \\n Validators\\n ~~~~~~~~~~\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":340},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":336},\"section\":\"Utilities\"},{\"body\":\" Upstream support for MySQL 8.0 ends in April 2026, and MySQL 8.1-8.3 are\\n short-term innovation releases. Django 6.1 supports MySQL 8.4 and higher.\\n \\n+Dropped support for MariaDB < 10.11\\n+-----------------------------------\\n+\\n+Upstream support for MariaDB 10.6 ends in July 2026, and MariaDB 10.7-10.10 are\\n+short-term maintenance releases. Django 6.1 supports MariaDB 10.11 and higher.\\n+\\n Miscellaneous\\n -------------\\n \\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":12,\"startLine\":403},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":6,\"startLine\":399},\"section\":\"Dropped support for MySQL < 8.4\"}],\"path\":\"docs/releases/6.1.txt\"},{\"added\":1,\"deleted\":0,\"hunks\":[{\"body\":\" .. toctree::\\n    :maxdepth: 1\\n \\n+   4.2.28\\n    4.2.27\\n    4.2.26\\n    4.2.25\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":103},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":6,\"startLine\":103},\"section\":\"versions of the documentation contain the release notes for any later releases.\"}],\"path\":\"docs/releases/index.txt\"},{\"added\":5,\"deleted\":0,\"hunks\":[{\"body\":\" Aceh\\n admindocs\\n affordances\\n+ai\\n Ai\\n Alchin\\n allowlist\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":4},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":6,\"startLine\":4},\"section\":\"accessors\"},{\"body\":\" Kyrgyz\\n latin\\n lawrence\\n+lexeme\\n+lexemes\\n Libera\\n lifecycle\\n lifecycles\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":8,\"startLine\":246},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":6,\"startLine\":245},\"section\":\"kwargs\"},{\"body\":\" needsnewfeatureprocess\\n n\u011bmecky\\n nginx\\n+nitpicky\\n noding\\n nonces\\n nonnegative\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":316},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":6,\"startLine\":313},\"section\":\"needsinfo\"},{\"body\":\" unapplying\\n uncategorized\\n unclaim\\n+unclosed\\n uncopyable\\n unencoded\\n unencrypted\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":535},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":6,\"startLine\":531},\"section\":\"unapplied\"}],\"path\":\"docs/spelling_wordlist\"},{\"added\":2,\"deleted\":2,\"hunks\":[{\"body\":\" \\n .. note::\\n \\n-    Django uses ``asgiref.sync.iscoroutinefunction`` to test if your view is\\n+    Django uses ``inspect.iscoroutinefunction`` to test if your view is\\n     asynchronous or not. ``asgiref.sync.iscoroutinefunction``.\\n+coroutine function, using ``inspect.iscoroutinefunction``.\\n \\n The ``django.utils.decorators`` module contains\\n :func:`~django.utils.decorators.sync_only_middleware`,\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":318},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":318},\"section\":\"If your middleware has both id=\\\"id\\\"></select>').appendTo('#qunit-fixture');\\n+    const grp = $('<optgroup label=\\\"group one\\\">').appendTo('#id');\\n+    $('<option value=\\\"0\\\">A</option>').appendTo(grp);\\n+ option').length, 2);\\n+    assert.equal($('#id optgroup').length, 1);\\n+});\\n+\\n+QUnit.test('sort optgroups', function(assert) {\\n+    const $ = django.jQuery;\\n+    $('<select id=\\\"id\\\"></select>').appendTo('#qunit-fixture');\\n+    // Add optgroups in non-alphabetical order\\n+    const grp2 = $('<optgroup label=\\\"Group B\\\">').appendTo('#id');\\n+    $('<option value=\\\"3\\\">Item 3</option>').appendTo(grp2);\\n+    $('<option value=\\\"4\\\">Item 4</option>').appendTo(grp2);\\n+    const grp1 = $('<optgroup label=\\\"Group A\\\">').appendTo('#id');\\n+ by group then by item\\n+    assert.equal(SelectBox.cache.id.length, 4);\\n+    assert.equal(SelectBox.cache.id[0].group, 'Group A');\\n+    assert.equal(SelectBox.cache.id[0].text, 'Item 1');\\n+ assert.equal(SelectBox.cache.id[2].text, 'Item 3');\\n+    assert.equal(SelectBox.cache.id[3].group, 'Group B');\\n+    assert.equal(SelectBox.cache.id[3].text, 'Item 4');\\n+});\\n+\\n+QUnit.test('do Check against the list to prevent duplicate errors.\\n+        if app not in installed_apps:\\n+            installed_apps.append(app)\\n+    apps.set_installed_apps(installed_apps)\\n+\\n+    # Note: We don't use check=True here because --check calls sys.exit(1)\\n+    # instead of raising CommandError when migrations are missing.\\n+    call_command(\\\"makemigrations\\\", \\\"--check\\\", verbosity=3)\\n+\\n+\\n+if __name__ == \\\"__main__\\\":\\n+    main()\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":31,\"startLine\":1},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":0,\"startLine\":0}}],\"path\":\"scripts/check_migrations.py\"},{\"added\":7,\"deleted\":0,\"hunks\":[{\"body\":\" class ProxyUser(User):\\n     class Meta:\\n         proxy = True\\n+\\n+\\n+class MixedFieldsModel(models.Model):\\n+    \\\"\\\"\\\"Model with multiple field types for testing search validation.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n+\\n+    int_field = models.IntegerField(null=True, blank=True)\\n+    json_field = models.JSONField(null=True, blank=True)\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":10,\"startLine\":140},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":3,\"startLine\":140},\"section\":\"class CharPK(models.Model):\"}],\"path\":\"tests/admin_changelist/models.py\"},{\"added\":85,\"deleted\":0,\"hunks\":[{\"body\":\"     Group,\\n     Invitation,\\n     Membership,\\n+    MixedFieldsModel,\\n     Musician,\\n     OrderedObject,\\n     Parent,\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":66},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":6,\"startLine\":66},\"section\":\"from .models import (\"},{\"body\":\"         cl = m.get_changelist_instance(request)\\n         self.assertCountEqual(cl.queryset, [abcd])\\n \\n+    def test_exact_lookup_with_invalid_value(self):\\n+        Child.objects.create(name=\\\"Test\\\", age=10)\\n+        m = admin.ModelAdmin(Child, custom_site)\\n+        m.search_fields = [\\\"pk__exact\\\"]\\n+\\n+        request = self.factory.get(\\\"/\\\", data={SEARCH_VAR: \\\"foo\\\"})\\n+        request.user = self.superuser\\n+\\n+        # Invalid values are gracefully ignored.\\n+        cl = m.get_changelist_instance(request)\\n+        self.assertCountEqual(cl.queryset, [])\\n+\\n+    def test_exact_lookup_mixed_terms(self):\\n+        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n+        Multi-term search validates each term independently.\\n+\\n+        For 'foo 123' with search_fields=['name__icontains', 'age__exact']:\\n+        - 'foo': age lookup skipped (invalid), name lookup used\\n+        - '123': both lookups used (valid for age)\\n+        No Cast should be used; invalid lookups are simply skipped.\\n+        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n+        child = Child.objects.create(name=\\\"foo123\\\", age=123)\\n+        Child.objects.create(name=\\\"other\\\", age=456)\\n+        m = admin.ModelAdmin(Child, custom_site)\\n+        m.search_fields = [\\\"name__icontains\\\", \\\"age__exact\\\"]\\n+\\n+        request = self.factory.get(\\\"/\\\", data={SEARCH_VAR: \\\"foo 123\\\"})\\n+        request.user = self.superuser\\n+\\n+        # One result matching on foo and 123.\\n+        cl = m.get_changelist_instance(request)\\n+        self.assertCountEqual(cl.queryset, [child])\\n+\\n+        # \\\"xyz\\\" - invalid for age (skipped), no match for name either.\\n+        request = self.factory.get(\\\"/\\\", data={SEARCH_VAR: \\\"xyz\\\"})\\n+        request.user = self.superuser\\n+        cl = m.get_changelist_instance(request)\\n+        self.assertCountEqual(cl.queryset, [])\\n+\\n+    def test_exact_lookup_with_more_lenient_formfield(self):\\n+        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n+        Exact lookups on BooleanField use formfield().to_python() for lenient\\n+        parsing. Using model field's to_python() would reject 'false' whereas\\n+        the form field accepts it.\\n+        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n+        obj = UnorderedObject.objects.create(bool=False)\\n+        UnorderedObject.objects.create(bool=True)\\n+        m = admin.ModelAdmin(UnorderedObject, custom_site)\\n+        m.search_fields = [\\\"bool__exact\\\"]\\n+\\n+        # 'false' is accepted by form field but rejected by model field.\\n+        request = self.factory.get(\\\"/\\\", data={SEARCH_VAR: \\\"false\\\"})\\n+        request.user = self.superuser\\n+\\n+        cl = m.get_changelist_instance(request)\\n+        self.assertCountEqual(cl.queryset, [obj])\\n+\\n+    @skipUnlessDBFeature(\\\"supports_primitives_in_json_field\\\")\\n+    def test_exact_lookup_validates_each_field_independently(self):\\n+        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n+        Each field validates the search term independently without leaking\\n+        converted values between fields.\\n+\\n+        \\\"3.\\\" is valid for IntegerField (converts to 3) but invalid for\\n+        JSONField. The converted value must not leak to the JSONField check.\\n+        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n+        # obj_int has int_field=3, should match \\\"3.\\\" via IntegerF\\n...       {
        "added": 68,
        "deleted": 0,
        "hunks": [
          {
            "body": "+name: Check migrations\n+\n+on:\n+  pull_request:\n+    paths:\n+      - 'tests/**/models.py'\n+      - 'tests/**/migrations/**'\n+      - 'tests/**/*migrations/**/*.py'\n+      - 'scripts/check_migrations.py'\n+  push:\n+    branches:\n+      - main\n+\n+concurrency:\n+  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}\n+  cancel-in-progress: true\n+\n+permissions:\n+  contents: read\n+\n+jobs:\n+  check-migrations:\n+    name: Check test migrations\n+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n+    timeout-minutes: 60\n+\n+    services:\n+      postgres:\n+        image: postgres:15-alpine\n+        env:\n+          POSTGRES_DB: django\n+          POSTGRES_USER: user\n+          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres\n+        ports:\n+          - 5432:5432\n+        options: >-\n+          --health-cmd pg_isready\n+          --health-interval 10s\n+          --health-timeout 5s\n+          --health-retries 5\n+\n+    steps:\n+      - name: Checkout\n+        uses: actions/checkout@v6\n+        with:\n+          persist-credentials: false\n+\n+      - name: Set up Python\n+        uses: actions/setup-python@v6\n+        with:\n+          python-version: '3.14'\n+          cache: 'pip'\n+          cache-dependency-path: 'tests/requirements/py3.txt'\n+\n+      - name: Install dependencies\n+        run: |\n+          python -m pip install --upgrade pip wheel\n+          python -m pip install -e . psycopg[binary]\n+\n+      - name: Create PostgreSQL settings file\n+        run: mv ./.github/workflows/data/test_postgres.py.tpl ./tests/test_postgres.py\n+\n+      - name: Check for missing migrations\n+        env:\n+          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: test_postgres\n+          PYTHONPATH: ${{ github.workspace }}/tests:${{ github.workspace }}\n+        run: |\n+          python scripts/check_migrations.py\n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 68,
              "startLine": 1
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 0,
              "startLine": 0
            }
          }
        ],
        "path": ".github/workflows/check-migrations.yml"
      },

            "body": " from django.core.paginator import Paginator\n from django.db import models, router, transaction\n from django.db.models.constants import LOOKUP_SEP\n-from django.db.models.functions import Cast\n from django.forms.formsets import DELETION_FIELD_NAME, all_valid\n from django.forms.models import (\n     BaseInlineFormSet,\n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 6,
              "startLine": 41
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 7,
              "startLine": 40
            },
            "section": "from django.core.exceptions import ("
          },
          {
            "body": " from django.views.generic import RedirectView\n \n IS_POPUP_VAR = \"_popup\"\n+SOURCE_MODEL_VAR = \"_source_model\"\n TO_FIELD_VAR = \"_to_field\"\n IS_FACETS_VAR = \"_facets\"\n \n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 7,
              "startLine": 71
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 6,
              "startLine": 71
            },
            "section": "from django.views.decorators.csrf import csrf_protect"
          },
          {
            "body": " \n         # Apply keyword searches.\n         def construct_search(field_name):\n+            \"\"\"\n+            Return a tuple of (lookup, field_to_validate).\n+\n+            field_to_validate is set for non-text exact lookups so that\n+            invalid search terms can be skipped (preserving index usage).\n+            \"\"\"\n             if field_name.startswith(\"^\"):\n                 return \"%s__istartswith\" % field_name.removeprefix(\"^\"), None\n             elif field_name.startswith(\"=\"):\n",

            "body": "         may_have_duplicates = False\n         search_fields = self.get_search_fields(request)\n         if search_fields and search_term:\n-            str_aliases = {}\n             orm_lookups = []\n             for field in search_fields:\n-                lookup, str_alias = construct_search(str(field))\n-                orm_lookups.append(lookup)\n-                if str_alias:\n-                    str_aliases[lookup] = str_alias\n-\n-            if str_aliases:\n-                queryset = queryset.alias(**str_aliases)\n+                orm_lookups.append(construct_search(str(field)))\n \n             term_queries = []\n             for bit in smart_split(search_term):\n                 if bit.startswith(('\"', \"'\")) and bit[0] == bit[-1]:\n                     bit = unescape_string_literal(bit)\n-                or_queries = models.Q.create(\n-                    [(orm_lookup, bit) for orm_lookup in orm_lookups],\n-                    connector=models.Q.OR,\n-                )\n-                term_queries.append(or_queries)\n-            queryset = queryset.filter(models.Q.create(term_queries))\n+                #             "body": "             else:\n                 attr = obj._meta.pk.attname\n             value = obj.serializable_value(attr)\n-            popup_response_data = json.dumps(\n-                {\n-                    \"value\": str(value),\n-                    \"obj\": str(obj),\n-                }\n-            )\n+            popup_response = {\n+                \"value\": str(value),\n+                \"obj\": str(obj),\n+            }\n+\n+            #     const box = document.getElementById(id);\n             SelectBox.cache[id] = [];\n             const cache = SelectBox.cache[id];\n             for (const node of box.options) {\n-                cache.push({value: node.value, text: node.text, displayed: 1});\n+                const group = getOptionGroupName(node);\n+                cache.push({group, value: node.value, text: node.text, displayed: 1});\n+            }\n+            // Only sort if there are any groups (to preserve existing behavior for non-grouped selects)\n+            if (cache.some(item => item.group)) {\n+                SelectBox.sort(id);\n             }\n         },\n         redisplay: function(id) {\n             // Repopulate HTML select box from cache\n             const box = document.getElementById(id);\n             const scroll_value_from_top = box.scrollTop;\n             box.innerHTML = '';\n-            for (const node of SelectBox.cache[id]) {\n-                if (node.displayed) {\n-                    const new_option = new Option(node.text, node.value, false, false);\n+            let node = box;\n+            let currentOptgroup = null;\n+            for           {
            "body": " import logging\n+from inspect import iscoroutinefunction, markcoroutinefunction\n from urllib.parse import urlparse\n \n-from asgiref.sync import iscoroutinefunction, markcoroutinefunction\n-\n from django.core.exceptions import ImproperlyConfigured\n from django.http import (\n     HttpResponse,\n",

        "path": "django/views/generic/base.py"
      },
      {
        "added": 109,
        "deleted": 6,
        "hunks": [
          {
            "body": " * Bug fixers: anyone can contribute by opening a pull request and working on a\n   solution for a ticket.\n \n-* Reviewers: anyone can review pull requests and suggest improvements.\n+* Reviewers: anyone can :ref:`review pull requests <reviewing-patches>` and\n+  suggest improvements.\n \n * Mergers: people with commit access who make the final decision to merge a\n   change.\n \n-Our Trac system is intentionally open to the public, and anyone can help by\n-working on tickets. Django is a community project, and we encourage\n-:ref:`triage and collaboration by the community\n-<how-can-i-help-with-triaging>`. This could be you!\n+.. admonition:: When we say anyone, we mean you!\n+\n+    Our Trac system and GitHub repositories are intentionally open to the\n+    public. Anyone can help by working on tickets, triaging tickets and\n+    reviewing changes. Django is a community project, and we encourage\n+    :ref:`triage and collaboration by the community\n+    <how-can-i-help-with-triaging>`. We truly want you to help!\n \n For example, here's the typical lifecycle of a ticket:\n \n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 19,
              "startLine": 63
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 15,
              "startLine": 63
            },
            "section": "vote of the :ref:`Steering Council <steering-council>`."
          },
          {
            "body": " * Please **don't** promote your own tickets to \"Ready for checkin\". You\n   may mark other people's tickets that you've reviewed as \"Ready for\n   checkin\", but you should get at minimum one other community member to\n-  review a patch that you submit.\n+  :ref:`review a patch <reviewing-patches>` that you submit.\n \n * Please **don't** reverse a decision without posting a message to the\n   `Django Forum`_ to find consensus.\n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 7,
              "startLine": 418
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 7,
              "startLine": 414
            },
            "section": "the ticket database:"
          },
          {
            "body": " Now, report your results on the Trac ticket, and please include the regression\n test as an attachment. When someone writes a fix for the bug, they'll already\n have your test as a starting point.\n+\n+.. _reviewing-patches:\n+\n+Reviewing patches\n+=================\n+\n+A great way to learn the Django codebase is to help review patches. It allows\n+you to focus on a fixed scope of Django with a particular perspective. Plus,\n+Django tends to receive more patches than it can review which causes the\n+`review queue`_ to build up. Contributors can help Django tremendously by\n+reviewing patches.\n+\n+The `review queue`_ is defined by tickets in Trac_ that are in the \"Accepted\"\n+state with the flag \"has patch\" set and the flags \"patch needs improvement\",\n+\"needs tests\" and \"needs documentation\" unset. There is also a set of\n+`Pull Requests in GitHub with the \"no ticket\" label`_ that can be reviewed.\n+\n+Code review process\n+-------------------\n+\n+The general process to reviewing a patch for Django is as follows:\n+\n+1. Pick a patch to review. This can be from the `review queue`_ or the\n+   `Pull Requests in GitHub with the \"no ticket\" label`_. If you're new, pick\n+   a specific component to stick with. Django is a big codebase, so it's best\n+   to contribute consistently with a narrow focus and grow it slowly.\n+\n+2. Pull the branch. See the :ref:`handling-pull-requests`, including the git\n+   alias to make checking out other pull requests easier.\n+\n+3. Check the tests. If the ticket is for a bug, there should be a regression\n+   test. Confirm that the test fails if the change is reverted. Check that the\n+   tests are adequately covering the code and logic as well. See\n+   :ref:`patch-review-checklist`.\n+\n+4. Test in your own Django project. See :ref:`installing-development-version`\n+   for how. Explore the user interface and user experience with the change.\n+   Does it feel right? Can you break it? Another approach is to assume\n+   everything is wrong. Can you demonstrate to yourself that each piece is\n+   correct?\n+\n+5. Check the documentation. Is the documentation understandable? Does it\n+   provide the information the reader needs at that moment? Does it read well?\n+   Is it consistent with the documentation before and after it? See\n+   :ref:`build-documentation-locally`.\n+\n+6. Are you happy with the patch? This is fairly subjective, but that's fine.\n+   You should ask, does the approach make sense to you and is there another\n+   way to do it that's better? Also ask yourself, is the scope of the change\n+   appropriate? Are things named appropriately? Renaming things is slow and\n+   challenging in Django, so we should try to get it right from the start.\n+   Are there backwards compatibility concerns? Lastly, are there any other\n+   general concerns such as things that may worry you or that should be looked\n+   into?\n+\n+7. Review your feedback. Before you submit your feedback, consider the\n+   feedback you want to share. It takes courage to open a Pull Request, w\n... [diff truncated]",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 102,
              "startLine": 488
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 3,
              "startLine": 484
            },
            "section": "commit where the test fails."
          }
        ],
        "path": "docs/internals/contributing/triaging-tickets.txt"
      },
      {
        "added": 5,
        "deleted": 2,
        "hunks": [
          {
            "body": "      $ source .venv/bin/activate\n      $ python -m pip install -r docs/requirements.txt\n \n+.. _build-documentation-locally:\n+\n Build the documentation locally\n -------------------------------\n \n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 8,
              "startLine": 124
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 6,
              "startLine": 124
            },
            "section": "Create and activate a virtual environment, then install the dependencies:"
          },
          {
            "body": " ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n \n Before you commit your docs, it's a good idea to run the spelling checker.\n-You'll need to install :pypi:`sphinxcontrib-spelling` first. Then from the\n-``docs`` directory, run:\n+You'll need to install :pypi:`sphinxcontrib-spelling` first. The spell checker\n+also requires a system-level spell checking backend such as `Aspell\n+<http://aspell.net/>`__. Then from the ``docs`` directory, run:\n \n .. console::\n \n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 9,
              "startLine": 180
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 8,
              "startLine": 178
            },
            "section": "Spelling check"
          }
        ],
        "path": "docs/internals/contributing/writing-documentation.txt"
      },
      {
        "added": 1,
        "deleted": 1,
        "hunks": [
          {
            "body": " \n * A Unix environment with these tools installed (in alphabetical order):\n \n-  * bash\n+  * bash (version 4.0+)\n   * git\n   * GPG\n   * make\n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 7,
              "startLine": 63
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 7,
              "startLine": 63
            },
            "section": "permissions."
          }
        ],
        "path": "docs/internals/howto-release-django.txt"
      },
      {
        "added": 2,
        "deleted": 2,
        "hunks": [
          {
            "body": " Reporting guidelines\n --------------------\n \n-Include a runnable proof of concept\n-~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n+Include a working proof of concept\n+~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n \n Please privately share a minimal Django project or code snippet that\n demonstrates the potential vulnerability. Include clear instructions on how to\n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 8,
              "startLine": 46
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 8,
              "startLine": 46
            },
            "section": "the industry-standard 90 days. Confirmed vulnerabilities with a"
          }
        ],
        "path": "docs/internals/security.txt"
      },
      {
        "added": 4,
        "deleted": 4,
        "hunks": [
          {
            "body": " .. code-block:: pycon\n \n     >>> City.objects.filter(name__trigram_similar=\"Middlesborough\")\n-    ['<City: Middlesbrough>']\n+    <QuerySet [<City: Middlesbrough>]>\n \n .. fieldlookup:: trigram_word_similar\n \n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 7,
              "startLine": 28
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 7,
              "startLine": 28
            },
            "section": "The ``trigram_similar`` lookup can be used on"
          },
          {
            "body": " .. code-block:: pycon\n \n     >>> Sentence.objects.filter(name__trigram_word_similar=\"Middlesborough\")\n-    ['<Sentence: Gumby rides on the path of Middlesbrough>']\n+    <QuerySet [<Sentence: Gumby rides on the path of Middlesbrough>]>\n \n .. fieldlookup:: trigram_strict_word_similar\n \n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 7,
              "startLine": 54
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 7,
              "startLine": 54
            },
            "section": "The ``trigram_word_similar`` lookup can be used on"
          },
          {
            "body": " .. code-block:: pycon\n \n     >>> City.objects.filter(name__unaccent=\"M\u00e9xico\")\n-    ['<City: Mexico>']\n+    <QuerySet [<City: Mexico>]>\n \n     >>> User.objects.filter(first_name__unaccent__startswith=\"Jerem\")\n-    ['<User: Jeremy>', '<User: J\u00e9r\u00e9my>', '<User: J\u00e9r\u00e9mie>', '<User: Jeremie>']\n+    <QuerySet [<User: Jeremy>, <User: J\u00e9r\u00e9my>, <User: J\u00e9r\u00e9mie>, <User: Jeremie>]>\n \n .. warning::\n \n",
            "newRange": {
