No signature found.\n1005:                                 current = context.template.engine.string_if_invalid\n1006:                             else:\n1007:                                 try:\n1008:                                     signature.bind()\n1009:                                 except TypeError:  # Arguments *were* required.\n1010:                                     # Invalid method call.\n1011:                                     current = context.template.engine.string_if_invalid\n1012:                                 else:\n1013:                                     raise\n1014:         except Exception as e:\n1015:             template_name = getattr(context, \"template_name\", None) or \"unknown\"\n1016:             logger.debug(\n1017:                 \"Exception while resolving variable '%s' in template '%s'.\",\n1018:                 bit,\n1019:                 template_name,\n1020:                 exc_info=True,\n1021:             )\n1022: \n1023:             if getattr(e, \"silent_variable_failure\", False):\n1024:                 current = context.template.engine.string_if_invalid\n1025:             else:\n1026:                 raise\n1027: \n1028:         return current\n1029: \n1030: \n1031: class Node:\n1032:     # Set this to True for nodes that must be first in the template (although\n1033:     # they can be preceded by text nodes.\n1034:     must_be_first = False\n1035:     child_nodelists = (\"nodelist\",)\n1036:     token = None\n1037: \n1038:     def render(self, context):\n1039:         \"\"\"\n1040:         Return the node rendered as a string.\n1041:         \"\"\"\n1042:         pass\n1043: \n1044:     def render_annotated(self, context):\n1045:         \"\"\"\n1046:         Render the node. If debug is True and an exception occurs during\n1047:         rendering, the exception is annotated with contextual line information\n1048:         where it occurred in the template. For internal usage this method is\n1049:         preferred over using the render method directly.\n1050:         \"\"\"\n1051:         try:\n1052:             return self.render(context)\n1053:         except Exception as e:\n1054:             if context.template.engine.debug:\n1055:                 # Store the actual node that caused the exception.\n1056:                 if not hasattr(e, \"_culprit_node\"):\n1057:                     e._culprit_node = self\n1058:                 if (\n1059:                     not hasattr(e, \"template_debug\")\n1060:                     and context.render_context.template.origin == e._culprit_node.origin\n1061:                 ):\n1062:                     e.template_debug = (\n1063:                         context.render_context.template.get_exception_info(\n1064:                             e,\n1065:                             e._culprit_node.token,\n1066:                         )\n1067:                     )\n1068:             raise\n1069: \n1070:     def get_nodes_by_type(self, nodetype):\n1071:         \"\"\"\n1072:         Return a list of all nodes (within this node and its nodelist)\n1073:         of the given type\n1074:         \"\"\"\n1075:         nodes = []\n1076:         if isinstance(self, nodetype):\n1077:             nodes.append(self)\n1078:         for attr in self.child_nodelists:\n1079:             nodelist = getattr(self, attr, None)\n1080:             if nodelist:\n1081:                 nodes.extend(nodelist.get_nodes_by_type(nodetype))\n1082:         return nodes\n1083: \n1084: \n1085: class NodeList(list):\n1086:     # Set to True the first time a non-TextNode is inserted by\n1087:     # extend_nodelist().\n1088:     contains_nontext = False\n1089: \n1090:     def render(self, context):\n1091:         return SafeString(\"\".join([node.render_annotated(context) for node in self]))\n1092: \n1093:     def get_nodes_by_type(self, nodetype):\n1094:         \"Return a list of all nodes of the given type\"\n1095:         nodes = []\n1096:         for node in self:\n1097:             nodes.extend(node.get_nodes_by_type(nodetype))\n1098:         return nodes\n1099: \n1100: \n1101: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c@674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3/-/blob/django/template/base.py%3FL980-1100"
  }

35. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "import posixpath\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\nfrom django.utils.safestring import mark_safe\n\nfrom .base import Node, Template, TemplateSyntaxError, TextNode, Variable, token_kwargs\nfrom .library import Library\n\nregister = Library()\n\nBLOCK_CONTEXT_KEY = \"block_context\"\n\n\nclass BlockContext:\n    def __init__(self):\n        # Dictionary of FIFO queues.\n        self.blocks = defaultdict(list)\n\n    def __repr__(self):\n        return f\"<{self.__class__.__qualname__}: blocks={self.blocks!r}>\"\n\n    def add_blocks(self, blocks):\n        for name, block in blocks.items():\n            self.blocks[name].insert(0, block)\n\n    def pop(self, name):\n        try:\n            return self.blocks[name].pop()\n        except IndexError:\n            return None\n\n    def push(self, name, block):\n        self.blocks[name].append(block)\n\n    def get_block(self, name):\n        try:\n            return self.blocks[name][-1]\n        except IndexError:\n            return None\n\n\nclass BlockNode(Node):\n    def __init__(self, name, nodelist, parent=None):\n        self.name = name\n        self.nodelist = nodelist\n        self.parent = parent\n\n    def __repr__(self):\n        return \"<Block Node: %s. Contents: %r>\" % (self.name, self.nodelist)\n\n    def render(self, context):\n        block_context = context.render_context.get(BLOCK_CONTEXT_KEY)\n        with context.push():\n            if block_context is None:\n                context[\"block\"] = self\n                result = self.nodelist.render(context)\n            else:\n                push = block = block_context.pop(self.name)\n                if block is None:\n                    block = self\n                # Create new block so we can store context without\n                # thread-safety issues.\n                block = type(self)(block.name, block.nodelist)\n                block.context = context\n                context[\"block\"] = block\n                result = block.nodelist.render(context)\n                if push is not None:\n                    block_context.push(self.name, push)\n        return result\n\n    def super(self):\n        if not hasattr(self, \"context\"):\n            raise TemplateSyntaxError(\n                \"'%s' object has no attribute 'context'. Did you use \"\n                \"{{ block.super }} in a base template?\" % self.__class__.__name__\n            )\n        render_context = self.context.render_context\n        if (\n            BLOCK_CONTEXT_KEY in render_context\n            and render_context[BLOCK_CONTEXT_KEY].get_block(self.name) is not None\n        ):\n            return mark_safe(self.render(self.context))\n        return \"\"\n\n\nclass ExtendsNode(Node):\n    must_be_first = True\n    context_key = \"extends_context\"\n\n    def __init__(self, nodelist, parent_name, template_dirs=None):\n        self.nodelist = nodelist\n        self.parent_name = parent_name\n        self.template_dirs = template_dirs\n        self.blocks = {n.name: n for n in nodelist.get_nodes_by_type(BlockNode)}\n\n    def __repr__(self):\n        return \"<%s: extends %s>\" % (self.__class__.__name__, self.parent_name.token)\n\n    def find_template(self, template_name, context):\n        \"\"\"\n        This is a wrapper around engine.find_template(). A history is kept in\n        the render_context attribute between successive extends calls and\n        passed as the skip argument. This enables extends to work recursively\n        without extending the same template twice.\n        \"\"\"\n        history = context.render_context.setdefault(\n            self.context_key,\n            [self.origin],\n        )\n        template, origin = context.template.engine.find_template(\n            template_name,\n            skip=history,\n        )\n        history.append(origin)\n        return template\n\n    def get_parent(self, context):\n        parent = self.parent_name.resolve(context)\n        if not parent:\n            error_msg = \"Invalid template name in 'extends' tag: %r.\" % parent\n            if self.parent_name.filters or isinstance(self.parent_name.var, Variable):\n                error_msg += (\n                    \" Got this from the '%s' variable.\" % self.parent_name.token\n                )\n            raise TemplateSyntaxError(error_msg)\n        if isinstance(parent, Template):\n            # parent is a django.template.Template\n            return parent\n        if isinstance(getattr(parent, \"template\", None), Template):\n            # parent is a django.template.backends.django.Template\n            return parent.template\n        return self.find_template(parent, context)\n\n    def render(self, context):\n        compiled_parent = self.get_parent(context)\n\n        if BLOCK_CONTEXT_KEY not in context.render_context:\n            context.render_context[BLOCK_CONTEXT_KEY] = BlockContext()\n        block_context = context.render_context[BLOCK_CONTEXT_KEY]\n\n        # Add the block nodes from this node to the block context\n        block_context.add_blocks(self.blocks)\n\n        # If this block's parent doesn't have an extends node it is the root,\n        # and its block nodes also need to be added to the block context.\n        for node in compiled_parent.nodelist:\n            # The ExtendsNode has to be the first non-text node.\n            if not isinstance(node, TextNode):\n                if not isinstance(node, ExtendsNode):\n                    blocks = {\n                        n.name: n\n                        for n in compiled_parent.nodelist.get_nodes_by_type(BlockNode)\n                    }\n                    block_context.add_blocks(blocks)\n                break\n\n        # Call Template._render explicitly so the parser context stays\n        # the same.\n        with context.render_context.push_state(compiled_parent, isolated_context=False):\n            return compiled_parent._render(context)\n",
  "path": "/workspace/sg_loader_tags.py"
}

repr(self.dicts)\\n49: \\n50:     def __iter__(self):\\n51:         return reversed(self.dicts)\\n52: \\n53:     def push(self, *args, **kwargs):\\n54:         dicts = []\\n55:         for d in = use_tz\\n148:         self.template_name = \\\"unknown\\\"\\n149:         self.render_context = RenderContext()\\n150:         # Set to the original template -- as opposed to extended or included\\n151:         # templates -- during rendering, see bind_template.\\n152:         self.template = None\\n153:         super().__init__(dict_)\\n154: \\n155:     @contextmanager\\n156:     def template\\n160:         try:\\n161:             yield\\n162:         finally:\\n163:             self.template = None\\n164: \\n165:     def __copy__(self):\\n166:         duplicate = super().__copy__()\\n167:         duplicate.render_context = copy(self.render_context)\\n168:         return duplicate\\n169: \\n170:     def update(self, other_dict):\\n171:         \\\"Push other_dict to the stack of dictionaries in the Context\\\"\\n172:         if not hasattr(other_dict, \\\"__getitem__\\\"):\\n173:             raise TypeError(\\\"other_dict must be a mapping (dictionary-like) object.\\\")\\n174:         if isinstance(other_dict, BaseContext):\\n175:             other_dict = other_dict.dicts[1:].pop()\\n176:         return ContextDict(self, other_dict)\\n177: \\n178: \\n179: class RenderContext(BaseContext):\\n180:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n181:     A stack container for storing Template state.\\n182: \\n183:     RenderContext simplifies the implementation of template Nodes by providing\\n184:     a safe place to store state between invocations of a node's `render`\\n185:     method.\\n186: \\n187:     The RenderContext also provides scoping rules that are more sensible for\\n188:     'template local' variables. The render context stack is pushed before each\\n189:     template is rendered, creating a fresh scope with nothing in it. Name\\n190:     resolution fails if a variable is not found at the top of the\\n191:     RequestContext stack. Thus, variables are local to a specific template and\\n192:     don't affect the rendering of other templates as they would if they were\\n193:     stored in the normal template context.\\n194:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n195: \\n196:     template = None\\n197: \\n198:     def __iter__(self):\\n199:         yield from self.dicts[-1]\\n200: \\n201:     def __contains__(self, key):\\n202:         return key in self.dicts[-1]\\n203: \\n204:     def get(self, key, otherwise=None):\\n205:         return self.dicts[-1].get(key, otherwise)\\n206: \\n207:     def __getitem__(self, key):\\n208:         return self.dicts[-1][key]\\n209: \\n210:     @contextmanager\\n211:     def push_state(self, template, isolated_context=True):\\n212:         initial = self.template\\n213:         self.template = template\\n214:         if isolated_context:\\n215:             self.push()\\n216:         try:\\n217:             yield\\n218:         finally:\\n219:             self.template = initial\\n220:             if isolated_context:\\n221:                 self.pop()\\n222: \\n223: \\n224: class RequestContext(Context):\\n225:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n226:     This subclass of template.Context automatically populates itself using\\n227:     the processors defined in the engine's configuration.\\n228:     Additional processors can be specified as a list of callables\\n229:     using the \\\"processors\\\" keyword argument.\\n230:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n231: \\n232:     def __init__(\\n233:         self,\\n234:         request,\\n235:         dict_=None,\\n236:         processors=None,\\n237:         use_l10n=None,\\n238:         use_tz=None,\\n239:         autoescape=True,\\n240:     ):\\n241:         super().__init__(dict_, use_l10n=use_l10n, use_tz=use_tz, autoescape=autoescape)\\n242:         self.request = request\\n243:         self._processors = () if processors is None else tuple(processors)\\n244:         self._processors_index = len(self.dicts)\\n245: \\n246:         # placeholder for context processors output\\n247:         self.update({})\\n248: \\n249:         # empty dict for any new modifications\\n250:         # (so that context processors don't overwrite them)\\n251:         self.update({})\\n252: \\n253:     @contextmanager\\n254:     def bind_template(self, template):\\n255:         if self.template is not None:\\n256:             raise RuntimeError(\\\"Context is already bound to a template\\\")\\n257: \\n258:         self.template = template\\n259:         # Set context processors according to the template engine's settings.\\n260:         processors = template.engine.template_context_processors + self._processors\\n261:         updates = {}\\n262:         for processor in processors:\\n263:             context = processor(self.request)\\n264:             try:\\n265:                 updates.update(context)\\n266:             except TypeError as e:\\n267:                 raise TypeError(\\n268:                     f\\\"Context processor {processor.__qualname__} didn't return a \\\"\\n269:                     \\\"dictionary.\\\"\\n270:                 ) from e\\n271: \\n272:         self.dicts[self._processors_index] = updates\\n273: \\n274:         try:\\n275:             yield\\n276:         finally:\\n277:             self.template = None\\n278:             # Unset context processors.\\n279:             self.dicts[self._processors_index] = {}\\n280: \\n281:     def new(self, values=None):\\n282:         new_context = super().new(values)\\n283:         # This is for backwards-compatibility: RequestContexts created via\\n284:         # Context.new don't include values from context processors.\\n285:         if hasattr(new_context, \\\"_processors_index\\\"):\\n286:             del new_context._processors_index\\n287:         return new_context\\n288: \\n289: \\n290: def make_context(context, request=None, **kwargs):\\n291:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n292:     Create a suitable Context from a plain dict and optionally an HttpRequest.\\n293:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n294:     if context is not None and not isinstance(context, dict):\\n295:         raise TypeError(\\n296:             \\\"context must be a dict rather than %s.\\\" % context.__class__.__name__\\n297:         )\\n298:     if request is None:\\n299:         context = Context(context, **kwargs)\\n300:     else:\\n301:         # The following pattern is required to ensure values from\\n302:         # context override those from template context processors.\\n303:         original_context = context\\n304:         context = RequestContext(request, **kwargs)\\n305:         if original_context:\\n306:             context.push(original_context)\\n307:     return context\\n308: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c@674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3/-/blob/django/template/context.py\"}",

value, starting at the current context and going\n86:         upward\n87:         \"\"\"\n88:         for d in reversed(self.dicts):\n89:             if key in d:\n90:                 return d[key]\n91:         raise KeyError(key)\n92: \n93:     def __delitem__(self, key):\n94:         \"Delete a variable from the current context\"\n95:         del self.dicts[-1][key]\n96: \n97:     def __contains__(self, key):\n98:         return any(key in d for d in self.dicts)\n99: \n100:     def get(self, key, otherwise=None):\n101:         for d in reversed(self.dicts):\n102:             if key in d:\n103:                 return d[key]\n104:         return otherwise\n105: \n106:     def setdefault(self, key, default=None):\n107:         try:\n108:             return self[key]\n109:         except KeyError:\n110:             self[key] = default\n111:         return default\n112: \n113:     def new(self, values=None):\n114:         \"\"\"\n115:         Return a new context with the same properties, but with only the\n116:         values given in 'values' stored.\n117:         \"\"\"\n118:         new_context = copy(self)\n119:         new_context._reset_dicts(values)\n120:         return new_context\n121: \n122:     def flatten(self):\n123:         \"\"\"\n124:         Return self.dicts as one dictionary.\n125:         \"\"\"\n126:         flat = {}\n127:         for d in self.dicts:\n128:             flat.update(d)\n129:         return flat\n130: \n131:     def __eq__(self, other):\n132:         \"\"\"\n133:         Compare two contexts by comparing theirs 'dicts' attributes.\n134:         \"\"\"\n135:         if not isinstance(other, BaseContext):\n136:             return NotImplemented\n137:         # flatten dictionaries because they can be put in a different order.\n138:         return self.flatten() == other.flatten()\n139: \n140: \n141: class Context(BaseContext):\n142:     \"A stack container for variable context\"\n143: \n144:     def __init__(self, dict_=None, autoescape=True, use_l10n=None, use_tz=None):\n145:         self.autoescape = autoescape\n146:         self.use_l10n = use_l10n\n147:         self.use_tz = use_tz\n148:         self.template_name = \"unknown\"\n149:         self.render_context = RenderContext()\n150:         # Set to the original template -- as opposed to extended or included\n151:         # templates -- during rendering, see bind_template.\n152:         self.template = None\n153:         super().__init__(dict_)\n154: \n155:     @contextmanager\n156:     def bind_template(self, template):\n157:         if self.template is not None:\n158:             raise RuntimeError(\"Context is already bound to a template\")\n159:         self.template = template\n160:         try:\n161:             yield\n162:         finally:\n163:             self.template = None\n164: \n165:     def __copy__(self):\n166:         duplicate = super().__copy__()\n167:         duplicate.render_context = copy(self.render_context)\n168:         return duplicate\n169: \n170:     def update(self, other_dict):\n171:         \"Push other_dict to the stack of dictionaries in the Context\"\n172:         if not hasattr(other_dict, \"__getitem__\"):\n173:             raise TypeError(\"other_dict must be a mapping (dictionary-like) object.\")\n174:         if isinstance(other_dict, BaseContext):\n175:             other_dict = other_dict.dicts[1:].pop()\n176:         return ContextDict(self, other_dict)\n177: \n178: \n179: class RenderContext(BaseContext):\n180:     \"\"\"\n181:     A stack container for storing Template state.\n182: \n183:     RenderContext simplifies the implementation of template Nodes by providing\n184:     a safe place to store state between invocations of a node's `render`\n185:     method.\n186: \n187:     The RenderContext also provides scoping rules that are more sensible for\n188:     'template local' variables. The render context stack is pushed before each\n189:     template is rendered, creating a fresh scope with nothing in it. Name\n190:     resolution fails if a variable is not found at the top of the\n191:     RequestContext stack. Thus, variables are local to a specific template and\n192:     don't affect the rendering of other templates as they would if they were\n193:     stored in the normal template context.\n194:     \"\"\"\n195: \n196:     template = None\n197: \n198:     def __iter__(self):\n199:         yield from self.dicts[-1]\n200: \n201:     def __contains__(self, key):\n202:         return key in self.dicts[-1]\n203: \n204:     def get(self, key, otherwise=None):\n205:         return self.dicts[-1].get(key, otherwise)\n206: \n207:     def __getitem__(self, key):\n208:         return self.dicts[-1][key]\n209: \n210:     @contextmanager\n211:     def push_state(self, template, isolated_context=True):\n212:         initial = self.template\n213:         self.template = template\n214:         if isolated_context:\n215:             self.push()\n216:         try:\n217:             yield\n218:         finally:\n219:             self.template = initial\n220:             if isolated_context:\n221:                 self.pop()\n222: \n223: \n224: class RequestContext(Context):\n225:     \"\"\"\n226:     This subclass of template.Context automatically populates itself using\n227:     the processors defined in the engine's configuration.\n228:     Additional processors can be specified as a list of callables\n229:     using the \"processors\" keyword argument.\n230:     \"\"\"\n231: \n232:     def __init__(\n233:         self,\n234:         request,\n235:         dict_=None,\n236:         processors=None,\n237:         use_l10n=None,\n238:         use_tz=None,\n239:         autoescape=True,\n240:     ):\n241:         super().__init__(dict_, use_l10n=use_l10n, use_tz=use_tz, autoescape=autoescape)\n242:         self.request = request\n243:         self._processors = () if processors is None else tuple(processors)\n244:         self._processors_index = len(self.dicts)\n245: \n246:         # placeholder for context processors output\n247:         self.update({})\n248: \n249:         # empty dict for any new modifications\n250:         # (so that context processors don't overwrite them)\n251:         self.update({})\n252: \n253:     @contextmanager\n254:     def bind_template(self, template):\n255:         if self.template is not None:\n256:             raise RuntimeError(\"Context is already bound to a template\")\n257: \n258:         self.template = template\n259:         # Set context processors according to the template engine's settings.\n260:         processors = template.engine.template_context_processors + self._processors\n261:         updates = {}\n262:         for processor in processors:\n263:             context = processor(self.request)\n264:             try:\n265:                 updates.update(context)\n266:             except TypeError as e:\n267:                 raise TypeError(\n268:                     f\"Context processor {processor.__qualname__} didn't return a \"\n269:                     \"dictionary.\"\n270:                 ) from e\n271: \n272:         self.dicts[self._processors_index] = updates\n273: \n274:         try:\n275:             yield\n276:         finally:\n277:             self.template = None\n278:             # Unset context processors.\n279:             self.dicts[self._processors_index] = {}\n280: \n281:     def new(self, values=None):\n282:         new_context = super().new(values)\n283:         # This is for backwards-compatibility: RequestContexts created via\n284:         # Context.new don't include values from context processors.\n285:         if hasattr(new_context, \"_processors_index\"):\n286:             del new_context._processors_index\n287:         return new_context\n288: \n289: \n290: def make_context(context, request=None, **kwargs):\n291:     \"\"\"\n292:     Create a suitable Context from a plain dict and optionally an HttpRequest.\n293:     \"\"\"\n294:     if context is not None and not isinstance(context, dict):\n295:         raise TypeError(\n296:             \"context must be a dict rather than %s.\" % context.__class__.__name__\n297:         )\n298:     if request is None:\n299:         context = Context(context, **kwargs)\n300:     else:\n301:         # The following pattern is required to ensure values from\n302:         # context override those from template context processors.\n303:         original_context = context\n304:         context = RequestContext(request, **kwargs)\n305:         if original_context:\n306:             context.push(original_context)\n307:     return context\n308: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c@674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3/-/blob/django/template/context.py"
  }
}

40. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "cat > /tmp/test_nested_inheritance.py << 'EOF'\nimport sys\nsys.path.insert(0, '/home/claude/.local/lib/python3.11/site-packages')\n\nfrom django.template import Engine, Context\n\nengine = Engine()\n\n# 3-level inheritance where middle template overrides OUTER block\n# and child template overrides INNER block (nested inside outer)\n# base.html: defines outer block containing inner block\n# layout.html: overrides outer block (which still has inner block inside)\n# page.html: overrides inner block only\n\ntemplates = {\n    \"base.html\": \"{% block outer %}[outer-base]{% block inner %}[inner-base]{% endblock %}[/outer-base]{% endblock %}\",\n    \"layout.html\": \"{% extends 'base.html' %}{% block outer %}[outer-layout]{% block inner %}[inner-layout]{% endblock %}[/outer-layout]{% endblock %}\",\n    \"page.html\": \"{% extends 'layout.html' %}{% block inner %}[inner-page]{% endblock %}\",\n}\n\n# Use locmem loader\nfrom django.template.loaders.locmem import Loader\nengine = Engine(loaders=[\n    ('django.template.loaders.locmem.Loader', templates),\n])\n\ntemplate = engine.get_template(\"page.html\")\noutput = template.render(Context({}))\nprint(f\"Output: {output!r}\")\nprint(f\"Expected: '[outer-layout][inner-page][/outer-layout]'\")\nprint(f\"Pass: {output == '[outer-layout][inner-page][/outer-layout]'}\")\nEOF\npython3.11 /tmp/test_nested_inheritance.py",
  "description": "Test nested block inheritance with 3 levels"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "Output: '[outer-layout][inner-page][/outer-layout]'\nExpected: '[outer-layout][inner-page][/outer-layout]'\nPass: True"
}

expectedFailure, skip\\n+\\n+        module_or_class_name, _, name_to_mark = test_name.rpartition(\\\".\\\")\\n+        test_app = test_name.split(\\\".\\\")[0]\\n+        # Importing a test app that isn't installed raises RuntimeError.\\n+        if test_app in settings.INSTALLED_APPS:\\n+            try:\\n+                test_frame = import_string(module_or_class_name)\\n+            except ImportError:\\n+                # import_string() can raise ImportError if a submodule's parent\\n+                # module hasn't already been imported during test discovery.\\n+                # This can happen in at least two cases:\\n+                # 1. When running a subset of tests in a module, the test\\n+                #    runner won't import tests in that module's other\\n+                #    submodules.\\n+                # 2. When the parallel test runner spawns workers with an empty\\n+                #    import cache.\\n+                test_to_mark = import_string(test_name)\\n+                test_frame = sys.modules.get(test_to_mark.__module__)\\n+            else:\\n+                test_to_mark = getattr(test_frame, name_to_mark)\\n+            if skip_reason:\\n+                setattr(test_frame, name_to_mark, skip(skip_reason)(test_to_mark))\\n+            else:\\n+                setattr(test_frame, name_to_mark, expectedFailure(test_to_mark))\\n... [diff truncated]\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":4,\"startLine\":1},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":4,\"startLine\":1}}],\"path\":\"docs/internals/_images/contribution_process.svg\"},{\"added\":3,\"deleted\":0,\"hunks\":[{\"body\":\" * Don't reopen issues that have been marked \\\"wontfix\\\" without finding consensus\\n   to do so on the `Django Forum`_.\\n \\n+* Don't reopen issues that have been marked \\\"needsnewfeatureprocess\\\" without\\n+  shepherding an issue through the `new feature ideas`_ GitHub project.\\n+\\n * Don't use the ticket tracker for lengthy discussions, because they're\\n   likely to get lost. If a particular ticket is controversial, please move the\\n   discussion to the `Django Forum`_.\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":9,\"startLine\":27},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":6,\"startLine\":27},\"section\":\"Before reporting a bug on the `ticket tracker\"}],\"path\":\"docs/internals/contributing/bugs-and-features.txt\"},{\"added\":11,\"deleted\":4,\"hunks\":[{\"body\":\"       close a ticket. Always use the forum to get a consensus before reopening\\n       tickets closed as \\\"wontfix\\\".\\n \\n+* needsnewfeatureprocess\\n+      Used when the ticket merits a new feature, which will need to get\\n+      community input and support.\\n+      See the :ref:`process for suggesting new features <requesting-features>`.\\n+\\n * duplicate\\n       Used when another ticket covers the same issue. By closing duplicate\\n       tickets, we keep all the discussion in one place, which helps\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":11,\"startLine\":327},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":6,\"startLine\":327},\"section\":\"A ticket can be resolved in a number of ways:\"},{\"body\":\" If you believe that the ticket was closed in error -- because you're\\n still having the issue, or it's popped up somewhere else, or the triagers have\\n made a mistake -- please reopen the ticket and provide further information.\\n-Again, please do not reopen tickets that have been marked as \\\"wontfix\\\" and\\n-bring the issue to the `Django Forum`_ instead.\\n+Again, please do not reopen tickets that have been marked as \\\"wontfix\\\" or\\n+\\\"needsnewfeatureprocess\\\". For \\\"wontfix\\\" tickets, bring the issue to the\\n+`Django Forum`_ instead. For \\\"needsnewfeatureprocess\\\" tickets, propose the\\n+feature through the :ref:`new features process <requesting-features>`.\\n \\n .. _how-can-i-help-with-triaging:\\n \\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":10,\"startLine\":349},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":8,\"startLine\":344},\"section\":\"A ticket can be resolved in a number of ways:\"},{\"body\":\" \\n Then, you can help out by:\\n \\n-* Closing \\\"Unreviewed\\\" tickets as \\\"invalid\\\", \\\"worksforme\\\", or \\\"duplicate\\\", or\\n-  \\\"wontfix\\\".\\n+* Closing \\\"Unreviewed\\\" tickets as \\\"invalid\\\", \\\"worksforme\\\", \\\"duplicate\\\",\\n+  \\\"wontfix\\\", or \\\"needsnewfeatureprocess\\\".\\n \\n * Closing \\\"Unreviewed\\\" tickets as \\\"needsinfo\\\" when the description is too\\n   sparse to be actionable.\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":8,\"startLine\":368},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":8,\"startLine\":361},\"section\":\"reset page`_.\"}],\"path\":\"docs/internals/contributing/triaging-tickets.txt\"},{\"added\":1,\"deleted\":1,\"hunks\":[{\"body\":\"     docs\\n     isort>=7.0.0\\n     lint-docs\\n-    zizmor>=1.16.3\\n+    zizmor>=1.19.0\\n \\n Testing other Python versions and database backends\\n ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":85},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":85},\"section\":\"default environments can be seen as follows:\"}],\"path\":\"docs/internals/contributing/writing-code/unit-tests.txt\"},{\"added\":2,\"deleted\":2,\"hunks\":[{\"body\":\" \\n Django's documentation uses a custom ``console`` directive for documenting\\n command-line examples involving ``django-admin``, ``manage.py``, ``python``,\\n-etc.). In the HTML documentation, it renders a two-tab UI, with one tab showing\\n+etc. In the HTML documentation, it renders a two-tab UI, with one tab showing\\n a Unix-style command prompt and a second tab showing a Windows prompt.\\n \\n For example, you can replace this fragment:\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":520},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":520},\"section\":\"define some extra description units:\"},{\"body\":\" \\n General improvements or other changes to the APIs that should be emphasized\\n should use the \\\"``.. versionchanged:: X.Y``\\\" directive (with the same format\\n-as the ``versionadded`` mentioned above.\\n+as the ``versionadded`` mentioned above).\\n \\n These ``versionadded`` and ``versionchanged`` blocks should be\\n \\\"self-contained.\\\" In other words, since we only keep these annotations around\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":584},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":584},\"section\":\"blank line and an optional description (indented).\"}],\"path\":\"docs/internals/contributing/writing-documentation.txt\"},{\"added\":2,\"deleted\":4,\"hunks\":[{\"body\":\"         >>> wgs84 = SpatialReference(\\\"EPSG:4326\\\")  # EPSG string\\n         >>> proj = \\\"+proj=longlat +ellps=WGS84 +datum=WGS84 +no_defs \\\"\\n         >>> wgs84 = SpatialReference(proj)  # PROJ string\\n-        >>> wgs84 = SpatialReference(\\n-        ...     \\\"\\\"\\\"GEOGCS[\\\"WGS 84\\\",\\n+        >>> wgs84 = SpatialReference(\\\"\\\"\\\"GEOGCS[\\\"WGS 84\\\",\\n         ... DATUM[\\\"WGS_1984\\\",\\n         ...      SPHEROID[\\\"WGS 84\\\",6378137,298.257223563,\\n         ...          AUTHORITY[\\\"EPSG\\\",\\\"7030\\\"]],\\n         ...      AUTHORITY[\\\"EPSG\\\",\\\"6326\\\"]],\\n         ... PRIMEM[\\\"Greenwich\\\",0,\\n         ...      AUTHORITY[\\\"EPSG\\\",\\\"8901\\\"]],\\n         ... UNIT[\\\"degree\\\",0.01745329251994328,\\n         ...      AUTHORITY[\\\"EPSG\\\",\\\"9122\\\"]],\\n-        ... AUTHORITY[\\\"EPSG\\\",\\\"4326\\\"]]\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n-        ... )  # OGC WKT\\n+        ... AUTHORITY[\\\"EPSG\\\",\\\"4326\\\"]]\\\"\\\"\\\")  # OGC WKT\\n \\n     .. method:: __getitem__(target)\\n \\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":16,\"startLine\":1072},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":18,\"startLine\":1072},\"section\":\"Coordinate System Objects\"}],\"path\":\"docs/ref/contrib/gis/gdal.txt\"},{\"added\":0,\"deleted\":1,\"hunks\":[{\"body\":\"     from django.contrib.postgres.fields import RangeOperators\\n     from django.db.models import Deferrable\\n \\n-\\n     ExclusionConstraint(\\n         name=\\\"exclude_overlapping_deferred\\\",\\n         expressions=[\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":6,\"startLine\":105},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":7,\"startLine\":105},\"section\":\"are ``Deferrable.DEFERRED`` or ``Deferrable.IMMEDIATE``. For example::\"}],\"path\":\"docs/ref/contrib/postgres/constraints.txt\"}],\"hasNextPage\":true,\"head\":\"674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3~20\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c\",\"totalAdded\":347,\"totalDeleted\":213,\"totalModified\":50}",

      {
        "added": 68,
        "deleted": 0,
        "hunks": [
          {
            "body": "+name: Check migrations\n+\n+on:\n+  pull_request:\n+    paths:\n+      - 'tests/**/models.py'\n+      - 'tests/**/migrations/**'\n+      - 'tests/**/*migrations/**/*.py'\n+      - 'scripts/check_migrations.py'\n+  push:\n+    branches:\n+      - main\n+\n+concurrency:\n+  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}\n+  cancel-in-progress: true\n+\n+permissions:\n+  contents: read\n+\n+jobs:\n+  check-migrations:\n+    name: Check test migrations\n+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n+    timeout-minutes: 60\n+\n+    services:\n+      postgres:\n+        image: postgres:15-alpine\n+        env:\n+          POSTGRES_DB: django\n+          POSTGRES_USER: user\n+          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres\n+        ports:\n+          - 5432:5432\n+        options: >-\n+          --health-cmd pg_isready\n+          --health-interval 10s\n+          --health-timeout 5s\n+          --health-retries 5\n+\n+    steps:\n+      - name: Checkout\n+        uses: actions/checkout@v6\n+        with:\n+          persist-credentials: false\n+\n+      - name: Set up Python\n+        uses: actions/setup-python@v6\n+        with:\n+          python-version: '3.14'\n+          cache: 'pip'\n+          cache-dependency-path: 'tests/requirements/py3.txt'\n+\n+      - name: Install dependencies\n+        run: |\n+          python -m pip install --upgrade pip wheel\n+          python -m pip install -e . psycopg[binary]\n+\n+      - name: Create PostgreSQL settings file\n+        run: mv ./.github/workflows/data/test_postgres.py.tpl ./tests/test_postgres.py\n+\n+      - name: Check for missing migrations\n+        env:\n+          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: test_postgres\n+          PYTHONPATH: ${{ github.workspace }}/tests:${{ github.workspace }}\n+        run: |\n+          python scripts/check_migrations.py\n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 68,
              "startLine": 1
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 0,
              "startLine": 0
            }
          }
        ],
        "path": ".github/workflows/check-migrations.yml"
      },
      {
        "added": 0,
        "deleted": 1,
        "hunks": [
          {
            "body": " \n jobs:\n   coverage:\n-    if: github.repository == 'django/django'\n     name: Coverage Tests (Windows)\n     runs-on: windows-latest\n     timeout-minutes: 60\n",

        "added": 4,

        "hunks": [
          {
            "body": " repos:\n   - repo: https://github.com/psf/black-pre-commit-mirror\n-    rev: 25.9.0\n+    rev: 26.1.0\n     hooks:\n     - id: black\n       exclude: \\.py-tpl$\n   - repo: https://github.com/adamchainz/blacken-docs\n     rev: 1.20.0\n     hooks:\n       - id: blacken-docs\n         additional_dependencies:\n-        - black==25.9.0\n+        - black==26.1.0\n         files: 'docs/.*\\.txt$'\n         args: [\"--rst-literal-block\"]\n   - repo: https://github.com/PyCQA/isort\n",

              "startLine": 1

          },

            "body": "     hooks:\n       - id: flake8\n   - repo: https://github.com/pre-commit/mirrors-eslint\n-    rev: v9.36.0\n+    rev: v9.39.1\n     hooks:\n       - id: eslint\n   - repo: https://github.com/zizmorcore/zizmor-pre-commit\n-    rev: v1.16.3 \n+    rev: v1.19.0\n     hooks:\n     - id: zizmor\n",

          {
            "body": " from urllib.parse import urlsplit\n \n from django import forms\n+from django.apps import apps\n from django.conf import settings\n from django.contrib import messages\n from django.contrib.admin import helpers, widgets\n",

          {
            "body": " from django.views.generic import RedirectView\n \n IS_POPUP_VAR = \"_popup\"\n+SOURCE_MODEL_VAR = \"_source_model\"\n TO_FIELD_VAR = \"_to_field\"\n IS_FACETS_VAR = \"_facets\"\n \n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 7,
              "startLine": 72
            },
            "oldRange": {

              "startLine": 71
            },

          {
            "body": "                 \"save_on_top\": self.save_on_top,\n                 \"to_field_var\": TO_FIELD_VAR,\n                 \"is_popup_var\": IS_POPUP_VAR,\n+                \"source_model_var\": SOURCE_MODEL_VAR,\n                 \"app_label\": app_label,\n             }\n         )\n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 7,

            "section": "class ModelAdmin(BaseModelAdmin):"

          {
            "body": "             else:\n                 attr = obj._meta.pk.attname\n             value = obj.serializable_value(attr)\n-            popup_response_data = json.dumps(\n-                {\n-                    \"value\": str(value),\n-                    \"obj\": str(obj),\n-                }\n-            )\n+            popup_response = {\n+                \"value\": str(value),\n+                \"obj\": str(obj),\n+            }\n+\n+            # Find the optgroup for the new item, if available\n+            source_model_name = request.POST.get(SOURCE_MODEL_VAR)\n+\n+            if source_model_name:\n+                app_label, model_name = source_model_name.split(\".\", 1)\n+                try:\n+                    source_model = apps.get_model(app_label, model_name)\n+                except LookupError:\n+                    msg = _('The app \"%s\" could not be found.') % source_model_name\n+                    self.message_user(request, msg, messages.ERROR)\n+                else:\n+                    source_admin = self.admin_site._registry[source_model]\n+                    form = source_admin.get_form(request)()\n+                    if self.opts.verbose_name_plural in form.fields:\n+                        field = form.fields[self.opts.verbose_name_plural]\n+                        for option_value, option_label in field.choices:\n+                            # Check if this is an optgroup (label is a sequence\n+                            # of choices rather than a single string value).\n+                            if isinstance(option_label, (list, tuple)):\n+                                # It's an optgroup:\n+                                # (group_name, [(value, label), ...])\n+                                optgroup_label = option_value\n+                                for choice_value, choice_display in option_label:\n+                                    if choice_display == str(obj):\n+                                        popup_response[\"optgroup\"] = str(optgroup_label)\n+                                        break\n+\n+            popup_response_data = json.dumps(popup_response)\n             return TemplateResponse(\n                 request,\n                 self.popup_response_template\n",

          },
          {
            "body": "             \"object_id\": object_id,\n             \"original\": obj,\n             \"is_popup\": IS_POPUP_VAR in request.POST or IS_POPUP_VAR in request.GET,\n+            \"source_model\": request.GET.get(SOURCE_MODEL_VAR),\n             \"to_field\": to_field,\n             \"media\": media,\n             \"inline_admin_formsets\": inline_formsets,\n",

          },
          {
            "body": "             # me back\" button on the action confirmation page.\n             return HttpResponseRedirect(request.get_full_path())\n \n-        # If we're allowing changelist editing, we need to construct a formset\n-        # for the changelist given all the fields to be edited. Then we'll\n-        # use the formset to validate/process POSTed data.\n-        formset = cl.formset = None\n-\n         # Handle POSTed bulk-edit data.\n         if request.method == \"POST\" and cl.list_editable and \"_save\" in request.POST:\n             if not self.has_change_permission(request):\n                 raise PermissionDenied\n             FormSet = self.get_changelist_formset(request)\n             modified_objects = self._get_list_editable_queryset(\n                 request, FormSet.get_default_prefix()\n             )\n-            formset = cl.formset = FormSet(\n-                request.POST, request.FILES, queryset=modified_objects\n-            )\n-            if formset.is_valid():\n+            cl.formset = FormSet(request.POST, request.FILES, queryset=modified_objects)\n+            if cl.formset.is_valid():\n                 changecount = 0\n                 with transaction.atomic(using=router.db_for_write(self.model)):\n-                    for form in formset.forms:\n+                    for form in cl.formset.forms:\n                         if form.has_changed():\n                             obj = self.save_form(request, form, change=True)\n                             self.save_model(request, obj, form, change=True)\n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 19,
              "startLine": 2081
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 26,
              "startLine": 2050
            },
            "section": "class ModelAdmin(BaseModelAdmin):"
          },
          {
            "body": "         # Handle GET -- construct a formset for display.\n         elif cl.list_editable and self.has_change_permission(request):\n             FormSet = self.get_changelist_formset(request)\n-            formset = cl.formset = FormSet(queryset=cl.result_list)\n+            cl.formset = FormSet(queryset=cl.result_list)\n \n         # Build the list of media to be used by the formset.\n-        if formset:\n-            media = self.media + formset.media\n+        if cl.formset:\n+            media = self.media + cl.formset.media\n         else:\n             media = self.media\n \n",
            "newRange": {

        "path": "django/contrib/admin/options.py"
      },
      {
        "added": 40,
        "deleted": 11,
        "hunks": [
          {
            "body": " 'use strict';\n {\n+    const getOptionGroupName = (option) => option.parentElement.label;\n     const SelectBox = {\n         cache: {},\n         init: function(id) {\n             const box = document.getElementById(id);\n             SelectBox.cache[id] = [];\n             const cache = SelectBox.cache[id];\n             for (const node of box.options) {\n-                cache.push({value: node.value, text: node.text, displayed: 1});\n+                const group = getOptionGroupName(node);\n+                cache.push({group, value: node.value, text: node.text, displayed: 1});\n+            }\n+            // Only sort if there are any groups (to preserve existing behavior for non-grouped selects)\n+            if (cache.some(item => item.group)) {\n+                SelectBox.sort(id);\n             }\n         },\n         redisplay: function(id) {\n             // Repopulate HTML select box from cache\n             const box = document.getElementById(id);\n             const scroll_value_from_top = box.scrollTop;\n             box.innerHTML = '';\n-            for (const node of SelectBox.cache[id]) {\n-                if (node.displayed) {\n-                    const new_option = new Option(node.text, node.value, false, false);\n+            let node = box;\n+            let currentOptgroup = null;\n+            for (const option of SelectBox.cache[id]) {\n+                if (option.displayed) {\n+                    // Create a new optgroup when the group changes\n+                    if (option.group && option.group !== currentOptgroup) {\n+                        currentOptgroup = option.group;\n+                        node = document.createElement('optgroup');\n+                        node.setAttribute('label', option.group);\n+                        box.appendChild(node);\n+                    } else if (!option.group && currentOptgroup !== null) {\n+                        // Back to ungrouped options\n+                        currentOptgroup = null;\n+                        node = box;\n+                    }\n+                    const new_option = new Option(option.text, option.value, false, false);\n                     // Shows a tooltip when hovering over the option\n-                    new_option.title = node.text;\n-                    box.appendChild(new_option);\n+                    new_option.title = option.text;\n+                    node.appendChild(new_option);\n                 }\n             }\n             box.scrollTop = scroll_value_from_top;\n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 45,
              "startLine": 1
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 26,
              "startLine": 1
            }
          },
          {
            "body": "             cache.splice(delete_index, 1);\n         },\n         add_to_cache: function(id, option) {\n-            SelectBox.cache[id].push({value: option.value, text: option.text, displayed: 1});\n+            SelectBox.cache[id].push({group: option.group, value: option.value, text: option.text, displayed: 1});\n         },\n         cache_contains: function(id, value) {\n             // Check if an item is contained in the cache\n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 7,

            },
            "oldRange": {

          },
          {
            "body": "             for (const option of from_box.options) {\n                 const option_value = option.value;\n                 if (option.selected && SelectBox.cache_contains(from, option_value)) {\n-                    SelectBox.add_to_cache(to, {value: option_value, text: option.text, displayed: 1});\n+                    const group = getOptionGroupName(option);\n+                    SelectBox.add_to_cache(to, {group, value: option_value, text: option.text, displayed: 1});\n                     SelectBox.delete_from_cache(from, option_value);\n                 }\n             }\n+            // Only sort if there are any groups (to preserve existing behavior for non-grouped selects)\n+            if (SelectBox.cache[to].some(item => item.group)) {\n+                SelectBox.sort(to);\n+            }\n             SelectBox.redisplay(from);\n             SelectBox.redisplay(to);\n         },\n         move_all: function(from, to) {\n             const from_box = document.getElementById(from);\n             for (const option of from_box.options) {\n                 const option_value = option.value;\n                 if (SelectBox.cache_contains(from,             "body": "         Mark tests in Django's test suite which are expected failures on this\n         database and test which should be skipped on this database.\n         \"\"\"\n-        # Only load unittest if we're actually testing.\n-        from unittest import expectedFailure, skip\n-\n         for test_name in self.connection.features.django_test_expected_failures:\n-            test_case_name, _, test_method_name = test_name.rpartition(\".\")\n-            test_app = test_name.split(\".\")[0]\n-            # Importing a test app that isn't installed raises\n-                # RuntimeError.\n-                if test_app in settings.INSTALLED_APPS:\n-                    test_case = import_string(test_case_name)\n-                    test_method = getattr(test_case, test_method_name)\n-                    setattr(test_case, test_method_name, skip(reason)(test_method))\n+                self._mark_test(test_name, reason)\n+\n+    def _mark_test(self, test_name, skip_reason=None):\n+        # Only load unittest during testing.\n+        from unittest import expectedFailure, skip\n+\n+        module_or_class_name, _, name_to_mark = test_name.rpartition(\".\")\n+        test_app = test_name.split(\".\")[0]\n+        # Importing a test app that isn't installed raises RuntimeError.\n+        if test_app in settings.INSTALLED_APPS:\n+            try:\n+                test_frame = import_string(module_or_class_name)\n+            except ImportError:\n+                # import_string() can raise ImportError if a submodule's parent\n+                # module hasn't already been imported during test discovery.\n+                # This can happen in at least two cases:\n+                # 1. When running a subset of tests in a module, the test\n+                #    runner won't import tests in that module's other\n+                #    submodules.\n+                # 2. When the parallel test runner spawns workers with an empty\n+                #    import cache.\n+                test_to_mark = import_string(test_name)\n+                test_frame = sys.modules.get(test_to_mark.__module__)\n+            else:\n+                test_to_mark = getattr(test_frame, name_to_mark)\n+            if skip_reason:\n+                setattr(test_frame, name_to_mark, skip(skip_reason)(test_to_mark))\n+            else:\n+                setattr(test_frame, name_to_mark, expectedFailure(test_to_mark))\n... parent=&quot;Ljd-IV1YiB1lfMfz34ik-48&quot; source=&quot;5&quot; target=&quot;17&quot; edge=&quot;1&quot;&gt;&#xA;          &lt;mxGeometry relative=&quot;1&quot; as=&quot;geometry&quot;&gt;&#xA;            &lt;Array as=&quot;points&quot;&gt;&#xA;              &lt;mxPoint x=&quot;51.21951219512195&quot; y=&quot;256.09999999999997&quot; /&gt;&#xA;              &lt;mxPoint x=&quot;597.560975609756&quot; y=&quot;256.09999999999997&quot; /&gt;&#xA;            &lt;/Array&gt;&#xA;          &lt;/mxGeometry&gt;&#xA;        &lt;/mxCell&gt;&#xA;        &lt;mxCell id=&quot;5&quot; value=&quot;Needs Triage&quot; style=&quot;rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#e1d5e7;strokeColor=#9673a6;strokeWidth=3\n... [diff truncated]",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 4,
              "startLine": 1
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 4,
              "startLine": 1
            }
          }
        ],
        "path": "docs/internals/_images/contribution_process.svg"
      },
      {
        "added": 3,
        "deleted": 0,
        "hunks": [
          {
            "body": " * Don't reopen issues that have been marked \"wontfix\" without finding consensus\n   to do so on the `Django Forum`_.\n \n+* Don't reopen issues that have been marked \"needsnewfeatureprocess\" without\n+  shepherding an issue through the `new feature ideas`_ GitHub project.\n+\n * Don't use the ticket tracker for lengthy discussions, because they're\n   likely to get lost. If a particular ticket is controversial, please move the\n   discussion to the `Django Forum`_.\n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 9,
              "startLine": 27
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 6,
              "startLine": 27
            },
            "section": "Before reporting a bug on the `ticket tracker"
          }
        ],
        "path": "docs/internals/contributing/bugs-and-features.txt"
      },
      {
        "added": 11,
        "deleted": 4,
        "hunks": [
          {
            "body": "       close a ticket. Always use the forum to get a consensus before reopening\n       tickets closed as \"wontfix\".\n \n+* needsnewfeatureprocess\n+      Used when the ticket merits a new feature, which will need to get\n+      community input and support.\n+      See the :ref:`process for suggesting new features <requesting-features>`.\n+\n * duplicate\n       Used when another ticket covers the same issue. By closing duplicate\n       tickets, we keep all the discussion in one place, which helps\n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 11,
              "startLine": 327
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 6,
              "startLine": 327
            },
            "section": "A ticket can be resolved in a number of ways:"
          },
          {
            "body": " If you believe that the ticket was closed in error -- because you're\n still having the issue, or it's popped up somewhere else, or the triagers have\n made a mistake -- please reopen the ticket and provide further information.\n-Again, please do not reopen tickets that have been marked as \"wontfix\" and\n-bring the issue to the `Django Forum`_ instead.\n+Again, please do not reopen tickets that have been marked as \"wontfix\" or\n+\"needsnewfeatureprocess\". For \"wontfix\" tickets, bring the issue to the\n+`Django Forum`_ instead. For \"needsnewfeatureprocess\" tickets, propose the\n+feature through the :ref:`new features process <requesting-features>`.\n \n .. _how-can-i-help-with-triaging:\n \n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 10,
              "startLine": 349
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 8,
              "startLine": 344
            },
            "section": "A ticket can be resolved in a number of ways:"
          },
          {
            "body": " \n Then, you can help out by:\n \n-* Closing \"Unreviewed\" tickets as \"invalid\", \"worksforme\", or \"duplicate\", or\n-  \"wontfix\".\n+* Closing \"Unreviewed\" tickets as \"invalid\", \"worksforme\", \"duplicate\",\n+  \"wontfix\", or \"needsnewfeatureprocess\".\n \n * Closing \"Unreviewed\" tickets as \"needsinfo\" when the description is too\n   sparse to be actionable.\n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 8,
              "startLine": 368
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 8,
              "startLine": 361
            },
            "section": "reset page`_."
          }
        ],
        "path": "docs/internals/contributing/triaging-tickets.txt"
      },
      {
        "added": 1,
        "deleted": 1,
        "hunks": [
          {
            "body": "     docs\n     isort>=7.0.0\n     lint-docs\n-    zizmor>=1.16.3\n+    zizmor>=1.19.0\n \n Testing other Python versions and database backends\n ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~\n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 7,
              "startLine": 85
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 7,
              "startLine": 85
            },
            "section": "default environments can be seen as follows:"
          }
        ],
        "path": "docs/internals/contributing/writing-code/unit-tests.txt"
      },
      {
        "added": 2,
        "deleted": 2,
        "hunks": [
          {
            "body": " \n Django's documentation uses a custom ``console`` directive for documenting\n command-line examples involving ``django-admin``, ``manage.py``, ``python``,\n-etc.). In the HTML documentation, it renders a two-tab UI, with one tab showing\n+etc. In the HTML documentation, it renders a two-tab UI, with one tab showing\n a Unix-style command prompt and a second tab showing a Windows prompt.\n \n For example, you can replace this fragment:\n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 7,
              "startLine": 520
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 7,
              "startLine": 520
            },
            "section": "define some extra description units:"
          },
          {
            "body": " \n General improvements or other changes to the APIs that should be emphasized\n should use the \"``.. versionchanged:: X.Y``\" directive (with the same format\n-as the ``versionadded`` mentioned above.\n+as the ``versionadded`` mentioned above).\n \n These ``versionadded`` and ``versionchanged`` blocks should be\n \"self-contained.\" In other words, since we only keep these annotations around\n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 7,
              "startLine": 584
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 7,
              "startLine": 584
            },
            "section": "blank line and an optional description (indented)."
          }
        ],
        "path": "docs/internals/contributing/writing-documentation.txt"
      },
      {
        "added": 2,
        "deleted": 4,
        "hunks": [
          {
            "body": "         >>> wgs84 = SpatialReference(\"EPSG:4326\")  # EPSG string\n         >>> proj = \"+proj=longlat +ellps=WGS84 +datum=WGS84 +no_defs \"\n         >>> wgs84 = SpatialReference(proj)  # PROJ string\n-        >>> wgs84 = SpatialReference(\n-        ...     \"\"\"GEOGCS[\"WGS 84\",\n+        >>> wgs84 = SpatialReference(\"\"\"GEOGCS[\"WGS 84\",\n         ... DATUM[\"WGS_1984\",\n         ...      SPHEROID[\"WGS 84\",6378137,298.257223563,\n         ...          AUTHORITY[\"EPSG\",\"7030\"]],\n         ...      AUTHORITY[\"EPSG\",\"6326\"]],\n         ... PRIMEM[\"Greenwich\",0,\n         ...      AUTHORITY[\"EPSG\",\"8901\"]],\n         ... UNIT[\"degree\",0.01745329251994328,\n         ...      AUTHORITY[\"EPSG\",\"9122\"]],\n-        ... AUTHORITY[\"EPSG\",\"4326\"]]\"\"\"\n-        ... )  # OGC WKT\n+        ... AUTHORITY[\"EPSG\",\"4326\"]]\"\"\")  # OGC WKT\n \n     .. method:: __getitem__(target)\n \n",
            "newRange": {

            "oldRange": {

            },

  "description": "Filter for template-related files in the diff"
}

  "noOutputExpected": false,

  "stdout": "django/contrib/admin/templates/admin/change_form.html\n <div>\n {% if is_popup %}<input type=\"hidden\" name=\"{{ is_popup_var }}\" value=\"1\">{% endif %}\n {% if to_field %}<input type=\"hidden\" name=\"{{ to_field_var }}\" value=\"{{ to_field }}\">{% endif %}\n+{% if source_model %}<input type=\"hidden\" name=\"{{ source_model_var }}\" value=\"{{ source_model }}\">{% endif %}\n {% if save_on_top %}{% block submit_buttons_top %}{% submit_row %}{% endblock %}{% endif %}\n {% if errors %}\n     <p class=\"errornote\">\n\n\ndjango/template/__init__.py\n # Import the .autoreload module to trigger the registrations of signals.\n from . import autoreload  # NOQA isort:skip\n \n-\n __all__ += (\"Template\", \"Context\", \"RequestContext\")\n\n\ndocs/howto/custom-template-tags.txt\n     from django import template\n     from django.utils.html import format_html\n \n-\n     register = template.Library()"
}

            "body": "     \"\"\"\n \n     def __init__(self):\n-        super().__init__(self._generate)\n-\n-    def _generate(self):\n-        return secrets.token_urlsafe(16)\n+        super().__init__(generate_nonce)\n \n     def __bool__(self):\n         return self._wrapped is not empty\n \n \n+def generate_nonce():\n+    return secrets.token_urlsafe(16)\n+\n+\n def build_policy(config, nonce=None):\n     policy = []\n \n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 16,
              "startLine": 71
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 15,
              "startLine": 71
            },
            "section": "class LazyNonce(SimpleLazyObject):"
          }
        ],
        "path": "django/utils/csp.py"
      },
      {
        "added": 5,
        "deleted": 1,
        "hunks": [
          {
            "body": "     # without evaluating the wrapped object.\n     def __repr__(self):\n         if self._wrapped is empty:\n-            repr_attr = self._setupfunc\n+            # Avoid recursion if setupfunc is a bound method of this instance.\n+            if getattr(self._setupfunc, \"__self__\", None) is self:\n+                repr_attr = f\"<bound method {self._setupfunc.__name__}>\"\n+            else:\n+                repr_attr = self._setupfunc\n         else:\n             repr_attr = self._wrapped\n         return \"<%s: %r>\" % (type(self).__name__, repr_attr)\n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 11,
              "startLine": 407
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 7,
              "startLine": 407
            },
            "section": "class SimpleLazyObject(LazyObject):"
          }
        ],

            "body": "   <head>\n     <meta http-equiv=\"Content-type\" content=\"text/html; charset=utf-8\">\n     <meta http-equiv=\"Content-Language\" content=\"en-us\">\n+    <meta name=\"color-scheme\" content=\"light dark\" />\n     <meta name=\"robots\" content=\"NONE,NOARCHIVE\">\n     <title>{% blocktranslate %}Index of {{ directory }}{% endblocktranslate %}</title>\n   </head>\n",

        "path": "django/views/templates/directory_index.html"
      },
      {
        "added": 2,
        "deleted": 1,
        "hunks": [
          {
            "body": " <head>\n   <meta http-equiv=\"content-type\" content=\"text/html; charset=utf-8\">\n   <title>Page not found at {{ request.path_info }}</title>\n+  <meta name=\"color-scheme\" content=\"light\" />\n   <meta name=\"robots\" content=\"NONE,NOARCHIVE\">\n   <style>\n     html * { padding:0; margin:0; }\n     body * { padding:10px 20px; }\n     body * * { padding:0; }\n     body { font-family: sans-serif; background:#eee; color:#000; }\n-    body > :where(header, main, footer) { border-bottom:1px solid #ddd; }\n+    body > header, body > main, body > footer { border-bottom:1px solid #ddd; }\n     h1 { font-weight:normal; margin-bottom:.4em; }\n     h1 small { font-size:60%; color:#666; font-weight:normal; }\n     table { border:none; border-collapse: collapse; width:100%; }\n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 14,
              "startLine": 3
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 13,
              "startLine": 3
            }
          }
        ],
        "path": "django/views/templates/technical_404.html"
      },
      {
        "added": 2,
        "deleted": 1,
        "hunks": [
          {
            "body": " <html lang=\"en\">\n <head>\n   <meta http-equiv=\"content-type\" content=\"text/html; charset=utf-8\">\n+  <meta name=\"color-scheme\" content=\"light\" />\n   <meta name=\"robots\" content=\"NONE,NOARCHIVE\">\n   <title>{% if exception_type %}{{ exception_type }}{% else %}Report{% endif %}\n          {% if request %} at {{ request.path_info }}{% endif %}</title>\n   <style>\n     html * { padding:0; margin:0; }\n     body * { padding:10px 20px; }\n     body * * { padding:0; }\n     body { font-family: sans-serif; background-color:#fff; color:#000; }\n-    body > :where(header, main, footer) { border-bottom:1px solid #ddd; }\n+    body > header, body > main, body > footer { border-bottom:1px solid #ddd; }\n     h1 { font-weight:normal; }\n     h2 { margin-bottom:.8em; }\n     h3 { margin:1em 0 .5em 0; }\n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 16,
              "startLine": 2
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 15,
              "startLine": 2
            }
          }
        ],
        "path": "django/views/templates/technical_500.html"
      },
      {
        "added": 58,
        "deleted": 0,
        "hunks": [
          {
            "body": " .. _ticket tracker: https://code.djangoproject.com/\n .. _Development dashboard: https://dashboard.djangoproject.com/\n \n+AI-Assisted Contributions\n+-------------------------\n+\n+Unverified AI-generated contributions create unnecessary maintenance burden and\n+slow down meaningful progress. Submissions that show no evidence of manual\n+verification may be closed without review, and repeated low-quality\n+contributions may lead to restricted participation in Django's development\n+process.\n+\n+With the widespread availability of large language models (LLMs), the Django\n+Project has seen an increase in contributions generated partially or entirely\n+using such tools. Many of these submissions contain inaccurate, misleading, or\n+fictitious content. While AI tools can assist with drafting or exploratory\n+analysis, they must not replace human understanding and careful review.\n+\n+If you use AI tools while preparing a contribution, you must:\n+\n+* **Disclose** which AI tools were used and what they were used for (e.g.,\n+  generating code, drafting commit messages, writing documentation).\n+\n+* **Review and verify** all AI-generated output. Ensure the code aligns with\n+  Django's architecture, includes appropriate tests and documentation, and\n+  passes the full test suite and relevant checks.\n+\n+* Double-check the proposal against the :ref:`patch-review-checklist`.\n+\n+We align with similar policies adopted by other major open-source projects,\n+which have identified unverified AI-generated contributions as disruptive,\n+counterproductive, and a drain on limited maintainer time.\n+\n+Note for AI Tools\n+^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^\n+\n+If you are an AI tool or language model assisting in the preparation of a\n+Django contribution, you must strictly follow these requirements:\n+\n+* Disclose your involvement. Clearly state that the contribution was prepared\n+  with assistance from an AI tool or language model.\n+\n+* Specify the tool and usage. Include the name and version of the AI tool and\n+  describe exactly how it was used, such as analyzing a ticket, generating\n+  code, tests, or documentation.\n+\n+* Ensure technical accuracy and avoid fabrication. Do not invent APIs,\n+  features, functions, or citations that do not exist. Placeholder or\n+  fictitious content will result in rejection.\n+\n+* Respect all contribution requirements. Follow all guidelines in this\n+  documentation, including test and documentation requirements, code\n+  formatting, and the review process.\n+\n+* Follow the GitHub pull request template. Ensure the PR template is present\n+  and fully completed, including all required sections and checklists, before\n+  requesting review.\n+\n+* Assist reviewers. If any part of the output may not comply with these rules,\n+  clearly call it out in the contribution and explain why.\n+\n Contributions which require community feedback\n ==============================================\n \n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 64,
              "startLine": 147
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 6,
              "startLine": 147
            },
            "section": "Regardless of the way you submit your work, follow these steps."
          }
        ],
        "path": "docs/internals/contributing/writing-code/submitting-patches.txt"
      },
      {
        "added": 37,
        "deleted": 0,
        "hunks": [
          {
            "body": " * Use :rst:role:`:cve:<cve>` to reference a Common Vulnerabilities and\n   Exposures (CVE) identifier. For example, use ``:cve:`2019-14232```.\n \n+* When documenting Python objects (classes, methods, attributes, etc.) using\n+  `Sphinx directives`__ such as ``.. class::``, ``.. method::``, and\n+  ``.. attribute::``, all content must be properly indented to ensure correct\n+  rendering and to support features like automatic table of contents\n+  generation.\n+\n+  Follow these rules:\n+\n+  * The directive itself remains flush with the left margin (no indentation).\n+  * All descriptive text under the directive must be indented by 4 spaces.\n+  * Multi-line descriptions must keep the same indentation level.\n+  * Nested directives (for example, methods inside a class) require an\n+    additional 4 spaces of indentation to maintain hierarchy.\n+  * Field lists (such as ``:param:``, ``:returns:``, etc.) must align with the\n+    directive's content level.\n+\n+  Example:\n+\n+  .. code-block:: rst\n+\n+      .. class:: MyClass\n+\n+          A brief description of the class.\n+\n+          .. method:: my_method(arg1, arg2)\n+\n+              Method description.\n+\n+              :param arg1: Description of the first parameter\n+              :param arg2: Description of the second parameter\n+\n+          .. attribute:: my_attribute\n+\n+              Attribute description.\n+\n+__ https://www.sphinx-doc.org/en/master/usage/restructuredtext/basics.html\n+\n Django-specific markup\n ======================\n \n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 43,
              "startLine": 420
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 6,
              "startLine": 420
            },
            "section": "documentation:"
          }
        ],
        "path": "docs/internals/contributing/writing-documentation.txt"
      },
      {
        "added": 5,
        "deleted": 0,
        "hunks": [
          {
            "body": " * Calling :meth:`.QuerySet.values_list` with ``flat=True`` and no field name\n   will raise ``TypeError``.\n \n+* The use of ``None`` when querying a :class:`~django.db.models.JSONField`\n+  top-level value will compile to SQL ``IS NULL``. Use\n+  :class:`~django.db.models.JSONNull` to query for a JSON ``null`` value\n+  instead.\n+\n .. _deprecation-removed-in-6.1:\n \n 6.1\n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 11,
              "startLine": 59
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 6,
              "startLine": 59
            },
            "section": "details on these changes."
          }
        ],
        "path": "docs/internals/deprecation.txt"
      },
      {
        "added": 5,
        "deleted": 2,
        "hunks": [
          {
            "body": "    to ``django_polls``.\n \n #. Edit ``django_polls/apps.py`` so that :attr:`~.AppConfig.name` refers to the\n-   new module name and add :attr:`~.AppConfig.label` to give a short name for\n-   the app:\n+   new module name, add :attr:`~.AppConfig.label` to give a short name for\n+   the app, and set :attr:`~.AppConfig.default_auto_field` to ensure your\n+   migrations are not affected by changes to :setting:`DEFAULT_AUTO_FIELD`\n+   made by users of your reusable app:\n \n    .. code-block:: python\n        :caption: ``django-polls/django_polls/apps.py``\n \n        from django.apps import AppConfig\n \n \n        class PollsConfig(AppConfig):\n+           default_auto_field = \"django.db.models.BigAutoField\"\n            name = \"django_polls\"\n            label = \"polls\"\n \n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 19,
              "startLine": 143
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 16,
              "startLine": 143
            },
            "section": "this. For a small app like polls, this process isn't too difficult."
          }
        ],
        "path": "docs/intro/reusable-apps.txt"
      },
      {
        "added": 1,
        "deleted": 2,
        "hunks": [
          {
            "body": "     Operations to perform:\n       Apply all migrations: admin, auth, contenttypes, polls, sessions\n     Running migrations:\n-      Rendering model states... DONE\n       Applying polls.0001_initial... OK\n \n The :djadmin:`migrate` command takes all the migrations that haven't been\n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 6,
              "startLine": 308
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 7,
              "startLine": 308
            },
            "section": "database:"
          },
          {
            "body": " \n * Change your models (in ``models.py``).\n * Run :djadmin:`python manage.py makemigrations <makemigrations>` to create\n-  migrations for those changes\n+  migrations for those changes.\n * Run :djadmin:`python manage.py migrate <migrate>` to apply those changes to\n   the database.\n \n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 7,
              "startLine": 324
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 7,
              "startLine": 325
            },
            "section": "but for now, remember the three-step guide to making model changes:"
          }
        ],
        "path": "docs/intro/tutorial02.txt"
      },
      {
        "added": 11,
        "deleted": 3,
        "hunks": [
          {
            "body": " \n .. attribute:: AppConfig.default_auto_field\n \n-    The implicit primary key type to add to models within this app. You can\n-    use this to keep :class:`~django.db.models.AutoField` as the primary key\n-    type for third party applications.\n+    The implicit primary key type to add to models within this app.\n+\n+    Reusable applications that define models **must** set this attribute to\n+    ensure migrations included in the application match the models. Otherwise,\n+    migrations will be generated for the application when a user runs\n+    :djadmin:`makemigrations` with a different :setting:`DEFAULT_AUTO_FIELD`,\n+    which may conflict with future migrations shipped by the application.\n+\n+    For example, to keep using :class:`~django.db.models.AutoField`::\n+\n+        default_auto_field = \"django.db.models.AutoField\"\n \n     By default, this is the value of :setting:`DEFAULT_AUTO_FIELD`.\n \n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 17,
              "startLine": 227
            },
