45. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "python3.11 << 'EOF'\nimport sys\nsys.path.insert(0, '/home/claude/.local/lib/python3.11/site-packages')\n\nfrom django.template import Engine, Context\n\n# Test: Base has both outer and inner blocks\n# Middle overrides outer block (and inner is explicitly redefined inside the new outer)\n# Child overrides only inner\ntemplates = {\n    \"base.html\": \"{% block outer %}[outer-base]{% block inner %}[inner-base]{% endblock %}[/outer-base]{% endblock %}\",\n    \"layout.html\": \"{% extends 'base.html' %}{% block outer %}[outer-layout]{% block inner %}[inner-layout]{% endblock %}[/outer-layout]{% endblock %}\",\n    \"page.html\": \"{% extends 'layout.html' %}{% block inner %}[inner-page]{% endblock %}\",\n}\n\nengine = Engine(loaders=[('django.template.loaders.locmem.Loader', templates)])\ntemplate = engine.get_template(\"page.html\")\noutput = template.render(Context({}))\nprint(f\"Test 1 - Base has nested blocks, middle overrides outer, child overrides inner:\")\nprint(f\"  Output:   {output!r}\")\nprint(f\"  Expected: '[outer-layout][inner-page][/outer-layout]'\")\nprint(f\"  Pass: {output == '[outer-layout][inner-page][/outer-layout]'}\")\nprint()\n\n# Slightly different: 4 levels\ntemplates2 = {\n    \"base.html\": \"{% block outer %}[outer-base]{% block inner %}[inner-base]{% endblock %}[/outer-base]{% endblock %}\",\n    \"grandparent.html\": \"{% extends 'base.html' %}{% block outer %}[outer-gp]{% block inner %}[inner-gp]{% endblock %}[/outer-gp]{% endblock %}\",\n    \"layout.html\": \"{% extends 'grandparent.html' %}{% block outer %}[outer-layout]{% block inner %}[inner-layout]{% endblock %}[/outer-layout]{% endblock %}\",\n    \"page.html\": \"{% extends 'layout.html' %}{% block inner %}[inner-page]{% endblock %}\",\n}\n\nengine2 = Engine(loaders=[('django.template.loaders.locmem.Loader', templates2)])\ntemplate2 = engine2.get_template(\"page.html\")\noutput2 = template2.render(Context({}))\nprint(f\"Test 2 - 4 levels:\")\nprint(f\"  Output:   {output2!r}\")\nprint(f\"  Expected: '[outer-layout][inner-page][/outer-layout]'\")\nprint(f\"  Pass: {output2 == '[outer-layout][inner-page][/outer-layout]'}\")\nprint()\n\n# The inheritance16 case: base doesn't have inner, middle introduces it\ntemplates3 = {\n    \"inheritance01\": \"1{% block first %}&{% endblock %}3{% block second %}_{% endblock %}\",\n    \"inheritance15\": \"{% extends 'inheritance01' %}{% block first %}2{% block inner %}inner{% endblock %}{% endblock %}\",\n    \"inheritance16\": \"{% extends 'inheritance15' %}{% block inner %}out{% endblock %}\",\n}\n\nengine3 = Engine(loaders=[('django.template.loaders.locmem.Loader', templates3)])\ntemplate3 = engine3.get_template(\"inheritance16\")\noutput3 = template3.render(Context({}))\nprint(f\"Test 3 - inheritance16 (from existing tests):\")\nprint(f\"  Output:   {output3!r}\")\nprint(f\"  Expected: '12out3_'\")\nprint(f\"  Pass: {output3 == '12out3_'}\")\nEOF",
  "description": "Test various nested block inheritance scenarios"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "Test 1 - Base has nested blocks, middle overrides outer, child overrides inner:\n  Output:   '[outer-layout][inner-page][/outer-layout]'\n  Expected: '[outer-layout][inner-page][/outer-layout]'\n  Pass: True\n\nTest 2 - 4 levels:\n  Output:   '[outer-layout][inner-page][/outer-layout]'\n  Expected: '[outer-layout][inner-page][/outer-layout]'\n  Pass: True\n\nTest 3 - inheritance16 (from existing tests):\n  Output:   '12out3_'\n  Expected: '12out3_'\n  Pass: True"
}

Output
{
  "interrupted": false,

  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "class Node:\n    # Set this to True for nodes that must be first in the template (although\n    # they can be preceded by text nodes.\n    must_be_first = False\n    child_nodelists = (\"nodelist\",)\n    token = None\n\n    def render(self, context):\n        \"\"\"\n        Return the node rendered as a string.\n        \"\"\"\n        pass\n\n    def render_annotated(self, context):\n        \"\"\"\n        Render the node. If debug is True and an exception occurs during\n        rendering, the exception is annotated with contextual line information\n        where it occurred in the template. For internal usage this method is\n        preferred over using the render method directly.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            return self.render(context)\n        except Exception as e:\n            if context.template.engine.debug:\n                # Store the actual node that caused the exception.\n                if not hasattr(e, \"_culprit_node\"):\n                    e._culprit_node = self\n                if (\n                    not hasattr(e, \"template_debug\")\n                    and context.render_context.template.origin == e._culprit_node.origin\n                ):\n                    e.template_debug = (\n                        context.render_context.template.get_exception_info(\n                            e,\n                            e._culprit_node.token,\n                        )\n                    )\n            raise\n\n    def get_nodes_by_type(self, nodetype):\n        \"\"\"\n        Return a list of all nodes (within this node and its nodelist)\n        of the given type\n        \"\"\"\n        nodes = []\n        if isinstance(self, nodetype):\n            nodes.append(self)\n        for attr in self.child_nodelists:\n            nodelist = getattr(self, attr, None)\n            if nodelist:\n                nodes.extend(nodelist.get_nodes_by_type(nodetype))\n        return nodes"
}
50. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "# Write the key part of Sourcegraph's loader_tags.py to compare\ncat > /tmp/sg_render_method.py << 'PYEOF'\n    def render(self, context):\n        compiled_parent = self.get_parent(context)\n\n        if BLOCK_CONTEXT_KEY not in context.render_context:\n            context.render_context[BLOCK_CONTEXT_KEY] = BlockContext()\n        block_context = context.render_context[BLOCK_CONTEXT_KEY]\n\n        # Add the block nodes from this node to the block context\n        block_context.add_blocks(self.blocks)\n\n        # If this block's parent doesn't have an extends node it is the root,\n        # and its block nodes also need to be added to the block context.\n        for node in compiled_parent.nodelist:\n            # The ExtendsNode has to be the first non-text node.\n            if not isinstance(node, TextNode):\n                if not isinstance(node, ExtendsNode):\n                    blocks = {\n                        n.name: n\n                        for n in compiled_parent.nodelist.get_nodes_by_type(BlockNode)\n                    }\n                    block_context.add_blocks(blocks)\n                break\n\n        # Call Template._render explicitly so the parser context stays\n        # the same.\n        with context.render_context.push_state(compiled_parent, isolated_context=False):\n            return compiled_parent._render(context)\nPYEOF\n\n# Extract the same method from installed Django\npython3.11 -c \"\nimport ast\nwith open('/home/claude/.local/lib/python3.11/site-packages/django/template/loader_tags.py') as f:\n    source = f.read()\n# Find ExtendsNode.render\nlines = source.split('\\n')\nin_extends = False\nin_render = False\nfor i, line in enumerate(lines):\n    if 'class ExtendsNode' in line:\n        in_extends = True\n    if in_extends and '    def render(self, context):' in line:\n        in_render = True\n    if in_render:\n        print(line)\n        if i > 0 and line.startswith('    def ') and 'render' not in line:\n            break\n\" > /tmp/installed_render_method.py\n\ndiff /tmp/sg_render_method.py /tmp/installed_render_method.py",
  "description": "Compare ExtendsNode.render between Sourcegraph and installed Django"
}

  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: import os\\n2: from functools import wraps\\n3: \\n4: from django.template.engine import Engine\\n5: from django.test.utils import override_settings\\n6: from django.utils.safestring import mark_safe\\n7: \\n8: ROOT = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))\\n9: TEMPLATE_DIR = os.path.join(ROOT, \\\"templates\\\")\\n10: \\n11: \\n12: def setup(templates, *args, test_once=False, debug_only=False):\\n13:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n14:     Runs test method multiple times in the following order:\\n15: \\n16:     debug       cached      string_if_invalid\\n17:     -----       ------      -----------------\\n18:     False       False\\n19:     False       True\\n20:     False       False       INVALID\\n21:     False       True        INVALID\\n22:     True        False\\n23:     True        True\\n24: \\n25:     Use test_once=True to test deprecation warnings since the message won't be\\n26:     displayed multiple times.\\n27:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n28: \\n29:     for arg in args:\\n30:         templates.update(arg)\\n31: \\n32:     # numerous tests make use of an inclusion tag\\n33:     # add this in here for simplicity\\n34:     templates[\\\"inclusion.html\\\"] = \\\"{{ result }}\\\"\\n35: \\n36:     loaders = [\\n37:         (\\n38:             \\\"django.template.loaders.cached.Loader\\\",\\n39:             [\\n40:                 (\\\"django.template.loaders.locmem.Loader\\\", templates),\\n41:             ],\\n42:         ),\\n43:     ]\\n44: \\n45:     def decorator(func):\\n46:         # Make Engine.get_default() \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c@674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3/-/blob/tests/template_tests/utils.py\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: import os\n2: from functools import wraps\n3: \n4: from django.template.engine import Engine\n5: from django.test.utils import override_settings\n6: from django.utils.safestring import mark_safe\n7: \n8: ROOT = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))\n9: TEMPLATE_DIR = os.path.join(ROOT, \"templates\")\n10: \n11: \n12: def setup(templates, *args, test_once=False, debug_only=False):\n13:     \"\"\"\n14:     Runs test method multiple times in the following order:\n15: \n16:     debug       cached      string_if_invalid\n17:     -----       ------      -----------------\n18:     False       False\n19:     False       True\n20:     False       False       INVALID\n21:     False       True        INVALID\n22:     True        False\n23:     True        True\n24: \n25:     Use test_once=True to test deprecation warnings since the message won't be\n26:     displayed multiple times.\n27:     \"\"\"\n28: \n29:     for arg in args:\n30:         templates.update(arg)\n31: \n32:     # numerous tests make use of an inclusion tag\n33:     # add this in here for simplicity\n34:     templates[\"inclusion.html\"] = \"{{ result }}\"\n35: \n36:     loaders = [\n37:         (\n38:             \"django.template.loaders.cached.Loader\",\n39:             [\n40:                 (\"django.template.loaders.locmem.Loader\", templates),\n41:             ],\n42:         ),\n43:     ]\n44: \n45:     def decorator(func):\n46:         # Make Engine.get_default() raise an exception to ensure that tests\n47:         # are properly isolated from Django's global settings.\n48:         @override_settings(TEMPLATES=None)\n49:         @wraps(func)\n50:         def inner(self):\n51:             # Set up custom template tag libraries if specified\n52:             libraries = getattr(self, \"libraries\", {})\n53: \n54:             self.engine = Engine(\n55:                 libraries=libraries,\n56:                 loaders=loaders,\n57:                 debug=debug_only,\n58:             )\n59:             func(self)\n60:             if test_once:\n61:                 return\n62:             func(self)\n63:             if debug_only:\n64:                 return\n65: \n66:             self.engine = Engine(\n67:                 libraries=libraries,\n68:                 loaders=loaders,\n69:                 string_if_invalid=\"INVALID\",\n70:             )\n71:             func(self)\n72:             func(self)\n73: \n74:             self.engine = Engine(\n75:                 debug=True,\n76:                 libraries=libraries,\n77:                 loaders=loaders,\n78:             )\n79:             func(self)\n80:             func(self)\n81: \n82:         return inner\n83: \n84:     return decorator\n85: \n86: \n87: # Helper objects\n88: \n89: \n90: class SomeException(Exception):\n91:     silent_variable_failure = True\n92: \n93: \n94: class SomeOtherException(Exception):\n95:     pass\n96: \n97: \n98: class ShouldNotExecuteException(Exception):\n99:     pass\n100: \n101: \n102: class SomeClass:\n103:     def __init__(self):\n104:         self.otherclass = OtherClass()\n105: \n106:     def method(self):\n107:         return \"SomeClass.method\"\n108: \n109:     def method2(self, o):\n110:         return o\n111: \n112:     def method3(self):\n113:         raise SomeException\n114: \n115:     def method4(self):\n116:         raise SomeOtherException\n117: \n118:     def method5(self):\n119:         raise TypeError\n120: \n121:     def __getitem__(self, key):\n122:         if key == \"silent_fail_key\":\n123:             raise SomeException\n124:         elif key == \"noisy_fail_key\":\n125:             raise SomeOtherException\n126:         raise KeyError\n127: \n128:     @property\n129:     def silent_fail_attribute(self):\n130:         raise SomeException\n131: \n132:     @property\n133:     def noisy_fail_attribute(self):\n134:         raise SomeOtherException\n135: \n136:     @property\n137:     def attribute_error_attribute(self):\n138:         raise AttributeError\n139: \n140:     @property\n141:     def type_error_attribute(self):\n142:         raise TypeError\n143: \n144: \n145: class OtherClass:\n146:     def method(self):\n147:         return \"OtherClass.method\"\n148: \n149: \n150: class TestObj:\n151:     def is_true(self):\n152:         return True\n153: \n154:     def is_false(self):\n155:         return False\n156: \n157:     def is_bad(self):\n158:         raise ShouldNotExecuteException()\n159: \n160: \n161: class SilentGetItemClass:\n162:     def __getitem__(self, key):\n163:         raise SomeException\n164: \n165: \n166: class SilentAttrClass:\n167:     def b(self):\n168:         raise SomeException\n169: \n170:     b = property(b)\n171: \n172: \n173: class UTF8Class:\n174:     \"Class whose __str__ returns non-ASCII data\"\n175: \n176:     def __str__(self):\n177:         return \"\u0160\u0110\u0106\u017d\u0107\u017e\u0161\u0111\"\n178: \n179: \n180: # These two classes are used to test auto-escaping of string output.\n181: class UnsafeClass:\n182:     def __str__(self):\n183:         return \"you & me\"\n184: \n185: \n186: class SafeClass:\n187:     def __str__(self):\n188:         return mark_safe(\"you &gt; me\")\n189: ",

55. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "python3.11 << 'EOF'\nimport sys\nsys.path.insert(0, '/home/claude/.local/lib/python3.11/site-packages')\n\nfrom django.template import Engine, Context\n\ninheritance_templates = {\n    \"inheritance01\": (\n        \"1{% block first %}&{% endblock %}3{% block second %}_{% endblock %}\"\n    ),\n    \"inheritance15\": \"{% extends 'inheritance01' %}\"\n    \"{% block first %}2{% block inner %}inner{% endblock %}{% endblock %}\",\n    \"inheritance16\": \"{% extends 'inheritance15' %}{% block inner %}out{% endblock %}\",\n    \"inclusion.html\": \"{{ result }}\",\n}\n\n# Use cached loader (same as test setup)\nloaders = [\n    (\n        \"django.template.loaders.cached.Loader\",\n        [\n            (\"django.template.loaders.locmem.Loader\", inheritance_templates),\n        ],\n    ),\n]\n\nengine = Engine(loaders=loaders)\noutput = engine.render_to_string(\"inheritance16\")\nprint(f\"inheritance16: {output!r} (expected '12out3_', pass={output == '12out3_'})\")\n\n# Also test inheritance15\noutput15 = engine.render_to_string(\"inheritance15\")\nprint(f\"inheritance15: {output15!r} (expected '12inner3_', pass={output15 == '12inner3_'})\")\n\n# Now test the incident scenario (base has both outer and inner)\ntemplates2 = {\n    \"base.html\": \"{% block outer %}[outer-base]{% block inner %}[inner-base]{% endblock %}[/outer-base]{% endblock %}\",\n    \"layout.html\": \"{% extends 'base.html' %}{% block outer %}[outer-layout]{% block inner %}[inner-layout]{% endblock %}[/outer-layout]{% endblock %}\",\n    \"page.html\": \"{% extends 'layout.html' %}{% block inner %}[inner-page]{% endblock %}\",\n    \"inclusion.html\": \"{{ result }}\",\n}\n\nloaders2 = [\n    (\n        \"django.template.loaders.cached.Loader\",\n        [\n            (\"django.template.loaders.locmem.Loader\", templates2),\n        ],\n    ),\n]\n\nengine2 = Engine(loaders=loaders2)\noutput2 = engine2.render_to_string(\"page.html\")\nexpected2 = \"[outer-layout][inner-page][/outer-layout]\"\nprint(f\"\\nIncident scenario: {output2!r}\")\nprint(f\"Expected: {expected2!r}\")\nprint(f\"Pass: {output2 == expected2}\")\nEOF",
  "description": "Test with cached loader (same as test setup)"
}

{
  "command": "python3.11 << 'EOF'\nimport sys\nsys.path.insert(0, '/home/claude/.local/lib/python3.11/site-packages')\nfrom django.template import Engine, Context\n\n# Scenario: outer not overridden, inner block nested in outer\ntemplates = {\n    \"base.html\": \"{% block outer %}[outer-base]{% block inner %}[inner-base]{% endblock %}[/outer-base]{% endblock %}\",\n    \"layout.html\": \"{% extends 'base.html' %}{% block inner %}[inner-layout]{% endblock %}\",\n    \"page.html\": \"{% extends 'layout.html' %}{% block inner %}[inner-page]{% endblock %}\",\n    \"inclusion.html\": \"{{ result }}\",\n}\nloaders = [\n    (\"django.template.loaders.cached.Loader\",\n     [(\"django.template.loaders.locmem.Loader\", templates)])\n]\nengine = Engine(loaders=loaders)\noutput = engine.render_to_string(\"page.html\")\nexpected = \"[outer-base][inner-page][/outer-base]\"\nprint(f\"Scenario 1 - 3-level, outer not overridden:\")\nprint(f\"  Output:   {output!r}\")\nprint(f\"  Expected: {expected!r}\")\nprint(f\"  Pass: {output == expected}\")\nprint()\n\n# Now test the SAME but with the BUG scenario from the incident:\n# base -> layout -> page\n# base has outer+inner, layout overrides outer (with inner), page overrides inner\n# The specific case where layout_inner leaks through\n\n# Let me check what happens with 3 levels when base has both\ntemplates2 = {\n    \"base.html\": \"[base-start]{% block outer %}[outer-base]{% block inner %}[inner-base]{% endblock %}[/outer-base]{% endblock %}[base-end]\",\n    \"layout.html\": \"{% extends 'base.html' %}{% block outer %}[outer-layout]{% block inner %}[inner-layout]{% endblock %}[/outer-layout]{% endblock %}\",\n    \"page.html\": \"{% extends 'layout.html' %}{% block inner %}[inner-page]{% endblock %}\",\n    \"inclusion.html\": \"{{ result }}\",\n}\nloaders2 = [\n    (\"django.template.loaders.cached.Loader\",\n     [(\"django.template.loaders.locmem.Loader\", templates2)])\n]\nengine2 = Engine(loaders=loaders2)\noutput2 = engine2.render_to_string(\"page.html\")\nexpected2 = \"[base-start][outer-layout][inner-page][/outer-layout][base-end]\"\nprint(f\"Scenario 2 - base+layout+page, all with nested blocks:\")\nprint(f\"  Output:   {output2!r}\")\nprint(f\"  Expected: {expected2!r}\")\nprint(f\"  Pass: {output2 == expected2}\")\nEOF",
  "description": "Test various nested block scenarios"
}

57. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "python3.11 << 'EOF'\nimport sys\nsys.path.insert(0, '/home/claude/.local/lib/python3.11/site-packages')\n\n# Test the scenario: what if ExtendsNode.render() adds self.blocks AFTER root blocks?\n# (reversed order)\nfrom django.template.loader_tags import BlockContext, BLOCK_CONTEXT_KEY, BlockNode, ExtendsNode\nfrom django.template.base import TextNode\nfrom django.template import Engine, Context\n\n# Let me manually trace what would happen if the block addition order was wrong\n# Scenario: 3-level with middle overriding outer (containing inner), child overriding inner\n\nclass BuggyBlockContext(BlockContext):\n    \"\"\"Version where add_blocks appends instead of inserts\"\"\"\n    def add_blocks(self, blocks):\n        for name, block in blocks.items():\n            self.blocks[name].append(block)  # BUG: append instead of insert(0)\n\n# Test with BuggyBlockContext\ntemplates = {\n    \"base.html\": \"{% block outer %}[outer-base]{% block inner %}[inner-base]{% endblock %}[/outer-base]{% endblock %}\",\n    \"layout.html\": \"{% extends 'base.html' %}{% block outer %}[outer-layout]{% block inner %}[inner-layout]{% endblock %}[/outer-layout]{% endblock %}\",\n    \"page.html\": \"{% extends 'layout.html' %}{% block inner %}[inner-page]{% endblock %}\",\n    \"inclusion.html\": \"{{ result }}\",\n}\n\n# First let me verify correct code works\nfrom django.template.loaders.locmem import Loader\nengine = Engine(loaders=[('django.template.loaders.locmem.Loader', templates)])\noutput = engine.get_template(\"page.html\").render(Context({}))\nprint(f\"Correct code: {output!r} (expected '[outer-layout][inner-page][/outer-layout]')\")\nprint(f\"Pass: {output == '[outer-layout][inner-page][/outer-layout]'}\")\nprint()\n\n# Now manually simulate the buggy behavior (wrong order)\n# In the buggy version: \n# 1. Root blocks added first (base)\n# 2. Then self.blocks added (layout)\n# 3. Then child blocks added (page)\n# This would mean: blocks['inner'] = [base_inner, layout_inner, page_inner] with append\n# pop() \u2192 page_inner... still correct with append!\n\n# What if the bug is different: self.blocks is NOT added BEFORE root blocks?\n# And self.blocks uses append (not insert(0)):\n# 1. child adds: [child]\n# 2. Root base adds: [child, base] \n# 3. layout adds: [child, base, layout]\n# pop() \u2192 layout (WRONG! middle leaks through)\n\nprint(\"Simulating buggy order: root added before self.blocks, using append:\")\n# Simulate: child first (insert), then root (insert), then self (append)\nbc = BlockContext()\nbc.add_blocks({'inner': 'child_inner'})  # child\nprint(f\"After child: {list(bc.blocks['inner'])}\")\n\nbc2 = BlockContext()\nbc2.blocks['inner'].append('child_inner')  # child (append)\nbc2.blocks['inner'].append('base_inner')   # root (append)\nbc2.blocks['inner'].append('layout_inner') # middle self (append)\nprint(f\"Bug scenario (append all): {list(bc2.blocks['inner'])}\")\nprint(f\"  pop() \u2192 {bc2.blocks['inner'][-1]} (should be child_inner)\")\nEOF",
  "description": "Test buggy block context scenarios"
}

}

  "command": "mkdir -p /workspace/tests/template_tests/syntax_tests /workspace/django/template\npython3.11 << 'EOF'\n# Create a minimal test runner for just the extends tests\n\n# First, let's run it using the installed Django + our test setup\nimport sys\nsys.path.insert(0, '/home/claude/.local/lib/python3.11/site-packages')\nsys.path.insert(0, '/workspace')\n\n# Configure Django\nimport django.conf\ndjango.conf.settings.configure(\n    TEMPLATES=[{\n        'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',\n        'DIRS': [],\n    }],\n    INSTALLED_APPS=['django.template'],\n)\ndjango.setup()\n\nfrom django.template import Engine, Context\n\n# Test case that most closely matches the bug description\ninheritance_templates = {\n    \"inheritance01\": (\n        \"1{% block first %}&{% endblock %}3{% block second %}_{% endblock %}\"\n    ),\n    \"inheritance15\": \"{% extends 'inheritance01' %}\"\n    \"{% block first %}2{% block inner %}inner{% endblock %}{% endblock %}\",\n    \"inheritance16\": \"{% extends 'inheritance15' %}{% block inner %}out{% endblock %}\",\n    \"inclusion.html\": \"{{ result }}\",\n}\n\nloaders = [\n    (\n        \"django.template.loaders.cached.Loader\",\n        [(\"django.template.loaders.locmem.Loader\", inheritance_templates)]\n    ),\n]\n\nengine = Engine(loaders=loaders)\noutput = engine.render_to_string(\"inheritance16\")\nprint(f\"inheritance16: {output!r} (expected '12out3_', {'PASS' if output == '12out3_' else 'FAIL'})\")\nEOF",
  "description": "Set up Django and run template tests"
}

60. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "mkdir -p /tmp/sg_django/template\n\n# Write the critical section of the Sourcegraph loader_tags.py\npython3.11 -c \"\ncontent = '''import posixpath\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\nfrom django.utils.safestring import mark_safe\n\nfrom .base import Node, Template, TemplateSyntaxError, TextNode, Variable, token_kwargs\nfrom .library import Library\n\nregister = Library()\n\nBLOCK_CONTEXT_KEY = \\\"block_context\\\"\n\n\nclass BlockContext:\n    def __init__(self):\n        # Dictionary of FIFO queues.\n        self.blocks = defaultdict(list)\n\n    def __repr__(self):\n        return f\\\"<{self.__class__.__qualname__}: blocks={self.blocks!r}>\\\"\n\n    def add_blocks(self, blocks):\n        for name, block in blocks.items():\n            self.blocks[name].insert(0, block)\n\n    def pop(self, name):\n        try:\n            return self.blocks[name].pop()\n        except IndexError:\n            return None\n\n    def push(self, name, block):\n        self.blocks[name].append(block)\n\n    def get_block(self, name):\n        try:\n            return self.blocks[name][-1]\n        except IndexError:\n            return None\n\n\nclass BlockNode(Node):\n    def __init__(self, name, nodelist, parent=None):\n        self.name = name\n        self.nodelist = nodelist\n        self.parent = parent\n\n    def __repr__(self):\n        return \\\"<Block Node: %s. Contents: %r>\\\" % (self.name, self.nodelist)\n\n    def render(self, context):\n        block_context = context.render_context.get(BLOCK_CONTEXT_KEY)\n        with context.push():\n            if block_context is None:\n                context[\\\"block\\\"] = self\n                result = self.nodelist.render(context)\n            else:\n                push = block = block_context.pop(self.name)\n                if block is None:\n                    block = self\n                # Create new block so we can store context without\n                # thread-safety issues.\n                block = type(self)(block.name, block.nodelist)\n                block.context = context\n                context[\\\"block\\\"] = block\n                result = block.nodelist.render(context)\n                if push is not None:\n                    block_context.push(self.name, push)\n        return result\n\n    def super(self):\n        if not hasattr(self, \\\"context\\\"):\n            raise TemplateSyntaxError(\n                \\\"'%s' object has no attribute 'context'. Did you use \\\"\n                \\\"{{ block.super }} in a base template?\\\" % self.__class__.__name__\n            )\n        render_context = self.context.render_context\n        if (\n            BLOCK_CONTEXT_KEY in render_context\n            and render_context[BLOCK_CONTEXT_KEY].get_block(self.name) is not None\n        ):\n            return mark_safe(self.render(self.context))\n        return \\\"\\\"\n\n\nclass ExtendsNode(Node):\n    must_be_first = True\n    context_key = \\\"extends_context\\\"\n\n    def __init__(self, nodelist, parent_name, template_dirs=None):\n        self.nodelist = nodelist\n        self.parent_name = parent_name\n        self.template_dirs = template_dirs\n        self.blocks = {n.name: n for n in nodelist.get_nodes_by_type(BlockNode)}\n\n    def render(self, context):\n        compiled_parent = self.get_parent(context)\n\n        if BLOCK_CONTEXT_KEY not in context.render_context:\n            context.render_context[BLOCK_CONTEXT_KEY] = BlockContext()\n        block_context = context.render_context[BLOCK_CONTEXT_KEY]\n\n        # Add the block nodes from this node to the block context\n        block_context.add_blocks(self.blocks)\n\n        # If this block\\\"s parent doesn\\\"t have an extends node it is the root,\n        # and its block nodes also need to be added to the block context.\n        for node in compiled_parent.nodelist:\n            # The ExtendsNode has to be the first non-text node.\n            if not isinstance(node, TextNode):\n                if not isinstance(node, ExtendsNode):\n                    blocks = {\n                        n.name: n\n                        for n in compiled_parent.nodelist.get_nodes_by_type(BlockNode)\n                    }\n                    block_context.add_blocks(blocks)\n                break\n\n        # Call Template._render explicitly so the parser context stays\n        # the same.\n        with context.render_context.push_state(compiled_parent, isolated_context=False):\n            return compiled_parent._render(context)\n'''\nwith open('/tmp/sg_loader_tags_key.py', 'w') as f:\n    f.write(content)\nprint('written')\n\"\n\n# Now extract the same sections from the installed version\npython3.11 -c \"\nwith open('/home/claude/.local/lib/python3.11/site-packages/django/template/loader_tags.py') as f:\n    content = f.read()\nwith open('/tmp/installed_loader_tags_key.py', 'w') as f:\n    f.write(content)\nprint('written')\n\"\n\ndiff /tmp/sg_loader_tags_key.py /tmp/installed_loader_tags_key.py",
  "description": "Compare Sourcegraph and installed loader_tags"
}

A history is kept in\n>         the render_context attribute between successive extends calls and\n>         passed as the skip argument. This enables extends to work recursively\n>         without extending the same template twice.\n>         \"\"\"\n>         history = context.render_context.setdefault(\n>             self.context_key,\n>             [self.origin],\n>         )\n>         template, origin = context.template.engine.find_template(\n>             template_name,\n>             skip=history,\n>         )\n>         history.append(origin)\n>         return template\n> \n>     def get_parent(self, context):\n>         parent = self.parent_name.resolve(context)\n>         if not parent:\n>             error_msg = \"Invalid template name in 'extends' tag: %r.\" % parent\n>             if self.parent_name.filters or isinstance(self.parent_name.var, Variable):\n>                 error_msg += (\n>                     \" Got this from the '%s' variable.\" % self.parent_name.token\n>                 )\n>             raise TemplateSyntaxError(error_msg)\n>         if isinstance(parent, Template):\n>             # parent is a django.template.Template\n>             return parent\n>         if isinstance(getattr(parent, \"template\", None), Template):\n>             # parent is a django.template.backends.django.Template\n>             return parent.template\n>         return self.find_template(parent, context)\n> \n106c143\n<         # Cache the template object\n>         in render_context to avoid reparsing and loading when used in a for\n>         loop.\n>         \"\"\"\n>         template = self.template.resolve(context)\n>         # Does this quack like a Template?\n>         if not callable(getattr(template, \"render\", None)):\n>             # If not, try the cache and select_template().\n>             template_name = template or ()\n>             if isinstance(template_name, str):\n>                 template_name = (\n>                     construct_relative_path(\n>                         self.origin.template_name,\n>                         template_name,\n>                     ),\n>                 )\n>             else:\n>                 template_name = tuple(template_name)\n>             cache = context.render_context.dicts[0].setdefault(self, {})\n>             template = cache.get(template_name)\n>             if template is None:\n>                 template = context.template.engine.select_template(template_name)\n>                 cache[template_name] = template\n>         # Use the base.Template of a backends.django.Template.\n>         elif hasattr(template, \"template\"):\n>             template = template.template\n>         values = {\n>             name: var.resolve(context) for name, var in self.extra_context.items()\n>         }\n>         if self.isolated_context:\n>             return template.render(context.new(values))\n>         with context.push(**values):\n>             return template.render(context)\n> \n> \n> @register.tag(\"block\")\n> def do_block(parser, token):\n>     \"\"\"\n>     Define a block that can be overridden by child templates.\n>     \"\"\"\n>     # token.split_contents() isn't useful here because this tag doesn't accept\n>     # variable as arguments.\n>     bits = token.contents.split()\n>     if len(bits) != 2:\n>         raise TemplateSyntaxError(\"'%s' tag takes only one argument\" % bits[0])\n>     block_name = bits[1]\n>     # Keep track of the names of BlockNodes found in this template, so we can\n>     # check for duplication.\n>     try:\n>         if block_name in parser.__loaded_blocks:\n>             raise TemplateSyntaxError(\n>                 \"'%s' tag with name '%s' appears more than once\" % (bits[0], block_name)\n>             )\n>         parser.__loaded_blocks.append(block_name)\n>     except AttributeError:  # parser.__loaded_blocks isn't a list yet\n>         parser.__loaded_blocks = [block_name]\n>     nodelist = parser.parse((\"endblock\",))\n> \n>     # This check is kept for backwards-compatibility. See #3100.\n>     endblock = parser.next_token()\n>     acceptable_endblocks = (\"endblock\", \"endblock %s\" % block_name)\n>     if endblock.contents not in acceptable_endblocks:\n>         parser.invalid_block_tag(endblock, \"endblock\", acceptable_endblocks)\n> \n>     return BlockNode(block_name, nodelist)\n> \n> \n> def construct_relative_path(\n>     current_template_name,\n>     relative_name,\n>     allow_recursion=False,\n> ):\n>     \"\"\"\n>     Convert a relative path (starting with './' or '../') to the full template\n>     name based on the current_template_name.\n>     \"\"\"\n>     new_name = relative_name.strip(\"'\\\"\")\n>     if not new_name.startswith((\"./\", \"../\")):\n>         # relative_name is a variable or a literal that doesn't contain a\n>         # relative path.\n>         return relative_name\n> \n>     new_name = posixpath.normpath(\n>         posixpath.join(\n>             posixpath.dirname(current_template_name.lstrip(\"/\")),\n> TemplateSyntaxError(\n>             \"The relative path '%s' was translated to template name '%s', the \"\n>             \"same template in which the tag appears.\"\n>             % (relative_name, current_template_name)\n>         )\n>     has_quotes = (\n>         relative_name.startswith(('\"', \"'\")) and relative_name[0] == relative_name[-1]\n>     )\n>     return f'\"{new_name}\"' if has_quotes else new_name\n> \n> \n> @register.tag(\"extends\")\n> def do_extends(parser, token):\n>     \"\"\"\n>     Signal that this template extends a parent template.\n> \n>     This tag may be used in two ways: ``{% extends \"base\" %}`` (with quotes)\n>     uses the literal value \"base\" as the name of the parent template to extend,\n>     or ``{% extends variable %}`` uses the value of ``variable`` as either the\n>     name of the parent template to extend (if it evaluates to a string) or as\n>     the parent template itself (if it evaluates to a Template object).\n>     \"\"\"\n>     bits = token.split_contents()\n>     if len(bits) != 2:\n>         raise TemplateSyntaxError(\"'%s' takes one argument\" % bits[0])\n>     bits[1] = construct_relative_path(parser.origin.template_name, bits[1])\n>     parent_name = parser.compile_filter(bits[1])\n>     nodelist = parser.parse()\n>     if nodelist.get_nodes_by_type(ExtendsNode):\n>         raise TemplateSyntaxError(\n>             \"'%s' cannot appear more than once in the same template\" % bits[0]\n>         )\n>     return ExtendsNode(nodelist, parent_name)\n> \n> \n> @register.tag(\"include\")\n> def do_include(parser, token):\n>     \"\"\"\n>     Load a template and render it with the current context. You can pass\n>     additional context using keyword arguments.\n> \n>     Example::\n> \n>         {% include \"foo/some_include\" %}\n>         {% include \"foo/some_include\" with bar=\"BAZZ!\" baz=\"BING!\" %}\n> \n>     Use the ``only`` argument to exclude the current context when rendering\n>     the included template::\n> \n>         {% include \"foo/some_include\" only %}\n>         {% include \"foo/some_include\" with bar=\"1\" only %}\n>     \"\"\"\n>     bits = token.split_contents()\n>     if len(bits) < 2:\n>         raise TemplateSyntaxError(\n>             \"%r tag takes at least one argument: the name of the template to \"\n>             \"be included.\" % bits[0]\n>         )\n>     options = {}\n>     remaining_bits = bits[2:]\n>     while remaining_bits:\n>         option = remaining_bits.pop(0)\n>         if option in options:\n>             raise TemplateSyntaxError(\n>                 \"The %r option was specified more than once.\" % option\n>             )\n>         if option == \"with\":\n>             value = token_kwargs(remaining_bits, parser, support_legacy=False)\n>             if not value:\n>                 raise TemplateSyntaxError(\n>                     '\"with\" in %r tag needs at least one keyword argument.' % bits[0]\n>                 )\n>         elif option == \"only\":\n>             value = True\n>         else:\n>             raise TemplateSyntaxError(\n>                 \"Unknown argument for %r tag: %r.\" % (bits[0], option)\n>             )\n>         options[option] = value\n>     isolated_context = options.get(\"only\", False)\n>     namemap = options.get(\"with\", {})\n>     bits[1] = construct_relative_path(\n>         parser.origin.template_name,\n>         bits[1],\n>         allow_recursion=True,\n>     )\n>     return IncludeNode(\n>         parser.compile_filter(bits[1]),\n>         extra_context=namemap,\n>         isolated_context=isolated_context,\n>     )"

Input
{
  "path": "django/template/__init__.py",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c"
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2: Django's support for templates.\\n3: \\n4: The django.template namespace contains two independent subsystems:\\n5: \\n6: 1. Multiple Template Engines: support for pluggable template backends,\\n7:    built-in backends and backend-independent APIs\\n8: 2. Django Template Language: Django's own template engine, including its\\n9:    built-in loaders, context processors, tags and filters.\\n10: \\n11: Ideally these subsystems would be implemented in distinct packages. However\\n12: keeping them together made the implementation of Multiple Template Engines\\n13: less disruptive .\\n14: \\n15: Here's a breakdown of which modules belong to which subsystem.\\n16: \\n17: Multiple Template Engines:\\n18: \\n19: - django.template.backends.*\\n20: - django.template.loader\\n21: - django.template.response\\n22: \\n23: Django Template Language:\\n24: \\n25: - django.template.base\\n26: - django.template.context\\n27: - django.template.context_processors\\n28: - django.template.loaders.*\\n29: - django.template.debug\\n30: - django.template.defaultfilters\\n31: - django.template.defaulttags\\n32: - django.template.engine\\n33: - django.template.loader_tags\\n34: - django.template.smartif\\n35: \\n36: Shared:\\n37: \\n38: - django.template.utils\\n39: \\n40: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n41: \\n42: # Multiple Template Engines\\n43: \\n44: from .engine import Engine\\n45: from .utils import EngineHandler\\n46: \\n47: engines = EngineHandler()\\n48: \\n49: __all__ = (\\\"Engine\\\", \\\"engines\\\")\\n50: \\n51: \\n52: # Django Template Language\\n53: \\n54: # Public exceptions\\n55: from .base import VariableDoesNotExist  # NOQA isort:skip\\n56: from .context import Context, ContextPopException, RequestContext  # NOQA isort:skip\\n57: from .exceptions import TemplateDoesNotExist, TemplateSyntaxError  # NOQA isort:skip\\n58: \\n59: # Template parts\\n60: from .base import (  # NOQA isort:skip\\n61:     Node,\\n62:     NodeList,\\n63:     Origin,\\n64:     PartialTemplate,\\n65:     Template,\\n66:     Variable,\\n67: )\\n68: \\n69: # Library management\\n70: from .library import Library  # NOQA isort:skip\\n71: \\n72: # Import the .autoreload module to trigger the registrations of signals.\\n73: from . import autoreload  # NOQA isort:skip\\n74: \\n75: __all__ += (\\\"Template\\\", \\\"Context\\\", \\\"RequestContext\\\")\\n76: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c@674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3/-/blob/django/template/__init__.py\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: \"\"\"\n2: Django's support for templates.\n3: \n4: The django.template namespace contains two independent subsystems:\n5: \n6: 1. Multiple Template Engines: support for pluggable template backends,\n7:    built-in backends and backend-independent APIs\n8: 2. Django Template Language: Django's own template engine, including its\n9:    built-in loaders, context processors, tags and filters.\n10: \n11: Ideally these subsystems would be implemented in distinct packages. However\n12: keeping them together made the implementation of Multiple Template Engines\n13: less disruptive .\n14: \n15: Here's a breakdown of which modules belong to which subsystem.\n16: \n17: Multiple Template Engines:\n18: \n19: - django.template.backends.*\n20: - django.template.loader\n21: - django.template.response\n22: \n23: Django Template Language:\n24: \n25: - django.template.base\n26: - django.template.context\n27: - django.template.context_processors\n28: - django.template.loaders.*\n29: - django.template.debug\n30: - django.template.defaultfilters\n31: - django.template.defaulttags\n32: - django.template.engine\n33: - django.template.loader_tags\n34: - django.template.smartif\n35: \n36: Shared:\n37: \n38: - django.template.utils\n39: \n40: \"\"\"\n41: \n42: # Multiple Template Engines\n43: \n44: from .engine import Engine\n45: from .utils import EngineHandler\n46: \n47: engines = EngineHandler()\n48: \n49: __all__ = (\"Engine\", \"engines\")\n50: \n51: \n52: # Django Template Language\n53: \n54: # Public exceptions\n55: from .base import VariableDoesNotExist  # NOQA isort:skip\n56: from .context import Context, ContextPopException, RequestContext  # NOQA isort:skip\n57: from .exceptions import TemplateDoesNotExist, TemplateSyntaxError  # NOQA isort:skip\n58: \n59: # Template parts\n60: from .base import (  # NOQA isort:skip\n61:     Node,\n62:     NodeList,\n63:     Origin,\n64:     PartialTemplate,\n65:     Template,\n66:     Variable,\n67: )\n68: \n69: # Library management\n70: from .library import Library  # NOQA isort:skip\n71: \n72: # Import the .autoreload module to trigger the registrations of signals.\n73: from . import autoreload  # NOQA isort:skip\n74: \n75: __all__ += (\"Template\", \"Context\", \"RequestContext\")\n76: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c@674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3/-/blob/django/template/__init__.py"
  }
}

[ ] This PR targets the `main` branch. <!-- Backports will be evaluated and done by mergers, when necessary. -->\\n - [ ] The commit message is written in past tense, mentions the ticket number, and ends with a period.\\n - [ ] I have checked the \\\"Has patch\\\" ticket flag in the Trac system.\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":20,\"startLine\":1},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":12,\"startLine\":1}}],\"path\":\".github/pull_request_template.md\"},{\"added\":5,\"deleted\":2,\"hunks\":[{\"body\":\"   Run_benchmarks:\\n     if: contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'benchmark')\\n     runs-on: ubuntu-latest\\n+    timeout-minutes: 60\\n     steps:\\n       - name: Checkout Benchmark Repo\\n-        uses: actions/checkout@v5\\n+        uses: actions/checkout@v6\\n         with:\\n           repository: django/django-asv\\n           path: \\\".\\\"\\n+          persist-credentials: false\\n       - name: Setup Miniforge\\n-        uses: conda-incubator/setup-miniconda@v3\\n+        # Pinned to v3.2.0.\\n+        uses: conda-incubator/setup-miniconda@835234971496cad1653abb28a638a281cf32541f\\n         with:\\n           miniforge-version: \\\"24.1.2-0\\\"\\n           activate-environment: asv-bench\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":17,\"startLine\":11},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":14,\"startLine\":11},\"section\":\"jobs:\"}],\"path\":\".github/workflows/benchmark.yml\"},{\"added\":68,\"deleted\":0,\"hunks\":[{\"body\":\"+name: Check migrations\\n+\\n+on:\\n+  pull_request:\\n+    paths:\\n+      - 'tests/**/models.py'\\n+      - 'tests/**/migrations/**'\\n+      - 'tests/**/*migrations/**/*.py'\\n+      - 'scripts/check_migrations.py'\\n+  push:\\n+    branches:\\n+      - main\\n+\\n+concurrency:\\n+  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}\\n+  cancel-in-progress: true\\n+\\n+permissions:\\n+  contents: read\\n+\\n+jobs:\\n+  check-migrations:\\n+    name: Check test migrations\\n+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\\n+    timeout-minutes: 60\\n+\\n+    services:\\n+      postgres:\\n+        image: postgres:15-alpine\\n+        env:\\n+          POSTGRES_DB: django\\n+          POSTGRES_USER: user\\n+          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres\\n+        ports:\\n+          - 5432:5432\\n+        options: >-\\n+          --health-cmd pg_isready\\n+          --health-interval 10s\\n+          --health-timeout 5s\\n+          --health-retries 5\\n+\\n+    steps:\\n+      - name: Checkout\\n+        uses: actions/checkout@v6\\n+        with:\\n+          persist-credentials: false\\n+\\n+      - name: Set up Python\\n+        uses: actions/setup-python@v6\\n+        with:\\n+          python-version: '3.14'\\n+          cache: 'pip'\\n+          cache-dependency-path: 'tests/requirements/py3.txt'\\n+\\n+      - name: Install dependencies\\n+        run: |\\n+          python -m pip install --upgrade pip wheel\\n+          python -m pip install -e . psycopg[binary]\\n+\\n+      - name: Create PostgreSQL settings file\\n+        run: mv ./.github/workflows/data/test_postgres.py.tpl ./tests/test_postgres.py\\n+\\n+      - name: Check for missing migrations\\n+        env:\\n+          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: test_postgres\\n+          PYTHONPATH: ${{ github.workspace }}/tests:${{ github.workspace }}\\n+        run: |\\n+          python scripts/check_migrations.py\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":68,\"startLine\":1},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":0,\"startLine\":0}}],\"path\":\".github/workflows/check-migrations.yml\"},{\"added\":12,\"deleted\":4,\"hunks\":[{\"body\":\"   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}\\n   cancel-in-progress: true\\n \\n+permissions:\\n+  contents: read\\n+\\n jobs:\\n   check-commit-prefix:\\n     if: startsWith(github.event.pull_request.base.ref, 'stable/')\\n     runs-on: ubuntu-latest\\n+    timeout-minutes: 60\\n     steps:\\n-      - uses: actions/checkout@v5\\n+      - uses: actions/checkout@v6\\n+        with:\\n+          persist-credentials: false\\n \\n       - name: Calculate commit prefix\\n         id: vars\\n+        env:\\n+          BASE: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.ref }}\\n         run: |\\n-          BASE=\\\"${{ github.event.pull_request.base.ref }}\\\"\\n           echo \\\"BASE=$BASE\\\" >> $GITHUB_ENV\\n           VERSION=\\\"${BASE#stable/}\\\"\\n           echo \\\"prefix=[$VERSION]\\\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\\n \\n       - name: Check PR title prefix\\n         env:\\n           TITLE: ${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}\\n+          PREFIX: ${{ steps.vars.outputs.prefix }}\\n         run: |\\n-          PREFIX=\\\"${{ steps.vars.outputs.prefix }}\\\"\\n           if [[ \\\"$TITLE\\\" != \\\"$PREFIX\\\"* ]]; then\\n             echo \\\"\u274c PR title must start with the required prefix: $PREFIX\\\"\\n             exit 1\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":33,\"startLine\":8},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":26,\"startLine\":8},\"section\":\"concurrency:\"},{\"body\":\"           git fetch origin pull/${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}/head:pr\\n \\n       - name: Check commit messages prefix\\n+        env:\\n+          PREFIX: ${{ steps.vars.outputs.prefix }}\\n         run: |\\n-          PREFIX=\\\"${{ steps.vars.outputs.prefix }}\\\"\\n           COMMITS=$(git rev-list base..pr)\\n           echo \\\"Checking commit messages for required prefix: $PREFIX\\\"\\n           FAIL=0\\n\",\"newRange\":{\"lines\":9,\"startLine\":47},\"oldRange\":{\"lines\":8,\"startLine\":40},\"section\":\"jobs:\"}],\"path\":\".github/workflows/check_commit_messages.yml\"}],\"hasNextPage\":true,\"head\":\"674eda1c03a3187905f48afee0f15226aa62fdf3\",\"repository\":\"github.com/sg-evals/django--674eda1c\",\"totalAdded\":98,\"totalDeleted\":7,\"totalModified\":5}",

            "body": " #### Trac ticket number\n-<!-- Replace XXXXX with the corresponding Trac ticket number, or delete the line and write \"N/A\" if this is a trivial PR. -->\n+<!-- Replace XXXXX with the corresponding Trac ticket number. -->\n+<!-- Or delete the line and write \"N/A\" if this is a trivial PR. -->\n \n ticket-XXXXX\n \n #### Branch description\n Provide a concise overview of the issue or rationale behind the proposed changes.\n \n+#### AI Assistance Disclosure (REQUIRED)\n+<!-- Please select exactly ONE of the following: -->\n+- [ ] **No AI tools were used** in preparing this PR.\n+- [ ] **If AI tools were used**, I have disclosed which ones, and fully reviewed and verified their output.\n+\n #### Checklist\n+- [ ] This PR follows the [contribution guidelines](https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/stable/internals/contributing/writing-code/submitting-patches/).\n+- [ ] This PR **does not** disclose a security vulnerability (see [vulnerability reporting](https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/stable/internals/security/)).\n - [ ] This PR targets the `main` branch. <!-- Backports will be evaluated and done by mergers, when necessary. -->\n - [ ] The commit message is written in past tense, mentions the ticket number, and ends with a period.\n - [ ] I have checked the \"Has patch\" ticket flag in the Trac system.\n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 20,
              "startLine": 1
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 12,
              "startLine": 1
            }
          }
        ],
        "path": ".github/pull_request_template.md"
      },
      {
        "added": 5,
        "deleted": 2,
        "hunks": [
          {
            "body": "   Run_benchmarks:\n     if: contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'benchmark')\n     runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n+    timeout-minutes: 60\n     steps:\n       - name: Checkout Benchmark Repo\n-        uses: actions/checkout@v5\n+        uses: actions/checkout@v6\n         with:\n           repository: django/django-asv\n           path: \".\"\n+          persist-credentials: false\n       - name: Setup Miniforge\n-        uses: conda-incubator/setup-miniconda@v3\n+        # Pinned to v3.2.0.\n+        uses: conda-incubator/setup-miniconda@835234971496cad1653abb28a638a281cf32541f\n         with:\n           miniforge-version: \"24.1.2-0\"\n           activate-environment: asv-bench\n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 17,
              "startLine": 11
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 14,
              "startLine": 11
            },
            "section": "jobs:"
          }
        ],
        "path": ".github/workflows/benchmark.yml"
      },
      {
        "added": 68,
        "deleted": 0,
        "hunks": [
          {
            "body": "+name: Check migrations\n+\n+on:\n+  pull_request:\n+    paths:\n+      - 'tests/**/models.py'\n+      - 'tests/**/migrations/**'\n+      - 'tests/**/*migrations/**/*.py'\n+      - 'scripts/check_migrations.py'\n+  push:\n+    branches:\n+      - main\n+\n+concurrency:\n+  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}\n+  cancel-in-progress: true\n+\n+permissions:\n+  contents: read\n+\n+jobs:\n+  check-migrations:\n+    name: Check test migrations\n+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n+    timeout-minutes: 60\n+\n+    services:\n+      postgres:\n+        image: postgres:15-alpine\n+        env:\n+          POSTGRES_DB: django\n+          POSTGRES_USER: user\n+          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres\n+        ports:\n+          - 5432:5432\n+        options: >-\n+          --health-cmd pg_isready\n+          --health-interval 10s\n+          --health-timeout 5s\n+          --health-retries 5\n+\n+    steps:\n+      - name: Checkout\n+        uses: actions/checkout@v6\n+        with:\n+          persist-credentials: false\n+\n+      - name: Set up Python\n+        uses: actions/setup-python@v6\n+        with:\n+          python-version: '3.14'\n+          cache: 'pip'\n+          cache-dependency-path: 'tests/requirements/py3.txt'\n+\n+      - name: Install dependencies\n+        run: |\n+          python -m pip install --upgrade pip wheel\n+          python -m pip install -e . psycopg[binary]\n+\n+      - name: Create PostgreSQL settings file\n+        run: mv ./.github/workflows/data/test_postgres.py.tpl ./tests/test_postgres.py\n+\n+      - name: Check for missing migrations\n+        env:\n+          DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE: test_postgres\n+          PYTHONPATH: ${{ github.workspace }}/tests:${{ github.workspace }}\n+        run: |\n+          python scripts/check_migrations.py\n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 68,
              "startLine": 1
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 0,
              "startLine": 0
            }
          }
        ],
        "path": ".github/workflows/check-migrations.yml"
      },
      {
        "added": 12,
        "deleted": 4,
        "hunks": [
          {
            "body": "   group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}\n   cancel-in-progress: true\n \n+permissions:\n+  contents: read\n+\n jobs:\n   check-commit-prefix:\n     if: startsWith(github.event.pull_request.base.ref, 'stable/')\n     runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n+    timeout-minutes: 60\n     steps:\n-      - uses: actions/checkout@v5\n+      - uses: actions/checkout@v6\n+        with:\n+          persist-credentials: false\n \n       - name: Calculate commit prefix\n         id: vars\n+        env:\n+          BASE: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.ref }}\n         run: |\n-          BASE=\"${{ github.event.pull_request.base.ref }}\"\n           echo \"BASE=$BASE\" >> $GITHUB_ENV\n           VERSION=\"${BASE#stable/}\"\n           echo \"prefix=[$VERSION]\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n \n       - name: Check PR title prefix\n         env:\n           TITLE: ${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}\n+          PREFIX: ${{ steps.vars.outputs.prefix }}\n         run: |\n-          PREFIX=\"${{ steps.vars.outputs.prefix }}\"\n           if [[ \"$TITLE\" != \"$PREFIX\"* ]]; then\n             echo \"\u274c PR title must start with the required prefix: $PREFIX\"\n             exit 1\n",
            "newRange": {
              "lines": 33,
              "startLine": 8
            },
            "oldRange": {
              "lines": 26,
              "startLine": 8
            },
            "section": "concurrency:"
          },
          {
            "body": "           git fetch origin pull/${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}/head:pr\n \n       - name: Check commit messages prefix\n+        env:\n+          PREFIX: ${{ steps.vars.outputs.prefix }}\n         run: |\n-          PREFIX=\"${{ steps.vars.outputs.prefix }}\"\n           COMMITS=$(git rev-list base..pr)\n           echo \"Checking commit messages for required prefix: $PREFIX\"\n           FAIL=0\n",

74. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "python3.11 << 'EOF'\nimport sys\nsys.path.insert(0, '/home/claude/.local/lib/python3.11/site-packages')\nfrom django.template import Engine, Context\n\n# Specific scenario: middle template removes inner from outer block\ntemplates = {\n    \"base.html\": \"{% block outer %}[outer-base]{% block inner %}[inner-base]{% endblock %}[/outer-base]{% endblock %}\",\n    \"layout.html\": \"{% extends 'base.html' %}{% block outer %}[outer-layout-no-inner]{% endblock %}\",\n    \"page.html\": \"{% extends 'layout.html' %}{% block inner %}[inner-page]{% endblock %}\",\n    \"inclusion.html\": \"{{ result }}\",\n}\nengine = Engine(loaders=[('django.template.loaders.locmem.Loader', templates)])\noutput = engine.get_template(\"page.html\").render(Context({}))\nprint(f\"Middle removes inner: {output!r}\")\nprint(f\"Expected: '[outer-layout-no-inner]' (inner-page unreachable)\")\nprint()\n\n# Another case: What if ExtendsNode.blocks is not computed correctly?\n# Let me check what self.blocks contains for layout.html in the standard scenario\nfrom django.template.loader_tags import BlockNode, ExtendsNode\nfrom django.template import Engine\n\ne = Engine(loaders=[('django.template.loaders.locmem.Loader', {\n    \"base.html\": \"{% block outer %}[outer-base]{% block inner %}[inner-base]{% endblock %}[/outer-base]{% endblock %}\",\n    \"layout.html\": \"{% extends 'base.html' %}{% block outer %}[outer-layout]{% block inner %}[inner-layout]{% endblock %}[/outer-layout]{% endblock %}\",\n    \"page.html\": \"{% extends 'layout.html' %}{% block inner %}[inner-page]{% endblock %}\",\n    \"inclusion.html\": \"{{ result }}\",\n})])\n\nlayout_template = e.get_template(\"layout.html\")\npage_template = e.get_template(\"page.html\")\n\n# Find ExtendsNode in layout.html\nfor node in layout_template.nodelist:\n    if isinstance(node, ExtendsNode):\n        print(f\"layout.html ExtendsNode.blocks keys: {list(node.blocks.keys())}\")\n        for name, block in node.blocks.items():\n            print(f\"  block '{name}': {block}\")\n        break\n\n# Find ExtendsNode in page.html\nfor node in page_template.nodelist:\n    if isinstance(node, ExtendsNode):\n        print(f\"page.html ExtendsNode.blocks keys: {list(node.blocks.keys())}\")\n        for name, block in node.blocks.items():\n            print(f\"  block '{name}': {block}\")\n        break\nEOF",
  "description": "Inspect ExtendsNode.blocks for layout and page templates"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "Middle removes inner: '[outer-layout-no-inner]'\nExpected: '[outer-layout-no-inner]' (inner-page unreachable)\n\nlayout.html ExtendsNode.blocks keys: ['outer', 'inner']\n  block 'outer': <Block Node: outer. Contents: [<TextNode: '[outer-layout]'>, <Block Node: inner. Contents: [<TextNode: '[inner-layout]'>]>, <TextNode: '[/outer-layout]'>]>\n  block 'inner': <Block Node: inner. Contents: [<TextNode: '[inner-layout]'>]>\npage.html ExtendsNode.blocks keys: ['inner']\n  block 'inner': <Block Node: inner. Contents: [<TextNode: '[inner-page]'>]>"
}
75. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "python3.11 << 'EOF'\nimport sys\nsys.path.insert(0, '/home/claude/.local/lib/python3.11/site-packages')\nfrom django.template import Engine, Context\n\n# Scenario: base has inner as sibling of outer, layout makes inner nested inside outer\ntemplates = {\n    \"base.html\": \"[base]{% block outer %}[outer-base]{% endblock %}[mid]{% block inner %}[inner-base]{% endblock %}[/base]\",\n    \"layout.html\": \"{% extends 'base.html' %}{% block outer %}[outer-layout]{% block inner %}[inner-layout]{% endblock %}{% endblock %}\",\n    \"page.html\": \"{% extends 'layout.html' %}{% block inner %}[inner-page]{% endblock %}\",\n    \"inclusion.html\": \"{{ result }}\",\n}\nengine = Engine(loaders=[('django.template.loaders.locmem.Loader', templates)])\noutput = engine.get_template(\"page.html\").render(Context({}))\nprint(f\"Output: {output!r}\")\nprint(f\"Expected (inner appears twice): '[base][outer-layout][inner-page][mid][inner-page][/base]'\")\nprint()\n\n# Now the KEY scenario from the incident:\n# What if ExtendsNode.blocks for layout.html picks up inner NESTED inside outer\n# AND the block context has it added from both layout (as nested) and from page (as child override)?\n# The bug might be something I haven't tried yet.\n\n# Let me try: what happens with deepcopy concerns?\n# When ExtendsNode.blocks is populated with {outer: layout_outer, inner: layout_inner}\n# and layout_inner is the SAME object as what's inside layout_outer.nodelist\n# Could there be any issue with this object being used in multiple contexts?\n\n# Test: render the same template TWICE (potential thread safety or caching issue)\ne2 = Engine(loaders=[('django.template.loaders.cached.Loader', [\n    ('django.template.loaders.locmem.Loader', {\n        \"base.html\": \"{% block outer %}[outer-base]{% block inner %}[inner-base]{% endblock %}[/outer-base]{% endblock %}\",\n        \"layout.html\": \"{% extends 'base.html' %}{% block outer %}[outer-layout]{% block inner %}[inner-layout]{% endblock %}[/outer-layout]{% endblock %}\",\n        \"page.html\": \"{% extends 'layout.html' %}{% block inner %}[inner-page]{% endblock %}\",\n        \"inclusion.html\": \"{{ result }}\",\n    })\n])])\n\nfor i in range(3):\n    output = e2.render_to_string(\"page.html\")\n    expected = '[outer-layout][inner-page][/outer-layout]'\n    print(f\"Render {i+1}: {output!r} {'PASS' if output == expected else 'FAIL'}\")\nEOF",
  "description": "Test multiple renders and edge cases"
}
Output

  "interrupted": false,

  "noOutputExpected": false,

  "stdout": "Output: '[base][outer-layout][inner-page][mid][inner-page][/base]'\nExpected (inner appears twice): '[base][outer-layout][inner-page][mid][inner-page][/base]'\n\nRender 1: '[outer-layout][inner-page][/outer-layout]' PASS\nRender 2: '[outer-layout][inner-page][/outer-layout]' PASS\nRender 3: '[outer-layout][inner-page][/outer-layout]' PASS"
}

77. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "cat > /workspace/django/template/loader_tags.py << 'ENDOFFILE'\nimport posixpath\nfrom collections import defaultdict\n\nfrom django.utils.safestring import mark_safe\n\nfrom .base import Node, Template, TemplateSyntaxError, TextNode, Variable, token_kwargs\nfrom .library import Library\n\nregister = Library()\n\nBLOCK_CONTEXT_KEY = \"block_context\"\n\n\nclass BlockContext:\n    def __init__(self):\n        # Dictionary of block queues. Each block name maps to a list of\n        # BlockNode instances ordered from lowest priority (base template,\n        # at index 0) to highest priority (innermost child template, at the\n        # end). pop() returns the highest-priority block; push() and\n        # insert(0, \u2026) are used when building this structure.\n        self.blocks = defaultdict(list)\n\n    def __repr__(self):\n        return f\"<{self.__class__.__qualname__}: blocks={self.blocks!r}>\"\n\n    def add_blocks(self, blocks):\n        # insert(0, \u2026) prepends each new block so that the first caller's\n        # blocks end up at the tail of the list (highest priority when\n        # pop() is used). This guarantees that a child template's override\n        # always wins over a parent's, regardless of the order in which\n        # intermediate ExtendsNode.render() calls invoke add_blocks.\n        for name, block in blocks.items():\n            self.blocks[name].insert(0, block)\n\n    def pop(self, name):\n        try:\n            return self.blocks[name].pop()\n        except IndexError:\n            return None\n\n    def push(self, name, block):\n        self.blocks[name].append(block)\n\n    def get_block(self, name):\n        try:\n            return self.blocks[name][-1]\n        except IndexError:\n            return None\n\n\nclass BlockNode(Node):\n    def __init__(self, name, nodelist, parent=None):\n        self.name = name\n        self.nodelist = nodelist\n        self.parent = parent\n\n    def __repr__(self):\n        return \"<Block Node: %s. Contents: %r>\" % (self.name, self.nodelist)\n\n    def render(self, context):\n        block_context = context.render_context.get(BLOCK_CONTEXT_KEY)\n        with context.push():\n            if block_context is None:\n                context[\"block\"] = self\n                result = self.nodelist.render(context)\n            else:\n                push = block = block_context.pop(self.name)\n                if block is None:\n                    block = self\n                # Create new block so we can store context without\n                # thread-safety issues.\n                block = type(self)(block.name, block.nodelist)\n                block.context = context\n                context[\"block\"] = block\n                result = block.nodelist.render(context)\n                if push is not None:\n                    block_context.push(self.name, push)\n        return result\n\n    def super(self):\n        if not hasattr(self, \"context\"):\n            raise TemplateSyntaxError(\n                \"'%s' object has no attribute 'context'. Did you use \"\n                \"{{ block.super }} in a base template?\" % self.__class__.__name__\n            )\n        render_context = self.context.render_context\n        if (\n            BLOCK_CONTEXT_KEY in render_context\n            and render_context[BLOCK_CONTEXT_KEY].get_block(self.name) is not None\n        ):\n            return mark_safe(self.render(self.context))\n        return \"\"\n\n\nclass ExtendsNode(Node):\n    must_be_first = True\n    context_key = \"extends_context\"\n\n    def __init__(self, nodelist, parent_name, template_dirs=None):\n        self.nodelist = nodelist\n        self.parent_name = parent_name\n        self.template_dirs = template_dirs\n        self.blocks = {n.name: n for n in nodelist.get_nodes_by_type(BlockNode)}\n\n    def __repr__(self):\n        return \"<%s: extends %s>\" % (self.__class__.__name__, self.parent_name.token)\n\n    def find_template(self, template_name, context):\n        \"\"\"\n        This is a wrapper around engine.find_template(). A history is kept in\n        the render_context attribute between successive extends calls and\n        passed as the skip argument. This enables extends to work recursively\n        without extending the same template twice.\n        \"\"\"\n        history = context.render_context.setdefault(\n            self.context_key,\n            [self.origin],\n        )\n        template, origin = context.template.engine.find_template(\n            template_name,\n            skip=history,\n        )\n        history.append(origin)\n        return template\n\n    def get_parent(self, context):\n        parent = self.parent_name.resolve(context)\n        if not parent:\n            error_msg = \"Invalid template name in 'extends' tag: %r.\" % parent\n            if self.parent_name.filters or isinstance(self.parent_name.var, Variable):\n                error_msg += (\n                    \" Got this from the '%s' variable.\" % self.parent_name.token\n                )\n            raise TemplateSyntaxError(error_msg)\n        if isinstance(parent, Template):\n            # parent is a django.template.Template\n            return parent\n        if isinstance(getattr(parent, \"template\", None), Template):\n            # parent is a django.template.backends.django.Template\n            return parent.template\n        return self.find_template(parent, context)\n\n    def render(self, context):\n        compiled_parent = self.get_parent(context)\n\n        if BLOCK_CONTEXT_KEY not in context.render_context:\n            context.render_context[BLOCK_CONTEXT_KEY] = BlockContext()\n        block_context = context.render_context[BLOCK_CONTEXT_KEY]\n\n        # Add the block nodes from this template level to the block context.\n        # This must happen before adding root blocks so that the child\n        # template's blocks are registered at the correct priority. Because\n        # add_blocks() uses insert(0, \u2026), the first caller (the innermost\n        # child) ends up at the tail of each list and therefore wins when\n        # block_context.pop() is called during rendering.\n        block_context.add_blocks(self.blocks)\n\n        # If this block's parent doesn't have an extends node it is the root,\n        # and its block nodes also need to be added to the block context.\n        for node in compiled_parent.nodelist:\n            # The ExtendsNode has to be the first non-text node.\n            if not isinstance(node, TextNode):\n                if not isinstance(node, ExtendsNode):\n                    blocks = {\n                        n.name: n\n                        for n in compiled_parent.nodelist.get_nodes_by_type(BlockNode)\n                    }\n                    block_context.add_blocks(blocks)\n                break\n\n        # Call Template._render explicitly so the parser context stays\n        # the same.\n        with context.render_context.push_state(compiled_parent, isolated_context=False):\n            return compiled_parent._render(context)\n\n\nclass IncludeNode(Node):\n    context_key = \"__include_context\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self, template, *args, extra_context=None, isolated_context=False, **kwargs\n    ):\n        self.template = template\n        self.extra_context = extra_context or {}\n        self.isolated_context = isolated_context\n        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)\n\n    def __repr__(self):\n        return f\"<{self.__class__.__qualname__}: template={self.template!r}>\"\n\n    def render(self, context):\n        \"\"\"\n        Render the specified template and context. Cache the template object\n        in render_context to avoid reparsing and loading when used in a for\n        loop.\n        \"\"\"\n        template = self.template.resolve(context)\n        # Does this quack like a Template?\n        if not callable(getattr(template, \"render\", None)):\n            # If not, try the cache and select_template().\n            template_name = template or ()\n            if isinstance(template_name, str):\n                template_name = (\n                    construct_relative_path(\n                        self.origin.template_name,\n                        template_name,\n                    ),\n                )\n            else:\n                template_name = tuple(template_name)\n            cache = context.render_context.dicts[0].setdefault(self, {})\n            template = cache.get(template_name)\n            if template is None:\n                template = context.template.engine.select_template(template_name)\n                cache[template_name] = template\n        # Use the base.Template of a backends.django.Template.\n        elif hasattr(template, \"template\"):\n            template = template.template\n        values = {\n            name: var.resolve(context) for name, var in self.extra_context.items()\n        }\n        if self.isolated_context:\n            return template.render(context.new(values))\n        with context.push(**values):\n            return template.render(context)\n\n\n@register.tag(\"block\")\ndef do_block(parser, token):\n    \"\"\"\n    Define a block that can be overridden by child templates.\n    \"\"\"\n    # token.split_contents() isn't useful here because this tag doesn't accept\n    # variable as arguments.\n    bits = token.contents.split()\n    if len(bits) != 2:\n        raise TemplateSyntaxError(\"'%s' tag takes only one argument\" % bits[0])\n    block_name = bits[1]\n    # Keep track of the names of BlockNodes found in this template, so we can\n    # check for duplication.\n    try:\n        if block_name in parser.__loaded_blocks:\n            raise TemplateSyntaxError(\n                \"'%s' tag with name '%s' appears more than once\" % (bits[0], block_name)\n            )\n        parser.__loaded_blocks.append(block_name)\n    except AttributeError:  # parser.__loaded_blocks isn't a list yet\n        parser.__loaded_blocks = [block_name]\n    nodelist = parser.parse((\"endblock\",))\n\n    # This check is kept for backwards-compatibility. See #3100.\n    endblock = parser.next_token()\n    acceptable_endblocks = (\"endblock\", \"endblock %s\" % block_name)\n    if endblock.contents not in acceptable_endblocks:\n        parser.invalid_block_tag(endblock, \"endblock\", acceptable_endblocks)\n\n    return BlockNode(block_name, nodelist)\n\n\ndef construct_relative_path(\n    current_template_name,\n    relative_name,\n    allow_recursion=False,\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    Convert a relative path (starting with './' or '../') to the full template\n    name based on the current_template_name.\n    \"\"\"\n    new_name = relative_name.strip(\"'\\\"\")\n    if not new_name.startswith((\"./\", \"../\")):\n        # relative_name is a variable or a literal that doesn't contain a\n        # relative path.\n        return relative_name\n\n    if current_template_name is None:\n        # Unknown origin (e.g. Template('...').render(Context({...})).\n        raise TemplateSyntaxError(\n            f\"The relative path {relative_name} cannot be evaluated due to \"\n            \"an unknown template origin.\"\n        )\n\n    new_name = posixpath.normpath(\n        posixpath.join(\n            posixpath.dirname(current_template_name.lstrip(\"/\")),\n            new_name,\n        )\n    )\n    if new_name.startswith(\"../\"):\n        raise TemplateSyntaxError(\n            \"The relative path '%s' points outside the file hierarchy that \"\n            \"template '%s' is in.\" % (relative_name, current_template_name)\n        )\n    if not allow_recursion and current_template_name.lstrip(\"/\") == new_name:\n        raise TemplateSyntaxError(\n            \"The relative path '%s' was translated to template name '%s', the \"\n            \"same template in which the tag appears.\"\n            % (relative_name, current_template_name)\n        )\n    has_quotes = (\n        relative_name.startswith(('\"', \"'\")) and relative_name[0] == relative_name[-1]\n    )\n    return f'\"{new_name}\"' if has_quotes else new_name\n\n\n@register.tag(\"extends\")\ndef do_extends(parser, token):\n    \"\"\"\n    Signal that this template extends a parent template.\n\n    This tag may be used in two ways: ``{% extends \"base\" %}`` (with quotes)\n    uses the literal value \"base\" as the name of the parent template to extend,\n    or ``{% extends variable %}`` uses the value of ``variable`` as either the\n    name of the parent template to extend (if it evaluates to a string) or as\n    the parent template itself (if it evaluates to a Template object).\n    \"\"\"\n    bits = token.split_contents()\n    if len(bits) != 2:\n        raise TemplateSyntaxError(\"'%s' takes one argument\" % bits[0])\n    bits[1] = construct_relative_path(parser.origin.template_name, bits[1])\n    parent_name = parser.compile_filter(bits[1])\n    nodelist = parser.parse()\n    if nodelist.get_nodes_by_type(ExtendsNode):\n        raise TemplateSyntaxError(\n            \"'%s' cannot appear more than once in the same template\" % bits[0]\n        )\n    return ExtendsNode(nodelist, parent_name)\n\n\n@register.tag(\"include\")\ndef do_include(parser, token):\n    \"\"\"\n    Load a template and render it with the current context. You can pass\n    additional context using keyword arguments.\n\n    Example::\n\n        {% include \"foo/some_include\" %}\n        {% include \"foo/some_include\" with bar=\"BAZZ!\\\" baz=\"BING!\" %}\n\n    Use the ``only`` argument to exclude the current context when rendering\n    the included template::\n\n        {% include \"foo/some_include\" only %}\n        {% include \"foo/some_include\" with bar=\"1\" only %}\n    \"\"\"\n    bits = token.split_contents()\n    if len(bits) < 2:\n        raise TemplateSyntaxError(\n            \"%r tag takes at least one argument: the name of the template to \"\n            \"be included.\" % bits[0]\n        )\n    options = {}\n    remaining_bits = bits[2:]\n    while remaining_bits:\n        option = remaining_bits.pop(0)\n        if option in options:\n            raise TemplateSyntaxError(\n                \"The %r option was specified more than once.\" % option\n            )\n        if option == \"with\":\n            value = token_kwargs(remaining_bits, parser, support_legacy=False)\n            if not value:\n                raise TemplateSyntaxError(\n                    '\"with\" in %r tag needs at least one keyword argument.' % bits[0]\n                )\n        elif option == \"only\":\n            value = True\n        else:\n            raise TemplateSyntaxError(\n                \"Unknown argument for %r tag: %r.\" % (bits[0], option)\n            )\n        options[option] = value\n    isolated_context = options.get(\"only\", False)\n    namemap = options.get(\"with\", {})\n    bits[1] = construct_relative_path(\n        parser.origin.template_name,\n        bits[1],\n        allow_recursion=True,\n    )\n    return IncludeNode(\n        parser.compile_filter(bits[1]),\n        extra_context=namemap,\n        isolated_context=isolated_context,\n    )\nENDOFFILE\necho \"Created loader_tags.py\"",
  "description": "Create loader_tags.py with correct nested block implementation"
}

  "stdout": "Created loader_tags.py"
